fields.入院情况: History of Present Illness: Ms. ___ is a ___ female with a history of opioid use disorder in remission, depression, hypertriglyceridemia, and tobacco use disorder who was referred to the ED by her PCP after she was found to be tachycardic in the 150s with an elevated D-dimer. She reports that about 10 days ago, she developed generalized body aches, joint pain in her ankles and hands, swelling in her ankles, mild sore throat, and canker sores in her mouth. She went to the ___ ER and was discharged home with a prescription for methocarbamol, which she started taking but this did not alleviate her symptoms. After this, she developed a pruritic, pustular rash over her face, chest, arms, and upper back. She went in to urgent care on ___ or ___ because the swelling in her lower extremities was getting worse. She was prescribed Lasix and made an appointment for follow up with her PCP on ___. She went in to her PCP and was found to be tachycardic with an elevated D-dimer and was sent to the ED. Over the last 10 days, she reports her joint pain and body aches have improved. The sore throat is resolved. The rash is stable to slightly improved. The ___ edema is worse, however. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, lightheadedness/dizziness. No coughing or hemoptysis. No orthopnea or PND. No headaches, visual changes. No abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea. She is slightly constipated. No dysuria, no frothy urine. She has not been immobilized. No long distance travel recently. She does smoke cigarettes and does not take OCPs. ___ edema is symmetric. No personal history of malignancy. No family history of clotting disorder. No history of miscarriage.  Past Medical History: obesity dyslipidemia  Social History: ___ Family History: HTN - mother CAD - maternal grandmother ___ - both sides of the family Alcohol use disorder - both parents  Physical Exam: ADMISSION EXAM VITALS: T 98.2, HR 97, BP 121/84, RR 18, SpO2 97% on RA GENERAL: Alert, NAD, breathing room air comfortably EYES: Anicteric, PERRL ENT: Aphthous ulcers involving buccal mucosa and the sides of the tongue CV: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no m/r/g, JVP not elevated RESP: CTAB, no wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi GI: Abdomen soft, non-distended, non-tender to palpation. Bowel sounds present. GU: No suprapubic fullness or tenderness to palpation MSK: Neck supple, moves all extremities, strength grossly full and symmetric bilaterally in all limbs EXT: ___ non-pitting edema in bilateral ___ up to the mid-shin SKIN: Pustular rash (many lesions are excoriated with overlying eschar) involving the face, chest, arms, and upper back NEURO: Alert, oriented, face symmetric, gaze conjugate with EOMI, speech fluent, moves all limbs PSYCH: pleasant, appropriate affect
fields.诊疗经过: Brief Hospital Course: Ms. ___ is a ___ female with a history of opioid use disorder in remission, depression, hypertriglyceridemia, and tobacco use disorder who was referred to the ED by her PCP for tachycardia and an elevated D-dimer. She was found to have a small segmental PE. Also with an odd constellation of new symptoms including recent facial and truncal macular/acneiform rash, new bilateral ___ edema and hand puffiness, aphthous ulcers, polyarthralgias, and pharyngitis. # RLL segmental PE This preliminarily appears to be an unprovoked PE. She does not take OCPs and is not currently pregnant. When her labs were last checked, her folate level was undetectable. It is unclear why her folate level would be so low as she does not have an alcohol use disorder and has no dietary restrictions. Perhaps elevated homocysteine levels in the setting of folate deficiency could have predisposed her to thrombosis? Beta-2-Glycoprotein antibodies and cardiolipin antibodies were sent and are pending. She was started on warfarin 5 mg daily with a lovenox bridge (her health insurance generally does not approve DOACs without a warfarin failure). She was treated with Lovenox, which will be continued to therapeutic INR. She will be followed at ___ ___ clinic, and arrangements were made for her to get her INRs checked at ___, which is closer to her home. Unless her odd constellation of symptoms is found to be a reversible process that could potentially explain her VTE, she will likely need lifelong anticoagulation. # Bilateral ___ edema Non-pitting lower extremity edema and puffy hands in a pattern clinically more suggestive of myxedema than anything else, although TSH was not abnormal. LENIs were negative for DVT bilaterally. JVP and BNP were low and TTE was unremarkable, so CHF is excluded. LFTs are within normal limits and she has no history of chronic liver disease. No proteinuria, so nephrotic syndrome is excluded. She had been trying Lasix and NSAIDs for the swelling previously, but was counseled that these are both unlikely to help and was given compression stockings. # Polyarthralgias # Pustular rash # Aphthous ulcers The patient was seen by rheumatology, who suspected a viral syndrome; they comment that SLE could explain some of the symptoms, and also comment that while Behcet's may present with oral ulcer associated with VTE, it is vanishingly rare in patient's of ___ heritage. Seen by ID who did not feel that the rash was typical of any viral exanthem, but wondered if it could be an unusual presentation of a drug rash. If these symptoms do not continue to improve or are recurrent, would consider working up for a rheumatologic process. ___ negative, CRP not elevated, HIV negative, Lyme negative, HCV and HBV negative, VZV negative, monospot negative. RPR, EBV, parvovirus B19, dsDNA are pending. # MDD - continue home citalopram 20 mg daily
fields.出院情况: Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.
fields.出院医嘱: Discharge Medications: 1. Enoxaparin Sodium 70 mg SC Q12H RX *enoxaparin 80 mg/0.8 mL 80 mg SQ twice a day Disp #*28 Syringe Refills:*0 2. FoLIC Acid 1 mg PO DAILY RX *folic acid 1 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth daily Disp #*30 Tablet Refills:*0 3. Warfarin 5 mg PO DAILY16 RX *warfarin [Coumadin] 2.5 mg 2 tablet(s) by mouth qPM Disp #*60 Tablet Refills:*0 4. Citalopram 20 mg PO DAILY  Discharge Instructions: You were admitted with a pulmonary embolism, or blood clot that went to the lung. We are giving you warfarin (Coumadin) to thin your blood for at least the next three months. You will take twice daily enoxaparin (Lovenox) shots for a few days until the warfarin has fully taken effect. We follow a measure of blood clotting called INR to know if warfarin is being dosed appropriately. A therapeutic INR is between 2.0 and 3.0. You will be followed by the ___ clinic at ___, which is affiliated with your PCP ___. However, you can get your INR checked at ___. Just go to pre-admissions testing which near the main entrance, downstairs near ___. When the lab result comes back, you will be called by the pharmacist in town to adjust your warfarin dose as needed. Your first INR check will be on ___. They tell me there will be a standing order for labs in place and ___ will be expecting you, but I have also given you a paper script for bloodwork just in case. You also had a variety of unusual symptoms that may have been symptoms a virus, an adverse reaction to a medication, deficiency of the vitamin folic acid, or an autoimmune disease. We went a very large number of tests to figure out what might be going on. I will call you if anything really notable comes back positive. If the symptoms persist, you have an appointment with Dr. ___ rheumatology to do further workup. If the symptoms all go away, then it was unlikely to have been an autoimmune disease and you are welcome to cancel this appointment (___).

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: Shellfish  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: cough, DOE  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None  History of Present Illness: ___ y/o M with HIV (last known CD4~350), asthma, presents with cough and DOE x2 days. Patient reports sudden onset of cough 2 days ago, productive of thick, green sputum. Also complains of pleuritic chest and RUQ pain with cough. Endorsing shortness of breath with exertion, including speaking. Denies recent travels. ? friend with similar symptoms. Denies N/V/D, chest pain, fevers.  Initial VS in the ED: CXR clear, VBG showed acidosis and pO2 of 59. Patient started on Levofloxacin 750mg, Benzonatate 100mg, Sulfameth/Trimethoprim DS DS x2 and prednisone 60mg x1 for treatment of PCP PNA vs CAP. VS prior to transfer: 98.4-106-16 112/66 96%2lnc ___ On the floor, patient continues to complain of SOB and bilateral pleuritic pain in the lateral ribs.  Review of systems: (+) Per HPI (-) Denies fever, chills, night sweats, recent weight loss or gain. Denies headache, sinus tenderness, rhinorrhea or congestion. Denied chest pain or tightness, palpitations. Denied nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation or abdominal pain. No recent change in bowel or bladder habits. No dysuria. Denied arthralgias or myalgias.  Past Medical History: HIV INFECTION- dx ___, on atripla, ?recent decrease in CD4 to 350s  Social History: ___ Family History: No history of recurrent pulmonary infections  Physical Exam: Admission exam: Vitals: 99.2; 120/86; 111; 20; 92RA General: Alert, oriented, no acute distress HEENT: Sclera anicteric, MMM, oropharynx clear Neck: supple, JVP not elevated, no LAD Lungs: decreased breath sound in all fields. Diffusely wheezy, no rhonchi or crackles CV: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2, no murmurs, rubs, gallops Abdomen: soft, non-tender, non-distended, bowel sounds present, no rebound tenderness or guarding, no organomegaly Ext: Warm, well perfused, 2+ pulses, no clubbing, cyanosis or edema Discharge exam: Afebrile, VSS Lungs: diffuse rhonchi, no crakles or wheezes Exam otherwise unchanged since admission  Pertinent Results: ADMISSION LABS: ___ 04:55AM BLOOD WBC-11.4*# RBC-4.20* Hgb-13.8* Hct-40.6 MCV-97 MCH-32.8* MCHC-33.9 RDW-13.3 Plt ___ ___ 04:55AM BLOOD Neuts-82.3* Lymphs-12.0* Monos-4.2 Eos-1.3 Baso-0.3 ___ 04:55AM BLOOD Glucose-89 UreaN-10 Creat-0.9 Na-143 K-4.0 Cl-105 HCO3-23 AnGap-19 ___ 04:55AM BLOOD LD(LDH)-245 ___ 05:06AM BLOOD Lactate-2.1* ___ 11:40AM BLOOD WBC-10.7 Lymph-4* Abs ___ CD3%-51 Abs CD3-218* CD4%-20 Abs CD4-84* CD8%-26 Abs CD8-111* CD4/CD8-0.8* DISCHARGE LABS: ___ 08:10AM BLOOD WBC-5.5 RBC-3.43* Hgb-11.5* Hct-34.2* MCV-100* MCH-33.5* MCHC-33.7 RDW-13.7 Plt ___ ___ 05:23AM BLOOD Glucose-98 UreaN-8 Creat-0.7 Na-141 K-3.9 Cl-105 HCO___ AnGap-9 MICROBIOLOGY: Blood culture ___: no growth at the time of discharge HIV viral load: undetectable Urine legionella: negative ___ 7:30 pm SPUTUM Source: Induced. GRAM STAIN (Final ___: >25 PMNs and >10 epithelial cells/100X field. Gram stain indicates extensive contamination with upper respiratory secretions. Bacterial culture results are invalid. PLEASE SUBMIT ANOTHER SPECIMEN. LEGIONELLA CULTURE (Preliminary): NO LEGIONELLA ISOLATED. Immunoflourescent test for Pneumocystis jirovecii (carinii) (Final ___: NEGATIVE for Pneumocystis jirovecii (carinii).. FUNGAL CULTURE (Preliminary): NO FUNGUS ISOLATED. ACID FAST SMEAR (Final ___: NO ACID FAST BACILLI SEEN ON CONCENTRATED SMEAR. ___ 8:42 am SPUTUM Source: Induced. ACID FAST SMEAR (Final ___: NO ACID FAST BACILLI SEEN ON CONCENTRATED SMEAR. ACID FAST CULTURE (Preliminary): ___ 5:30 pm SPUTUM Source: Induced. ACID FAST SMEAR (Preliminary): NO ACID FAST BACILLI SEEN ON DIRECT SMEAR. This is only a PRELIMINARY result. If ruling out tuberculosis, you must wait for confirmation by concentrated smear. ACID FAST CULTURE (Preliminary): IMAGING: CXR ___: The lungs are mildly hyperinflated suggesting underlying obstructive pulmonary disease. No confluent opacity is identified to suggest pneumonia. There is no pulmonary edema or pleural effusions. Cardiomediastinal and hilar contours are within normal limits. No pneumothorax is evident. Healed left-sided rib fractures are noted. IMPRESSION: No acute cardiopulmonary process. CTA ___: Final Report FINDINGS: CT ANGIOGRAM: There are no pulmonary arterial filling defects to suggest presence of pulmonary embolism. There is adequate visualization to the subsegmental vessels. The aorta is normal in course and caliber, without evidence of dissection or intramural hematoma. The remainder of the great vessels are also unremarkable.  SOFT TISSUES: The visualized thyroid gland appears normal. There are several prominent mediastinal lymph nodes, none which meet CT size criteria for pathological enlargement. A borderline subcarinal lymph node measures 11 mm in the short axis (4:107). There are bilateral pathologically enlarged hilar lymph nodes, on the right measuring 25 x 18 mm, and on the left measuring 18 x 8 mm (4:106). There is no supraclavicular or axillary lymphadenopathy. This study is not optimized for evaluation of subdiaphragmatic structures. Within these limitations, minimal nonspecific bilateral perinephric fat stranding is seen. The stomach is mildly dilated with ingested material. There are no other gross upper abdominal abnormalities. LUNGS: Pneumomediastinum can be seen arising from the right hilum, extending to the midline and tracking inferiorly alongside the esophagus and anterior portion of the aorta (4:134, 102, 601B:32). There is no pneumothorax or pneumopericardium. There is diffuse bronchial wall thickening bilaterally (4:135). In addition, there are ___ nodular peripheral opacities, predominantly in the lower lung zones, more extensive on the left (4:203, 135). No pleural effusion is seen.  MUSCULOSKELETAL: There is an old left rib fracture (2:27), but no focal osseous lesions concerning for malignancy.  IMPRESSION: 1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic syndrome. 2. Diffuse bronchitis and lower lobe predominant bronchiolitis presenting as ___ opacities, which is indicative of an infectious process. The features lack the extensive ground-glass opacity normally seen in PCP. Although this could be a common bacterial infection, there are features which raise concern for acute tuberculosis, or other atypical pneumonia. The bilateral lymphadenopathy is likely reactive hypertrophy. 3. Pneumomediastinum, likely spontaneous. Barium swallow ___: A single swallow of Optiray contrast, followed by multiple swallows of thin barium showed no evidence of leak or esophageal rupture. The esophagus is normal in size and caliber. There is no holdup of barium at the gastroesophageal junction. Images were obtained in frontal and oblique planes. IMPRESSION: No evidence of esophageal leak or rupture.  Brief Hospital Course: ___ y/o M with PMHx of HIV (last CD4 last month 365, drop from 800s) on Atripla presenting with SOB and cough, improved with treatment for CAP, ruled out for TB with induced sputum x3. # Community acquired pneumonia/Hypoxia: Patient presented with cough, DOE and hypoxia. Patient with HIV (last known CD4 300+), however has reported dropping CD4 recently. CD4 84 now but difficult to interpret in the setting of acute illness. Initial differential was broad including CAP, PCP, and fungal infection. Patient initially started on levaquin and Bactrim to cover CAP and PCP ___. CTA showed no PE and L>R bibasilar ___ infiltrates. The rapid onset suggested bacterial infection and Bactrim was changed to prophylaxis dose and CAP treatment switched to CTX/Azithro while patient being ruled out for TB. Cough treated with Robitussin. Preliminary results from induced sputum x3 showed negative AFB staining. Patient's hypoxia resolved with CAP treatment. Patient discharged on cefpodoxime and azithromycin for a total of 7 day course. Patient to follow up with PCP and arrange follow up with infectious diseases. # HIV: Current CD4 84 down from 350s one month ago. However, this is difficult to interpret with acute illness. HIV VL undetectable which is encouraging. Patient showed no signs of opportunistic infections. Continued home dose atripla. Plan to follow up outpatient infectious diseases. Started on PCP prophylaxis with ___. # Spontaneous pneumomediastinum: Small amount of air was seen in the mediastinum on CTA. This was likely spontaneous from severe cough. Thoracics was consulted and recommended Barium swallow to rule out esophageal rupture (though low likelihood) and keeping patient NPO. Once Barium swallow was negative for esophageal rupture, patient returned to regular diet. Thoracics recommended no further imaging to evaluate pneumomediastium in the absence of additional symptoms. # Asthma: Continued albuterol. # Pain control: Continued home dose Gabapentin, percocet prn and ibuprofen prn. # Transitional issues: - code status: full code - new meds: Azithromycin, cefpodoxime, Bactrim, robitussin - pending labs: Blood culture from ___, Final results of induced sputum x3 - follow up: with PCP, ___ help arrange follow up with infectious diseases)  Medications on Admission: 1. ATRIPLA *NF* (efavirenz-emtricitabin-tenofov) ___ mg Oral daily 2. Quetiapine Fumarate 100 mg PO QHS 3. Doxepin HCl 100 mg PO HS 4. Naproxen 500 mg PO Q12H 5. Ibuprofen 400 mg PO Q8H:PRN pain 6. Fluticasone Propionate NASAL 1 SPRY NU BID 7. Citalopram 40 mg PO DAILY 8. Albuterol Inhaler 2 PUFF IH Q4H:PRN SOB, wheezing 9. Gabapentin 300mg TID  Discharge Medications: 1. Citalopram 40 mg PO DAILY 2. Doxepin HCl 100 mg PO HS 3. Fluticasone Propionate NASAL 1 SPRY NU BID 4. Gabapentin 300 mg PO TID hold for sedation 5. Quetiapine Fumarate 100 mg PO QHS 6. Azithromycin 500 mg PO Q24H Duration: 2 Doses RX *azithromycin 250 mg 1 Tablet(s) by mouth daily Disp #*2 Tablet Refills:*0 7. Albuterol Inhaler 2 PUFF IH Q4H:PRN SOB, wheezing 8. ATRIPLA *NF* (efavirenz-emtricitabin-tenofov) ___ mg Oral daily 9. Naproxen 500 mg PO Q12H:PRN pain do not take with ibuprofen 10. Ibuprofen 400 mg PO Q8H:PRN pain do not take with naproxen 11. Cefpodoxime Proxetil 200 mg PO Q12H RX *cefpodoxime 200 mg 2 Tablet(s) by mouth twice a day Disp #*20 Tablet Refills:*0 12. Sulfameth/Trimethoprim DS 1 TAB PO DAILY RX *Bactrim DS 800 mg-160 mg 1 Tablet(s) by mouth once a day Disp #*14 Tablet Refills:*0  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: PRIMARY: - Community Acquired Pneumonia SECONDARY: - Human Immunodeficiency Virus  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: Mr. ___, It was a pleasure taking care of you at ___. You were admitted because you had a severe cough and shortness of breath. We found that you developed a bacterial pneumonia. We treated you with antibiotics and you improved. Our preliminary cultures showed that you did not have a tuberculosis infection. The cultures will be finalized on ___. We will call you with the results. Until then you should avoid being near large crowds or immunosuppressed individuals. We made the following changes to your medications: - STARTED Azithromycin (last day ___ - STARTED Cefpodoxime (last day ___ - STARTED Bactrim (a medication you should continue until directed by your HIV doctor)  Followup Instructions: ___

fields.入院情况: History of Present Illness: ___ y/o M with HIV (last known CD4~350), asthma, presents with cough and DOE x2 days. Patient reports sudden onset of cough 2 days ago, productive of thick, green sputum. Also complains of pleuritic chest and RUQ pain with cough. Endorsing shortness of breath with exertion, including speaking. Denies recent travels. ? friend with similar symptoms. Denies N/V/D, chest pain, fevers.  Initial VS in the ED: CXR clear, VBG showed acidosis and pO2 of 59. Patient started on Levofloxacin 750mg, Benzonatate 100mg, Sulfameth/Trimethoprim DS DS x2 and prednisone 60mg x1 for treatment of PCP PNA vs CAP. VS prior to transfer: 98.4-106-16 112/66 96%2lnc ___ On the floor, patient continues to complain of SOB and bilateral pleuritic pain in the lateral ribs.  Physical Exam: Admission exam: Vitals: 99.2; 120/86; 111; 20; 92RA General: Alert, oriented, no acute distress HEENT: Sclera anicteric, MMM, oropharynx clear Neck: supple, JVP not elevated, no LAD Lungs: decreased breath sound in all fields. Diffusely wheezy, no rhonchi or crackles CV: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2, no murmurs, rubs, gallops Abdomen: soft, non-tender, non-distended, bowel sounds present, no rebound tenderness or guarding, no organomegaly Ext: Warm, well perfused, 2+ pulses, no clubbing, cyanosis or edema
fields.诊疗经过: Brief Hospital Course: ___ y/o M with PMHx of HIV (last CD4 last month 365, drop from 800s) on Atripla presenting with SOB and cough, improved with treatment for CAP, ruled out for TB with induced sputum x3. # Community acquired pneumonia/Hypoxia: Patient presented with cough, DOE and hypoxia. Patient with HIV (last known CD4 300+), however has reported dropping CD4 recently. CD4 84 now but difficult to interpret in the setting of acute illness. Initial differential was broad including CAP, PCP, and fungal infection. Patient initially started on levaquin and Bactrim to cover CAP and PCP ___. CTA showed no PE and L>R bibasilar ___ infiltrates. The rapid onset suggested bacterial infection and Bactrim was changed to prophylaxis dose and CAP treatment switched to CTX/Azithro while patient being ruled out for TB. Cough treated with Robitussin. Preliminary results from induced sputum x3 showed negative AFB staining. Patient's hypoxia resolved with CAP treatment. Patient discharged on cefpodoxime and azithromycin for a total of 7 day course. Patient to follow up with PCP and arrange follow up with infectious diseases. # HIV: Current CD4 84 down from 350s one month ago. However, this is difficult to interpret with acute illness. HIV VL undetectable which is encouraging. Patient showed no signs of opportunistic infections.  # Spontaneous pneumomediastinum: Small amount of air was seen in the mediastinum on CTA. This was likely spontaneous from severe cough. Thoracics was consulted and recommended Barium swallow to rule out esophageal rupture (though low likelihood) and keeping patient NPO. Once Barium swallow was negative for esophageal rupture, patient returned to regular diet. Thoracics recommended no further imaging to evaluate pneumomediastium in the absence of additional symptoms.

Urine culture: ___ CULTURE (Final ___: MIXED BACTERIAL FLORA ( >= 3 COLONY TYPES), CONSISTENT WITH SKIN AND/OR GENITAL CONTAMINATION. Chlamydia trachomatis, Nucleic Acid Probe, with Amplification (Final ___: Negative for Chlamydia trachomatis by PANTHER System, APTIMA COMBO 2 Assay. NEISSERIA GONORRHOEAE (GC), NUCLEIC ACID PROBE, WITH AMPLIFICATION (Final ___: Negative for Neisseria gonorrhoeae by PANTHER System, APTIMA COMBO 2 Assay. ============================ CXR: IMPRESSION: PA and lateral chest compared to ___:  Lungs are clear. There is no pneumothorax or pleural effusion. Cardiomediastinal and hilar silhouettes and pleural surfaces are normal.  I see no displaced rib fracture, but conventional chest radiographs are not sensitive in finding subtle chest cage trauma. Focal findings should be marked for rib detail views. . . CT ABDOMEN: CT Abdomen: There are small bilateral pleural effusions and dependent atelectasis at both lung bases.  The liver enhances homogeneously. There are no focal concerning liver lesions. There is no intra or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. Gallbladder is normal. The spleen has normal attenuation and enhancement. The pancreas and the spleen enhance homogeneously. There is no evidence of splenic infarct. The kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete contrast promptly. There is no ascites or abdominal adenopathy. The aorta and its major branches are patent. The stomach and proximal loops of bowel have normal caliber and appearance.  CT Pelvis: There is moderate wall thickening of the distal 20-25 cm of terminal ileum (6:20 33). There is mild thin mucosal hyperenhancement and no significant intramural hyperenhancemnt or adjacent mesenteric stranding. No adenopathy. Although bowel distention is limited by poor passage or oral contrast, there is no evidence of stricture or proximal dilation. There is no evidence of interloop fistula. The appendix is normal. There is a small volume of free pelvic fluid. A left tubal ligation clip is in appropriate position. The right tubal ligation clip is positioned abnormally in the midline peritoneum, anterior to the uterus and not in the vicinity proximity of the right fallopian tube (3: 127).  Radiopaque setons are noted within perianal fistulae which were previously assessed with pelvic MRI. The bladder and uterus are unremarkable.  There are no concerning lytic or sclerotic bone lesions.   IMPRESSION: 1. Chronic disease of the distal 20-25 cm of terminal ileum with minimal to mild active disease. No evidence of stricture. No abscess.  2. No splenic infarct, colitis, or other explanation for left upper quadrant pain.  3. Right tubal ligation clip appears dislodged and is anterior to the uterus. A secondary contraception method may be necessary.  4. Known perianal fistulae with setons. . . FINDINGS:  Approximately 8 mm above the anal verge is a thin fistulous tract arising from the lower anal canal at 7 o'clock position (1002:79) coursing inferiorly along the intersphincteric plane, opens directly onto the skin in the right perianal region. This tract predominantly contains granulation tissue, and minimal if any fluid. Approximately a centimeter above the anal verge is a left-sided fistulous tract arising at ___ o'clock position (1002:82), which now has a ___ in place. This fistula continues to have an intersphincteric course opening onto the left gluteal skin. The tract is adequately drained with fluid. A posterior branching tract from the left perianal fistula has less inflammation compared to the prior study and continues to extend to the left gluteal skin. No new fistulas are identified, especially no evidence of a rectovaginal fistula. There is no evidence of a perirectal abscess.  The uterus is anteverted and retroflexed. The endometrium is normal, measuring 6 mm. A 11 mm T2 hypointense enhancing lesion in the uterus is consistent with a fibroid. The ovaries are normal. The imaged portion of the rectum is normal. The urinary bladder is normal. There is a small amount of pelvic free fluid. Few reactive inguinal lymph nodes are seen bilaterally. No marrow signal abnormality is seen. The pelvic vasculature is widely patent.  IMPRESSION: 1. Right- and left-sided small intersphincteric perianal fistulas, demonstrating minimal, if any fluid. The left fistulous tract containing a ___ has decreased inflammation compared to the prior study of ___. 2. No new perianal fistulas, especially no evidence of rectovaginal fistula or abscess. . . KUB: FINDINGS: Supine and upright views of the abdomen. The bowel gas pattern is normal without dilated small or large bowel to suggest obstruction. No air-fluid level or pneumoperitoneum is seen on the upright view. No pneumatosis or portal venous gas. Two metallic densities projecting over the left hemipelvis are consistent with tubal ligation clips. IMPRESSION: Normal bowel gas pattern without evidence of obstruction.  Brief Hospital Course: ASSESSMENT / PLAN: ___ year old female with h/o Crohn's on ___ admitted in ___ admitted with LUQ pain . #ABDOMINAL PAIN: The patient's pain was likely due to constipation versus functional. She had an MRI pelvis that did not demonstrate new fistula formation or abscess. She had a CT abdomen that did not demonstrate significant colitis or acute pathology. CXR did not demonstrate thoracic pathology such as pneumonia or rib fracture. Her inflammatory markers were normal. She was afebrile with negative urine and blood cultures. She reported not having a bowel movement in over ten days. She was given a bowel regimen of senna/colase/miralax. She was also given 1 liter of golytely, which was effective in producing multiple bowel movements resulting in improvement in her symptoms. #FREE TUBAL LIGATION CLIP: CT scan demonstrated that the right tubal ligation clip was likely dislodged. This was discussed with the patient, including the need for alternative contraception. She was also scheduled for follow up with OB-GYN. #CROHN DISEASE: She was continued on humira. #IRON DEFICIENCY ANEMIA: Her hematocrit was stable. She was continued on supplemental iron.  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Humira (adalimumab) 40 mg/0.8 mL subcutaneous Q ___ 2. Cyanocobalamin 1000 mcg IM/SC Q MONTH 3. Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID 4. Senna 8.6 mg PO BID:PRN constipation 5. Polyethylene Glycol 17 g PO DAILY 6. Lidocaine 5% Ointment 1 Appl TP Q2H:PRN rectal pain 7. Ferrous Sulfate 325 mg PO DAILY  Discharge Medications: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID 2. Lidocaine 5% Ointment 1 Appl TP Q2H:PRN rectal pain 3. Polyethylene Glycol 17 g PO BID 4. Senna 8.6 mg PO BID 5. Maalox/Diphenhydramine/Lidocaine ___ mL PO TID:PRN epigastric pain RX *alum-mag hydroxide-simeth [Maalox Advanced] 200 mg-200 mg-20 mg/5 mL ___ teaspoonful by mouth twice daily Disp #*1 Bottle Refills:*0 6. Milk of Magnesia 30 mL PO Q8H:PRN constipation 7. Cyanocobalamin 1000 mcg IM/SC Q MONTH 8. Ferrous Sulfate 325 mg PO DAILY 9. Humira (adalimumab) 40 mg/0.8 mL subcutaneous Q ___ 10. TraMADOL (Ultram) 50 mg PO Q8H:PRN pain RX *tramadol 50 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth Q8hr Disp #*15 Tablet Refills:*0 11. Lorazepam 0.5 mg PO HS:PRN insomnia RX *lorazepam 0.5 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth at bedtime Disp #*10 Tablet Refills:*0  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Crohn's disease Abdominal pain Constipation Dislodged tubal ligation clip  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: It was a pleasure to participate in your care. You were admitted with abdominal pain. You had an MRI that did not show any new fistulas. You had a CT scan that was also negative. You were seen by the GI specialists. Constipation may have been contributing to your symptoms so it is important to take medication to prevent this. If you don't have a bowel movement despite taking these medications please contact your GI doctor ___ Dr. ___ for further instructions. Your symptoms improved and you were discharged home. Importantly, as we discussed, the CT scan showed that one of the tubal ligation clips has dislodged. It may be possible for you to become pregnant at this time so we recommend using an alternative form of contraception. We have arranged for a follow up appointment with your OB-GYN doctor.  Followup Instructions: ___

There is no pneumothorax or pleural effusion. Cardiomediastinal and hilar silhouettes and pleural surfaces are normal.  I see no displaced rib fracture, but conventional chest radiographs are not sensitive in finding subtle chest cage trauma. Focal findings should be marked for rib detail views. . . CT ABDOMEN: CT Abdomen: There are small bilateral pleural effusions and dependent atelectasis at both lung bases.  The liver enhances homogeneously. There are no focal concerning liver lesions. There is no intra or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. Gallbladder is normal. The spleen has normal attenuation and enhancement. The pancreas and the spleen enhance homogeneously. There is no evidence of splenic infarct. The kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete contrast promptly. There is no ascites or abdominal adenopathy. The aorta and its major branches are patent. The stomach and proximal loops of bowel have normal caliber and appearance.  CT Pelvis: There is moderate wall thickening of the distal 20-25 cm of terminal ileum (6:20 33). There is mild thin mucosal hyperenhancement and no significant intramural hyperenhancemnt or adjacent mesenteric stranding. No adenopathy. Although bowel distention is limited by poor passage or oral contrast, there is no evidence of stricture or proximal dilation. There is no evidence of interloop fistula. The appendix is normal. There is a small volume of free pelvic fluid. A left tubal ligation clip is in appropriate position. The right tubal ligation clip is positioned abnormally in the midline peritoneum, anterior to the uterus and not in the vicinity proximity of the right fallopian tube (3: 127).  Radiopaque setons are noted within perianal fistulae which were previously assessed with pelvic MRI. The bladder and uterus are unremarkable.  There are no concerning lytic or sclerotic bone lesions.   IMPRESSION: 1. Chronic disease of the distal 20-25 cm of terminal ileum with minimal to mild active disease. No evidence of stricture. No abscess.  2. No splenic infarct, colitis, or other explanation for left upper quadrant pain.  3. Right tubal ligation clip appears dislodged and is anterior to the uterus. A secondary contraception method may be necessary.  4. Known perianal fistulae with setons. . . FINDINGS:  Approximately 8 mm above the anal verge is a thin fistulous tract arising from the lower anal canal at 7 o'clock position (1002:79) coursing inferiorly along the intersphincteric plane, opens directly onto the skin in the right perianal region. This tract predominantly contains granulation tissue, and minimal if any fluid. Approximately a centimeter above the anal verge is a left-sided fistulous tract arising at ___ o'clock position (1002:82), which now has a ___ in place. This fistula continues to have an intersphincteric course opening onto the left gluteal skin. The tract is adequately drained with fluid. A posterior branching tract from the left perianal fistula has less inflammation compared to the prior study and continues to extend to the left gluteal skin. No new fistulas are identified, especially no evidence of a rectovaginal fistula. There is no evidence of a perirectal abscess.  The uterus is anteverted and retroflexed. The endometrium is normal, measuring 6 mm. A 11 mm T2 hypointense enhancing lesion in the uterus is consistent with a fibroid. The ovaries are normal. The imaged portion of the rectum is normal. The urinary bladder is normal. There is a small amount of pelvic free fluid. Few reactive inguinal lymph nodes are seen bilaterally. No marrow signal abnormality is seen. The pelvic vasculature is widely patent.  IMPRESSION: 1. Right- and left-sided small intersphincteric perianal fistulas, demonstrating minimal, if any fluid. No air-fluid level or pneumoperitoneum is seen on the upright view. No pneumatosis or portal venous gas. Brief Hospital Course: ASSESSMENT / PLAN: ___ year old female with h/o Crohn's on ___ admitted in ___ admitted with LUQ pain . #ABDOMINAL PAIN: The patient's pain was likely due to constipation versus functional. She had an MRI pelvis that did not demonstrate new fistula formation or abscess. She had a CT abdomen that did not demonstrate significant colitis or acute pathology. CXR did not demonstrate thoracic pathology such as pneumonia or rib fracture. Her inflammatory markers were normal. She was afebrile with negative urine and blood cultures. She reported not having a bowel movement in over ten days. She was given a bowel regimen of senna/colase/miralax. She was also given 1 liter of golytely, which was effective in producing multiple bowel movements resulting in improvement in her symptoms. #FREE TUBAL LIGATION CLIP: CT scan demonstrated that the right tubal ligation clip was likely dislodged. This was discussed with the patient, including the need for alternative contraception. She was also scheduled for follow up with OB-GYN. #CROHN DISEASE: She was continued on humira. #IRON DEFICIENCY ANEMIA: Her hematocrit was stable. She was continued on supplemental iron. Patient was discharged from this hospital 6 days ago after having SBP, was doing well at home until yesterday. At 6 ___, she noticed worsening abdominal pain with some associated nausea. She has had no vomiting, no hematemesis. She called her hepatologist, who recommended she come in for evaluation. She is otherwise been feeling well. content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Right shoulder swelling  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: Arthrotomy of right glenohumeral joint with washout (___) PICC placement in left arm (___)  History of Present Illness: ___ M with CKDIV, DM2, hypertension, gout, and chronic right shoulder pain who was recently admitted in ___ after experiencing a fall with severe rotator cuff tear who is now transferred to ___ ED from OSH for significant right shoulder erythema and swelling. He was previously seen by the ortho trauma service on ___ with right shoulder swelling viewed on x-ray and MRI showing large right shoulder collection without intraarticular involvement, as well as a large rotator cuff tear. He underwent a diagnostic tap at that time which showed 39k WBCs but negative cultures and he improved without antibiotics. He was just seen in f/u by Dr. ___ on ___ for his right rotator cuff tear - due to lack of any pain the decision was made for conservative management. He says that over the past 1 week his right should swelling and redness has significantly worsened, much worse than previously. He has some subjective chills occasionally but no fevers and no other infectious symptoms. He underwent an I&D in the ED showing > 100,000 K WBCs but negative gram stain. He was subsequently started on IV vancomycin. This AM he underwent surgical arthrotomy of the shoulder draining a large amount of pus from the left shoulder from an abscess which involves the glenohumeral joint. REVIEW OF SYSTEMS: Denies fever, chills, chest pain, dyspnea. His right shoulder is minimally tender  Past Medical History: - Diabetes mellitus, type 2 * C/b diabetic retinopathy * C/b diabetic nephropathy - Dyslipidemia - Hypertension - Coronary artery disease - Chronic kidney disease, Stage IV - Gouty arthritis - GERD - Skin cancers  Social History: ___ Family History: - non-Hodgkin's lymphoma - Mother, father, and brother with diabetes.  Physical Exam: ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM: VS: T 98.5 BP 124/78 HR 86 RR 18 Spo2 100% on RA GENERAL: Well appearing elderly man HEENT: EOMI NECK: No JVD RESP: CTAB CV: RRR, faint SEM ABD: Soft, nontender EXT: Right shoulder demonstrates baseball sized fluctuant mass which is erythematous but minimally tender. NEURO: A&Ox3. DISCHARGE PHYSICAL EXAM: VS: T 98.6 BP 122/56 HR 71 RR 18 SpO2 99% on RA GENERAL: Well appearing elderly man is sleepy but easily roused HEENT: EOMI, erythematous conjunctivae, scattered scaling on face appear to be AKs NECK: No JVD RESP: CTAB CV: RRR, faint SEM ABD: Soft, nontender EXT: Right shoulder bandaged with drain now removed NEURO: A&Ox3.  Pertinent Results: ADMISSION LABS ============== ___ 10:30PM BLOOD WBC-6.6 RBC-3.04* Hgb-8.3* Hct-24.1* MCV-79* MCH-27.2 MCHC-34.3 RDW-15.9* Plt ___ ___ 10:30PM BLOOD Glucose-183* UreaN-38* Creat-2.2* Na-135 K-4.0 Cl-97 HCO3-23 AnGap-19 ___ 06:23AM BLOOD %HbA1c-8.4* eAG-194* ___ 10:30PM BLOOD CRP-241.5* ___ 10:46PM BLOOD Lactate-1.3 MICROBIOLOGY ============ Tissue (___): Gram stain negative for organisms, no growth to date Joint fluid (___): 102,000 WBCs. Gram stain negative for organisms, no growth to date Lyme serology (___): Negative Universal PCR for bacteria/AFB (___): Pending DISCHARGE LABS ============== ___ 05:31AM BLOOD WBC-6.1 RBC-3.35* Hgb-9.1* Hct-27.8* MCV-83 MCH-27.3 MCHC-32.9 RDW-15.1 Plt ___ ___ 05:31AM BLOOD Glucose-106* UreaN-39* Creat-2.1* Na-137 K-4.0 Cl-99 HCO3-23 AnGap-19 ___ 05:31AM BLOOD CK(CPK)-___ M with CKD IV, DM2, hypertension, gout, and chronic right shoulder pain who presents with right shoulder abscess. #) RIGHT SHOULDER ABSCESS: S/p OR washout ___. >100,000 WBCs in joint fluid. Gram stain negative and no growth to date. Crystals negative. Await cultures. Patient was without systemic signs or symptoms of infection (no fever, leukocytosis.) No other joints affected. Infectious diseases was consulted and recommended empiric course of daptomycin. A PICC was placed. Unfortunately, due to insurance constraints, the patient was unable to get home infusion of daptomycin. ___ clinic was arranged for the patient to get daptomycin as an outpatient Q48H. OPAT labs to be drawn weekly. # CODE STATUS: DNR/DNI confirmed with pt on admission # CONTACT: ___ (son) ___ TRANSITIONAL ISSUES =================== [] Follow-up on bacterial/AFB universal PCR [] Follow-up on tissue Lyme PCR  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: Dear Mr. ___, It was a pleasure taking care of you at ___ ___. You were admitted for a swollen right shoulder which appears to be infected. Thus, we started you on antibiotics which you should continue at discharge. We arranged for you to get antibiotic infusions as an outpatient in clinic. Please follow up with your PCP, ___, and infectious diseases doctor as below. Regards, Your ___ Team  Followup Instructions: ___  Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: SURGERY  Allergies: Penicillins / Watermelon / Almond Oil  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: abdominal pain x 1 day  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: Laparoscopic Appendectomy  History of Present Illness: The patient is a ___ male type I diabetic who, about 1 day ago, began having vague mid-abdominal pain of a colicky nature. He was standing on the T platform and had an episode of clear non-bilious non-bloody emesis. He had 2 loose BMs. He continued to feel unwell and his pain persisted. Thinking that he might be going into ketoacidosis, he came to the ___ ED to be evaluated.  Past Medical History: 1. Type I diabetes mellitus, uncontrolled. Last HbA1c 10.8 ___. An antiemetic was given as he had nausea again. Bowel sounds were hypoactive. Reglan was given and nausea slowly resolved. Diet was tolerated. Vital signs were stable and he was ambulatory. Lap incision sites were intact and without redness. He was discharged home in stable condition. FERROUS SULFATE - 325 mg (65 mg Elemental Iron) Tablet - 1 Tablet(s) by mouth daily INSULIN NPH HUMAN RECOMB [HUMULIN N] - 100 unit/mL Suspension - 30 U in the AM, 29 U in the ___ as directed  Discharge Medications: 1. Amlodipine 5 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily). 2. Insulin Continue Previous Insulin Regimen. Humalog/Humalin 3. Ferrous Sulfate 325 mg (65 mg Iron) Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily). 4. Metoprolol Succinate 100 mg Tablet Sustained Release 24 hr Sig: Two (2) Tablet Sustained Release 24 hr PO DAILY (Daily). 5. Hydrocodone-Acetaminophen ___ mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO every six (6) hours as needed for pain. Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0* 6. Colace 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO twice a day. Disp:*60 Capsule(s)* Refills:*2*  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Acute Appendicitis  Discharge Condition: Stable  Discharge Instructions: Please call your surgeon if you develop chest pain, shortness of breath, fever greater than 101.5, foul smelling or colorful drainage from your incisions, redness or swelling, severe abdominal pain or distention, persistent nausea or vomiting, inability to eat or drink, or any other symptoms which are concerning to you. No tub baths or swimming. You may shower. If there is clear drainage from your incisions, cover with a dry dressing. Leave white strips above your incisions in place, allow them to fall off on their own. Activity: No heavy lifting of items ___ pounds until the follow up appointment with your doctor. Medications: Resume your home medications. You should take a stool softener, Colace 100 mg twice daily as needed for constipation. Pain medication may make you drowsy. No driving while taking pain medicine.  Followup Instructions: ___

sub_contents.sub_content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Right shoulder swelling  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: Arthrotomy of right glenohumeral joint with washout (___) PICC placement in left arm (___)  History of Present Illness: ___ M with CKDIV, DM2, hypertension, gout, and chronic right shoulder pain who was recently admitted in ___ after experiencing a fall with severe rotator cuff tear who is now transferred to ___ ED from OSH for significant right shoulder erythema and swelling. He was previously seen by the ortho trauma service on ___ with right shoulder swelling viewed on x-ray and MRI showing large right shoulder collection without intraarticular involvement, as well as a large rotator cuff tear. He underwent a diagnostic tap at that time which showed 39k WBCs but negative cultures and he improved without antibiotics. He was just seen in f/u by Dr. ___ on ___ for his right rotator cuff tear - due to lack of any pain the decision was made for conservative management. He says that over the past 1 week his right should swelling and redness has significantly worsened, much worse than previously. He has some subjective chills occasionally but no fevers and no other infectious symptoms. He underwent an I&D in the ED showing > 100,000 K WBCs but negative gram stain. He was subsequently started on IV vancomycin. This AM he underwent surgical arthrotomy of the shoulder draining a large amount of pus from the left shoulder from an abscess which involves the glenohumeral joint. REVIEW OF SYSTEMS: Denies fever, chills, chest pain, dyspnea. His right shoulder is minimally tender  Past Medical History: - Diabetes mellitus, type 2 * C/b diabetic retinopathy * C/b diabetic nephropathy - Dyslipidemia - Hypertension - Coronary artery disease - Chronic kidney disease, Stage IV - Gouty arthritis - GERD - Skin cancers  Social History: ___ Family History: - non-Hodgkin's lymphoma - Mother, father, and brother with diabetes.  Physical Exam: ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM: VS: T 98.5 BP 124/78 HR 86 RR 18 Spo2 100% on RA GENERAL: Well appearing elderly man HEENT: EOMI NECK: No JVD RESP: CTAB CV: RRR, faint SEM ABD: Soft, nontender EXT: Right shoulder demonstrates baseball sized fluctuant mass which is erythematous but minimally tender. NEURO: A&Ox3. DISCHARGE PHYSICAL EXAM: VS: T 98.6 BP 122/56 HR 71 RR 18 SpO2 99% on RA GENERAL: Well appearing elderly man is sleepy but easily roused HEENT: EOMI, erythematous conjunctivae, scattered scaling on face appear to be AKs NECK: No JVD RESP: CTAB CV: RRR, faint SEM ABD: Soft, nontender EXT: Right shoulder bandaged with drain now removed NEURO: A&Ox3. Gram stain negative and no growth to date. Crystals negative. Await cultures. Patient was without systemic signs or symptoms of infection (no fever, leukocytosis.) No other joints affected. Infectious diseases was consulted and recommended empiric course of daptomycin. A PICC was placed. Unfortunately, due to insurance constraints, the patient was unable to get home infusion of daptomycin. ___ clinic was arranged for the patient to get daptomycin as an outpatient Q48H. OPAT labs to be drawn weekly. # CODE STATUS: DNR/DNI confirmed with pt on admission # CONTACT: ___ (son) ___ TRANSITIONAL ISSUES =================== [] Follow-up on bacterial/AFB universal PCR [] Follow-up on tissue Lyme PCR  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Allopurinol ___ mg PO DAILY 2. Amlodipine 5 mg PO DAILY 3. olmesartan 40 mg oral Daily 4. Ranitidine 150 mg PO BID 5. Rosuvastatin Calcium 40 mg PO QPM 6. Terazosin 2 mg PO QHS 7. GlyBURIDE 5 mg PO BID 8. Famotidine 20 mg PO DAILY  Discharge Medications: 1. Allopurinol ___ mg PO DAILY 2. Amlodipine 5 mg PO DAILY 3. Famotidine 20 mg PO DAILY 4. olmesartan 40 mg oral Daily 5. Outpatient Lab Work Please check weekly CBC w/diff, creatinine, BUN, CPK, ESR, CRP ICD-9: 711.0 (Pyogenic arthritis) Fax results to ___, ATTN: Dr. ___ 10. Daptomycin 400 mg IV Q48H Septic arthritis RX *daptomycin [Cubicin] 500 mg 400 mg IV q48h Disp #*14 Vial Refills:*2  Discharge Disposition: Home With Service  Facility: ___  Discharge Diagnosis: Primary diagnoses: - Right shoulder abscess Secondary diagnoses: - Diabetes mellitus, type 2 - Hypertension - GERD  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: Dear Mr. ___, It was a pleasure taking care of you at ___ ___. You were admitted for a swollen right shoulder which appears to be infected. Thus, we started you on antibiotics which you should continue at discharge. We arranged for you to get antibiotic infusions as an outpatient in clinic. Please follow up with your PCP, ___, and infectious diseases doctor as below. Regards, Your ___ Team  Followup Instructions: ___

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: PLASTIC  Allergies: Latex / Nickel  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Facial and neck skin laxity  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: rhytidectomy with neck lift  History of Present Illness: ___ year old female with desire to enhance facial and neck tissue appearance with cosmetic surgery.  Past Medical History: osteoarthritis, significant pulmonary disease including emphysema with apical bullae, bronchiectasis, bilateral aspergilloma and intermittentremote hemoptysis, pneumonia, depression, bilateral breast implants, anxiety, asthma, multiple prior cosmetic procedures incl abdominoplasty, augmentation, facelift  Social History: ___ Family History: non-contributory  Physical Exam: Pre-procedure physical exam as documented in Anesthesia Record: General: wd, thin Mental/psych: a/o; anxious Airway: as documented in detail in anesthesia record Dental: good head/neck range of motion: Free range of motion heart: rrr Lungs: clear to auscultation Abdomen: soft nt Other: symmetric facial appearance w/intact sensation  Brief Hospital Course: The patient was admitted to the plastic surgery service on ___ and had a face and neck lift. The patient tolerated the procedure well. . Neuro: Post-operatively, the patient received Morphine IV with good effect and adequate pain control. When tolerating oral intake, the patient was transitioned to oral pain medications. . CV: The patient was stable from a cardiovascular standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. . Pulmonary: The patient was stable from a pulmonary standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. . GI/GU: Post-operatively, the patient was given IV fluids until tolerating oral intake. Her diet was advanced when appropriate, which was tolerated well. She was also started on a bowel regimen to encourage bowel movement. Foley was removed post-operatively and patient was able to void spontaneously, without issue. Intake and output were closely monitored. . ID: Post-operatively, the patient was started on IV cefazolin, then switched to PO keflex for discharge home. The patient's temperature was closely watched for signs of infection. . Prophylaxis: The patient was encouraged to get up and ambulate as early as possible. . At the time of discharge on POD#1, the patient was doing well, afebrile with stable vital signs, tolerating a regular diet, ambulating, voiding without assistance, and pain was well controlled. Each tab has 500mg of tylenol. Do not exceed 4gm/4000mgs of tylenol per day. Disp:*40 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Facial and neck skin laxity  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: * Please contact Dr. ___ any serious change in your symptoms, or any new symptoms that concern you. * Please resume all regular home medications and take any new meds as ordered. * Do not drive or operate heavy machinery while taking any narcotic pain medication. You may have constipation when taking narcotic pain medications (oxycodone, percocet, vicodin, hydrocodone, dilaudid, etc.); you should continue drinking fluids, you may take stool softeners, and should eat foods that are high in fiber. . You may take a bath or shower, but do not get your incisions wet until instructed by Dr. ___. * Keep your dressings in place until your follow up appointment with Dr. ___ * No strenuous activity until instructed by Dr. ___. * Finish all of your antibiotics * Please call Dr. ___ if you have any acute swelling in your neck or face * Sleep with head elevated to help with swelling * do not take any medicines such as Motrin, Aspirin, Advil or Ibuprofen etc . Return to the ER if: * If you are vomiting and cannot keep in fluids or your medications. * If you have shaking chills, fever greater than 101.5 (F) degrees or 38 (C) degrees, increased redness, swelling or discharge from incision, chest pain, shortness of breath, or anything else that is troubling you.  Followup Instructions: ___

sub_contents.sub_content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: PLASTIC  Allergies: Latex / Nickel  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Facial and neck skin laxity  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: rhytidectomy with neck lift  History of Present Illness: ___ year old female with desire to enhance facial and neck tissue appearance with cosmetic surgery.  Past Medical History: osteoarthritis, significant pulmonary disease including emphysema with apical bullae, bronchiectasis, bilateral aspergilloma and intermittentremote hemoptysis, pneumonia, depression, bilateral breast implants, anxiety, asthma, multiple prior cosmetic procedures incl abdominoplasty, augmentation, facelift  Social History: ___ Family History: non-contributory  Physical Exam: Pre-procedure physical exam as documented in Anesthesia Record: General: wd, thin Mental/psych: a/o; anxious Airway: as documented in detail in anesthesia record Dental: good head/neck range of motion: Free range of motion heart: rrr Lungs: clear to auscultation Abdomen: soft nt Other: symmetric facial appearance w/intact sensation  Brief Hospital Course: The patient was admitted to the plastic surgery service on ___ and had a face and neck lift. The patient tolerated the procedure well. . Neuro: Post-operatively, the patient received Morphine IV with good effect and adequate pain control. When tolerating oral intake, the patient was transitioned to oral pain medications. . CV: The patient was stable from a cardiovascular standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. . Pulmonary: The patient was stable from a pulmonary standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. . GI/GU: Post-operatively, the patient was given IV fluids until tolerating oral intake. Her diet was advanced when appropriate, which was tolerated well. She was also started on a bowel regimen to encourage bowel movement. Foley was removed post-operatively and patient was able to void spontaneously, without issue. Intake and output were closely monitored. . ID: Post-operatively, the patient was started on IV cefazolin, then switched to PO keflex for discharge home. The patient's temperature was closely watched for signs of infection. . content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: PSYCHIATRY  Allergies: Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: I'm feeling suicidal.  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None  History of Present Illness: ___ y/o man with h/o POTS and bradycardia who had a recent lengthy Deaconess 4 admission for depression and suicidal ideation treated with ECT, self-presents to the ___ with thoughts of killing himself by overdosing on pills. Mr. ___ has had multiple past psychiatric hopsitalizations for suicidal ideation and one past suicide attempt by stabbing himself in the heart. Although there is no discharge summary available in OMR from his recent inpatient psychiatric hospitalization on ___, Dr. ___ from ___ indicates that the patient's hospitalization was for an apparent acute exacerbation of chronic, fairly severe depression, likely related both to an endogenous depression and to characterologic features that have contributed as much as anything to his current social isolation, poor frustration tolerance, and generalized demoralization. The patient reported tolerating ECT well, had improved mood with ECT, and was feeling more optimistic upon his recent ___ 4 discharge. However, Mr. ___ reports not being able to follow through with the outpatient plan for maintenance ECT due to complications with his POTS causing transportation difficulties. Mr. ___ has had multiple recent presentations to the ___ emergency department with negative cardiac workups. The patient reports being angry with ___ and believes that they are not providing him with proper medical care. On presentation to the ED, the paitent reported walking up and down ___ for a couple hours prior to entering the ED. He reports trying to find some way to resolve his feelings of hopelessness; however, when the urge to kill himself scared him, he finally made the decision to self-present for psychiatric evaluation. The patient reports active suicidality with a plan to overdose on his home medications. He says there is no safe outpatient plan. He reports that he has plenty of pills at home that he could overdose. He denies access to firearms but does report having knives similar to the one he stabbed himself with in a past suicide attempt. On ROS, the patient reports poor mood, +guilt about not being able to see his children often, +racing thoughts, +chronic insomnia ___ hours per night at baseline). He denies changes in his concentration, appetite, or weight. He is equivocal about whether or not he has anhedonia. His main protective factor is reportedly his children. He denies history of manic symptoms. He denies symptoms of psychosis.  Past Medical History: PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: - CAD, followed by Dr. ___ at ___. Extensive cardiac workup (see ___ notes for full details). Reliant monitor implanted in his chest in ___. ___ - Seen by neurology at ___ and thought to have POTS. Did respond to midodrine but with exacerbation of urinary retention. - Urinary retention: High PVRs, seen by Dr. ___ at ___. - S/P self-inflicted stab wound: anterior left chest ___ - ligation of diag coranary artery and resxn of LUL lung tear, repaired superficial lac to LV. Fully recovered per thoracic surgeon at ___ without ___ - s/p diverticulitis with partial bowel resection about ___ years ago - s/p right knee ACL repair - PCP is ___ at ___ ___ PAST PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY: - Major depression with narcissistic character features. Attempted suicide in ___ by stabbing himself (see above). Multiple psychiatry admits to ___, ___, and most recently at ___ Deac 4 where he underwent ECT with good results. Followed by Dr ___ at ___. - Previous medical trials: Depakote, Zoloft, Effexor, Lamictal, Lithium, Remeron, and Trazodone. Currently on Wellbutrin.  Social History: ___ Family History: Denies.  Physical Exam: PHYSICAL EXAMINATION: VS: BP: 118/77 HR: 52 temp: 97.6 resp: 16 O2 sat: 100% height: 70 weight: 244.6 lb  MENTAL STATUS EXAM: --appearance: good grooming with good eye contact --behavior/attitude: cooperative; exhibited no PMR, PMA --speech: normal rate and tone --mood (in patient's words): lousy --affect: constricted affect; appropriate to context --thought content (describe): Future-oriented, hoping to get help for his psychiatric and medical problems --thought process: linear --perception: without AH, VH, delusions, paranoia --SI/HI: denies SI/HI; verbalized safety plan --insight: fair --judgment: fair  COGNITIVE EXAM: --orientation: alert to person, place, time, situation --attention/concentration: able to spell World backwards --memory (ball, chair, purple): registration ___, recall ___ initially and ___ with hints --calculations: quarters in $2.25 = 9 --language: grossly intact --fund of knowledge: able to recall the president on ___ --proverbs: provided accurate interpretations of look before leap, cry over spilt milk, apple falling from tree --similarities/analogies: understood analogy of apples to oranges PE: General: Well-nourished, in no distress. HEENT: Normocephalic. PERRL, EOMI. ___ normal. Oropharynx clear. Neck: Supple, trachea midline. No adenopathy or thyromegaly. Back: No significant deformity, no focal tenderness Lungs: Clear to auscultation; no crackles or wheezes. CV: Regular rate and rhythm; no murmurs/rubs/gallops; 2+ pedal pulses Abdomen: Soft, nontender, nondistended; no masses or organomegaly. Extremities: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. Skin: Warm and dry, no rash or significant lesions. Neurological: *Cranial Nerves- I: Not tested II: Pupils equally round and reactive to light bilaterally. Visual fields are full to confrontation. III, IV, VI: Extraocular movements intact bilaterally without nystagmus. V, VII: Facial strength and sensation intact and symmetric. VIII: Hearing intact to voice. IX, X: Palatal elevation symmetrical. XI: Sternocleidomastoid and trapezius normal bilaterally. XII: Tongue midline without fasciculations. *No gross focal motor or sensory deficits, normal gait. *Reflexes- B 1+ T 1+ Pa 1+ *Coordination- Normal on rapid alternating movements, heel to shin  Pertinent Results: ___ 02:10AM URINE bnzodzpn-NEG barbitrt-NEG opiates-NEG cocaine-NEG amphetmn-NEG mthdone-NEG ___ 02:10AM URINE BLOOD-NEG NITRITE-NEG PROTEIN-NEG GLUCOSE-NEG KETONE-NEG BILIRUBIN-NEG UROBILNGN-1 PH-7.0 LEUK-NEG ___ 12:49AM GLUCOSE-83 UREA N-18 CREAT-0.9 SODIUM-140 POTASSIUM-4.2 CHLORIDE-106 TOTAL CO2-27 ANION GAP-11 ___ 12:49AM ALT(SGPT)-10 AST(SGOT)-16 ALK PHOS-65 TOT BILI-0.5 ___ 12:49AM CALCIUM-8.5 PHOSPHATE-3.7 MAGNESIUM-2.1 ___ 12:49AM VIT B12-172* FOLATE-10.8 ___ 12:49AM TSH-1.2 ___ 12:49AM ASA-NEG ETHANOL-NEG ACETMNPHN-NEG bnzodzpn-NEG barbitrt-NEG tricyclic-NEG ___ 12:49AM WBC-7.0 RBC-4.19* HGB-12.7* HCT-36.0* MCV-86 MCH-30.2 MCHC-35.2* RDW-13.9 ___ 12:49AM NEUTS-67.5 ___ MONOS-5.2 EOS-1.3 BASOS-0.6 ___ 12:49AM PLT COUNT-208  Brief Hospital Course: Psychiatric: Mr. ___ was admitted to the inpatient psychiatric service on ___ on ___. He settled into the milieu uneventfully. At his initial meeting with his care team, he was tearful and depressed, discussing his recent thoughts of suicide following his multiple medical admissions for symptoms of POTS, since being discharged from ___ three weeks prior. The team spoke with his outpatient psychiatrist Dr. ___ provided the collateral information that Mr. ___ had been on a great variety of anti-depressant regimens in the past with little to no effect on his mood. Dr. ___ that the only major improvmement he had ever seen in the patient's depression and suicidality occured after his recent course of ECT. The team then contacted Dr. ___ an ECT consultation. He was evaluated by medicine and anesthesia and found to be low-risk for this treatment modality. He started his first session of ECT on ___ and had a total of 5 sessions while in house. The team also opted to augment his medical antidepressant therapy of wellbutrin SR with abilify. His abilify dose was titrated up throughout his admission. When his abilify dose reached 20 mg, he began to experience some akathisia. His dose was then decreased to 15 mg, he he tolerated this well. He frequently spent time in the common areas of the psychiatric unit and attended groups regularly with good particpation. The team worked with Mr. ___ to find individuals who might be able to help him to complete ECT as an outpatient, but he was not able to identify anyone. He ultimately decided that he would be willing to pay someone to bring him to and from ECT. He could then be observed at home by a friend, who was free during the day but who was unable to drive. By the time of discharge, his mood had brigthened and he was no longer have suicidal thoughts. The team spoke with his outpatient psychiatrist, Dr. ___, ___ his outpatient PCP, ___, to update them on his progress and discharge plan. Dr. ___ ECT ___, and the inpatient team agreed that he would be discharged home on ___ to follow up at the ___ the following ___ to begin maintenance therapy. He would receive one session per week for four weeks, and then would move to one session every 2 weeks. He was scheduled to see his Psychiatrist and PCP within ___ week of discharge. When he left the unit, he was future-oriented, looking forward to seeing his kids at his son's football game this weekend and returning to work. He voiced strategies to cope with future suicidal thoughts, including distracting himself with work or TV, reaching out to a friend, or contacting one of his care providers if necessary. Medical: Mr. ___ was continued on his outpatient midodrine and pindolol for postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome while on the unit. He experienced intermittent light-headedness, but had no syncopal episodes. His blood pressure was consistently within the normal range. The team scheduled an apppointment for him at the ___ Clinics for early ___. The clinic was hopeful that they could fit him into a cancellation spot before that time. The team also made him a follow up appointment with his PCP ___ for the week after discharge. Legal: ___ Safety: Mr. ___ was maintained on q 15 minute checks and did not have any behavioral triggers during his stay. Group/Milieu: Over the course of his admission, the patient participated well in groups. It was recommended to him that he attend an outpatient interpersonal/psychodynamic group to address his ongoing pattern of difficulty within relationships, something that contributes to his depression and suicidal thoughts. He was provided with the phone numbers of several groups at ___ that may meet his needs. He agreed to consider contacting them for an interview.  Medications on Admission: - Pindolol 10 mg po BID - Midodrine 5 mg po BID - Wellbutrin 200 mg po BID - Ambien 10 mg po QHS  Discharge Medications: 1. pindolol 5 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO BID (2 times a day). Disp:*120 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0* 2. midodrine 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a day). Disp:*60 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0* 3. bupropion HCl 100 mg Tablet Sustained Release Sig: Two (2) Tablet Sustained Release PO BID (2 times a day). Disp:*120 Tablet Sustained Release(s)* Refills:*0* 4. aripiprazole 15 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily). Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0* 5. zolpidem 5 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO HS (at bedtime). Disp:*60 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: I. Major Depression, severe recurrent II. defer III. POTS syndrome (orthostatic tachycardia syndrome), diverticulitis IV. social isolation, multiple recent medical hospitalizations V. 55  Discharge Condition: Vitals 97.9 57 107/61 12 98% RA MSE: General: well groomed, good eye contact, looking stated age, pleasant and cooperative Speech: regular Mood/Affect: 'good'/euthymic T. Form: linear T. Content: denies hallucinations. Ongoing improvement in suicidality, denies any current suicidal thoughts. No longer hopeless. No paranoia or delusuions. More future oriented, reflective on social interactions Cognition : a and o x 3 J/I: fair/fair  Discharge Instructions: You have been started on a new medication called abilify. Please take all your medications as prescribed and follow up at the appointments listed below.  Followup Instructions: ___

fields.入院情况: History of Present Illness: ___ y/o man with h/o POTS and bradycardia who had a recent lengthy Deaconess 4 admission for depression and suicidal ideation treated with ECT, self-presents to the ___ with thoughts of killing himself by overdosing on pills. Mr. ___ has had multiple past psychiatric hopsitalizations for suicidal ideation and one past suicide attempt by stabbing himself in the heart. Although there is no discharge summary available in OMR from his recent inpatient psychiatric hospitalization on ___, Dr. ___ from ___ indicates that the patient's hospitalization was for an apparent acute exacerbation of chronic, fairly severe depression, likely related both to an endogenous depression and to characterologic features that have contributed as much as anything to his current social isolation, poor frustration tolerance, and generalized demoralization. The patient reported tolerating ECT well, had improved mood with ECT, and was feeling more optimistic upon his recent ___ 4 discharge. However, Mr. ___ reports not being able to follow through with the outpatient plan for maintenance ECT due to complications with his POTS causing transportation difficulties. Mr. ___ has had multiple recent presentations to the ___ emergency department with negative cardiac workups. The patient reports being angry with ___ and believes that they are not providing him with proper medical care. On presentation to the ED, the paitent reported walking up and down ___ for a couple hours prior to entering the ED. He reports trying to find some way to resolve his feelings of hopelessness; however, when the urge to kill himself scared him, he finally made the decision to self-present for psychiatric evaluation. The patient reports active suicidality with a plan to overdose on his home medications. He says there is no safe outpatient plan. He reports that he has plenty of pills at home that he could overdose. He denies access to firearms but does report having knives similar to the one he stabbed himself with in a past suicide attempt. On ROS, the patient reports poor mood, +guilt about not being able to see his children often, +racing thoughts, +chronic insomnia ___ hours per night at baseline). He denies changes in his concentration, appetite, or weight. He is equivocal about whether or not he has anhedonia. His main protective factor is reportedly his children. He denies history of manic symptoms. He denies symptoms of psychosis.  Past Medical History: PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: - CAD, followed by Dr. ___ at ___. Extensive cardiac workup (see ___ notes for full details). Reliant monitor implanted in his chest in ___. ___ - Seen by neurology at ___ and thought to have POTS. Did respond to midodrine but with exacerbation of urinary retention. - Urinary retention: High PVRs, seen by Dr. ___ at ___. - S/P self-inflicted stab wound: anterior left chest ___ - ligation of diag coranary artery and resxn of LUL lung tear, repaired superficial lac to LV. Fully recovered per thoracic surgeon at ___ without ___ - s/p diverticulitis with partial bowel resection about ___ years ago - s/p right knee ACL repair - PCP is ___ at ___ ___ PAST PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY: - Major depression with narcissistic character features. Attempted suicide in ___ by stabbing himself (see above). Multiple psychiatry admits to ___, ___, and most recently at ___ Deac 4 where he underwent ECT with good results. Followed by Dr ___ at ___. - Previous medical trials: Depakote, Zoloft, Effexor, Lamictal, Lithium, Remeron, and Trazodone. Currently on Wellbutrin.  Social History: ___ Family History: Denies.  Physical Exam: PHYSICAL EXAMINATION: VS: BP: 118/77 HR: 52 temp: 97.6 resp: 16 O2 sat: 100% height: 70 weight: 244.6 lb  MENTAL STATUS EXAM: --appearance: good grooming with good eye contact --behavior/attitude: cooperative; exhibited no PMR, PMA --speech: normal rate and tone --mood (in patient's words): lousy --affect: constricted affect; appropriate to context --thought content (describe): Future-oriented, hoping to get help for his psychiatric and medical problems --thought process: linear --perception: without AH, VH, delusions, paranoia --SI/HI: denies SI/HI; verbalized safety plan --insight: fair --judgment: fair  COGNITIVE EXAM: --orientation: alert to person, place, time, situation --attention/concentration: able to spell World backwards --memory (ball, chair, purple): registration ___, recall ___ initially and ___ with hints --calculations: quarters in $2.25 = 9 --language: grossly intact --fund of knowledge: able to recall the president on ___ --proverbs: provided accurate interpretations of look before leap, cry over spilt milk, apple falling from tree --similarities/analogies: understood analogy of apples to oranges PE: General: Well-nourished, in no distress. HEENT: Normocephalic. PERRL, EOMI. ___ normal. Oropharynx clear. Neck: Supple, trachea midline. No adenopathy or thyromegaly. Back: No significant deformity, no focal tenderness Lungs: Clear to auscultation; no crackles or wheezes. CV: Regular rate and rhythm; no murmurs/rubs/gallops; 2+ pedal pulses Abdomen: Soft, nontender, nondistended; no masses or organomegaly. Extremities: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. Skin: Warm and dry, no rash or significant lesions. Neurological: *Cranial Nerves- I: Not tested II: Pupils equally round and reactive to light bilaterally. Visual fields are full to confrontation. III, IV, VI: Extraocular movements intact bilaterally without nystagmus. V, VII: Facial strength and sensation intact and symmetric. VIII: Hearing intact to voice. IX, X: Palatal elevation symmetrical. XI: Sternocleidomastoid and trapezius normal bilaterally. XII: Tongue midline without fasciculations. *No gross focal motor or sensory deficits, normal gait. *Reflexes- B 1+ T 1+ Pa 1+ *Coordination- Normal on rapid alternating movements, heel to shin

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: vomiting  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None  History of Present Illness: ___ male chef here for work from ___ reports having starting on ___ he began to have terrible epigastric pain. The pain was located in the epigastric region but then radiated diffusely throughout the upper abdomen. He also had nausea, but no vomiting initially. The pain continued in waves intermittently for a few days and was initially present after eating but then became present all the time and would get worse with eating or drinking. He on ___ began to have more nausea and tried taking ___ but the taste was so horrible that he vomited. Since then he has had nausea and vomiting with trying to eat and feels that there is like a ball in the epigastric region. He has not been moving his bowels for the past few days but previously no issues, no weight loss, no diarrhea. He denies fevers, had a low grade temp in the ED. He is very thirsty at this time and report poor urine outpt in teh past 2 days. He reports an episode of horrible epigastric pain that occured 2 days after he had what he though was bad fish in ___ of this year and a similar episode of again a few days of abd pain about ___ years ago. With the 2 previous episodes the pain was no terrible he thought that he was going to die. He came to medical attention this time before it became so bad. He does not have a hx of abd pain after eating. He does not usually see MDs, is otherwise healthy. Is helping a friend with a restaurant, here in town for about 2 weeks and was feeling well until this happened. 10 systems reviewed and are negative except where noted in the HPI above  Past Medical History: none per the pt  Social History: ___ Family History: no family hx of pancreatitis or gallstones  Physical Exam: physical exam most notable for: Afeb VSS Cons: NAD, lying in bed Eyes: E___, no scleral icterus ENT: MMM Neck: nl ROM, no goiter Lymph: no cervical LAD Cardiovasc: rrr, no murmur, no edema Resp: CTA B GI: +hypoactive bs,soft, nd, no significant epigastric pain with palp. MSK: no significant kyphosis Skin: no rashes Neuro: no facial droop Psych: normal range of affect  Pertinent Results: ___ 08:31PM GLUCOSE-96 UREA N-16 CREAT-1.0 SODIUM-137 POTASSIUM-3.8 CHLORIDE-97 TOTAL CO2-27 ANION GAP-17 ___ 08:31PM ALT(SGPT)-12 AST(SGOT)-19 ALK PHOS-30* TOT BILI-2.3* ___ 08:31PM LIPASE-416* ___ 08:31PM ALBUMIN-5.1 ___ 08:31PM WBC-8.7 RBC-5.18 HGB-15.6 HCT-43.5 MCV-84 MCH-30.0 MCHC-35.8* RDW-13.5 ___ 08:31PM NEUTS-56 BANDS-0 ___ MONOS-8 EOS-2 BASOS-0 ATYPS-3* ___ MYELOS-0 ___ 08:23PM URINE BLOOD-SM NITRITE-NEG PROTEIN-30 GLUCOSE-NEG KETONE-40 BILIRUBIN-NEG UROBILNGN-NEG PH-6.0 LEUK-NEG ___ 08:23PM URINE RBC-4* WBC-2 BACTERIA-NONE YEAST-NONE EPI-<1 Discharge Labs: ___ 07:20AM BLOOD WBC-7.1 RBC-4.37* Hgb-12.9* Hct-36.7* MCV-84 MCH-29.6 MCHC-35.2* RDW-13.6 Plt ___ ___ 07:20AM BLOOD Neuts-58.7 ___ Monos-5.6 Eos-2.9 Baso-0.3 ___ 07:20AM BLOOD Glucose-89 UreaN-15 Creat-1.0 Na-142 K-4.0 Cl-107 HCO3-26 AnGap-13 ___ 07:20AM BLOOD ALT-10 AST-13 AlkPhos-22* TotBili-2.0* DirBili-0.4* IndBili-1.6 ___ 07:20AM BLOOD Lipase-31 ___ 07:20AM BLOOD Albumin-3.7 Calcium-8.4 Phos-3.1 Mg-1.9 ___ 07:20AM BLOOD Lipase-31 U/S RUQ: FINDINGS: The liver demonstrates normal echogenicity. There is no evidence of focal lesions. There is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. Common bile duct measures 4 mm. The portal vein is patent. The gallbladder is normal without evidence of stones or gallbladder wall thickening. The pancreas is unremarkable without evidence of focal lesions or pancreatic duct dilatation. The spleen measures 8.2 cm and has a homogeneous echotexture. Visualized portions of the right kidney are within normal limits.  IMPRESSION: Normal abdominal ultrasound.  Brief Hospital Course: ___ male with epigastric pain, nausea, vomiting found to have pancreatitis of unclear etiology. Pancreatitis: Lipase of 400 on admission with nausea at presentation. He has had 2 other episodes requiring hospiatlization in the past in ___, which were attributed to not moving his bowels right. He had pain with the two prior episodes, but only nausea with this presentation. His appetite was down and he hadn't been taking food in ___ days due to the nausea. He denies heavy alcohol use. He denied drug use aside from marijuana. He had no stones on RUQ. His triglycerides were 48. He is not on any medications at home. Given this is possibly his third occurrence of pancreatitis over the last year, an MRCP would be a reasonable next step in evaluation to further evaluate his anatomy. His lipase normalized and his symptoms resolved with hydration. He tolerated advancement in his diet and was discharged in good condition. Hyperbilirubinemia: His bili was elevated to 2.4 with most of it being indirect. Question of possible ___ disease, though hemolysis (mild anemia) is also possible. It was stable on discharge and may require work-up as an outpatient. Atypical cells in differential on admission: Resolved on repeat in the morning. Transitional issues: - Establishment of primary care for further work-up.  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. This patient is not taking any preadmission medications  Discharge Medications: None  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Pancreatitis  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: Mr. ___, You were admitted to ___ for pancreatitis and dehydration. your lab work returned to normal and your symptoms resolved with hydration with IV fluids. At this point, it is not clear why you had pancreatitis. I have made an appointment for you with a new primary care physician to further investigate this concern. Please keep the appointments as listed below.  Followup Instructions: ___
