Followed by Dr. ___ at ___. Complicated by gastroparesis, nephropathy. 2. Erosive gastritis per EGD ___. Noncompliant with GI follow up for EGD after ___ hospitalization/elopement. Noncompliant with PPI. 3. Hypertension, uncontrolled 4. Chronic renal insufficiency, baseline 1.5 5. Gastroesophageal reflux disease 6. Depression   Social History: ___ Family History: Mr. ___ has 4 brothers and 5 sisters, all with no known Hx of diabetes or significant medical problems. His parents are alive and well. He reports that his grandfather had ___, but he isnt sure what type.  Physical Exam: 100.3 ___ 16 99RA Gen: healthy male, NAD, no icterus HEENT: NC/AT, EOMI, PERRLA bilat., MMM, without cervical LAD on my exam Cor: RRR without m/g/r, no JVD, no bruits Lungs: CTA bilat. ___: +BS, soft, ND, tender at ___, +voluntary guarding at ___, no masses, mild focal percussion tenderness Ext: warm feet, no edema ______________________ Labs: | 142 | 105 | 51 / 177 AGap=23 | 4.4 | 18 |6.4 \ ALT: 19 AP: 134 Tbili: 0.5 AST: 23 LDH: 326 Lip: 52 13.3 > 31.2 < 278  N:92.5 L:4.8 M:2.1 E:0.6 Bas:0.1  U/A: Bld Mod Prot 500 Glu Tr Ket 15 RBC ___ WBC ___ Bact Few Epi ___ Imaging: CT ___ with po/IV contrast ___: * findings suggesting acute uncomplicated appendicitis with likely appendicolith/retained contrast from prior imaging at base and more distally in appendix, wall thickening, dilation to 11 mm, and surrounding stranding. suggestion of anemia. prostatic enlargement.  Pertinent Results: ___ 06:00AM BLOOD WBC-6.4 RBC-3.27* Hgb-9.3* Hct-27.6* MCV-84 MCH-28.5 MCHC-33.8 RDW-13.3 Plt ___ ___ 06:00AM BLOOD Glucose-76 UreaN-38* Creat-6.8* Na-144 K-4.0 Cl-109* HCO3-21* AnGap-18 ___ 06:00AM BLOOD Calcium-8.2* Phos-4.1 Mg-1.6  Brief Hospital Course: On ___, he was taken to the OR for laparoscopic appendectomy. Surgeon was Dr. ___. Please see operative note for complete report. He did well postop. He experience a hypoglycemic episode on pod 1 that was treated with juice. He felt nauseated and vomited therefore, he remained in the hospital for another day. On ___, his BP was elevated to 223/128. IV lopressor and iv hydralazine were given with BP decreasing to 184/114. An antiemetic was given as he had nausea again. Bowel sounds were hypoactive. Reglan was given and nausea slowly resolved. Diet was tolerated. Vital signs were stable and he was ambulatory. Lap incision sites were intact and without redness. He was discharged home in stable condition.  Medications on Admission: AMLODIPINE - 10 mg Tablet - 1 Tablet(s) by mouth daily GLUCAGON (HUMAN RECOMBINANT) [GLUCAGON EMERGENCY] - 1 mg Kit - use as directed for low blood sugar or passing out INSULIN LISPRO [HUMALOG] - 100 unit/mL Solution - 10 units twice a day METOPROLOL SUCCINATE [TOPROL XL] - 200 mg Tablet Sustained Release 24 hr - 1 Tablet(s) by mouth daily  Medications - OTC BLOOD SUGAR DIAGNOSTIC [FREESTYLE TEST] - Strip - To be used four times daily DEXTROSE [GLUCOSE GEL] - 40 % Gel - ___ Gel(s) by mouth for blood sugar < 60 If blood sugar < 60, take ___ gels and recheck blood sugar in 30 minutes to one hour. FERROUS SULFATE - 325 mg (65 mg Elemental Iron) Tablet - 1 Tablet(s) by mouth daily INSULIN NPH HUMAN RECOMB [HUMULIN N] - 100 unit/mL Suspension - 30 U in the AM, 29 U in the ___ as directed  Discharge Medications: 1. Amlodipine 5 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily). 2. Insulin Continue Previous Insulin Regimen. Humalog/Humalin 3. Ferrous Sulfate 325 mg (65 mg Iron) Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily). 4. Metoprolol Succinate 100 mg Tablet Sustained Release 24 hr Sig: Two (2) Tablet Sustained Release 24 hr PO DAILY (Daily). 5. Hydrocodone-Acetaminophen ___ mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO every six (6) hours as needed for pain. Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0* 6. Colace 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO twice a day. Disp:*60 Capsule(s)* Refills:*2*  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Acute Appendicitis  Discharge Condition: Stable  Discharge Instructions: Please call your surgeon if you develop chest pain, shortness of breath, fever greater than 101.5, foul smelling or colorful drainage from your incisions, redness or swelling, severe abdominal pain or distention, persistent nausea or vomiting, inability to eat or drink, or any other symptoms which are concerning to you. No tub baths or swimming. You may shower. If there is clear drainage from your incisions, cover with a dry dressing. Leave white strips above your incisions in place, allow them to fall off on their own. Activity: No heavy lifting of items ___ pounds until the follow up appointment with your doctor. Medications: Resume your home medications. You should take a stool softener, Colace 100 mg twice daily as needed for constipation. Pain medication may make you drowsy. No driving while taking pain medicine.  Followup Instructions: ___

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: NEUROLOGY  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Long Term Monitoring and medication management for seizures  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: none  History of Present Illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ yo left-handed man with a history of a traumatic brain injury in ___ when riding a motorcycle. He reportedly was not wearing a helmet while riding and became unconscious, lost control and was comatose after 3 days. He was not left with any motor deficits, only minor visual problems and was able to continue working regularly. In ___ he began having seizures. This occurred at a time of significant sleep depiravtion and anxeity while driving. He felt he was going to lose consciousness and he pulled over. At that time, he was admitted to the hospital and had an MRI and EEG. He then remained seizure free until ___ when he had a seizure at 2:00am, hit his head when he fell off the bed and had a generalized seizure for 10min reportedly. After this, he was started on LTG. Next seizure was in ___ while in his office and had a generaliezd seizure for ___ min with confusion lasting 45 min after. In ___ he had a generalized event proceeded by several nights of poor sleep. This occurred at work where he was found stiff, eyes rolled up and making grunting ___. This lasted about 2 min. Prior to this he had started on adderall 5mg BID and this was lowered to 5mg daily. He was started on OXC at that time. He has also had other episodes concerning for seizure that been described that are not generalized convulsions. These episodes typically consist of him seemingly being less responsive in conversation and then asking questions like where he is, what is going on, and amnesia afterwards. After the event he is ok and does not feel fatigued and is oriented fully. In ___ he had an episode at work of yelling and not acting himself. In ___ he had an episode of disorientation while on a bus. Around the time of these events, he had an episode of nresponsiveness and biting the inside of his cheek lasting several minutes. In ___, he had two episodes of tongue thrusting and guttural sounds that lasted 10 min each. In ___, he had an episode while driving to a concert of disorientation and confusion. Lately, these events happen every 1.5 to 2 weeks or so, most recently last ___ which consisted of confusion and difficulty speaking for about 20 min. In retrospect, ___ noted that these events tend to occur after taking ambien several nights in a row. Each of these events has a prodrome of irritability or anxiety or some automatisms like biting the inside of his cheek. As for seizure medications, he was on ___ to ___, he was on LTG low dose (25mg daily). He has also been on OXC frm ___ till ___ but had increasing irritability and this was discontinued. He was then started on Depakote for seizure control as well as behavioral manifestations at 750mg/1000mg. In ___, LTG was restarted at 25 mg daily with a goal of weaning VPA and increasing LTG.  Past Medical History: Borderline Dyslipidemia ?Bipolar disorder ADD Possible PTDSD TBI  Social History: ___ Family History: No family history of seizures. Mom has a pacemaker and father had a stroke.  Physical Exam: General: awake, alert, NAD HEENT: scar of cleft lip repair. scar above his right eye. ___: S1S2 RRR Chest: CTAB Abdomen: +BS, soft, NT ND Extremities: warm, well perfused, some areas of excoriation Neuro Mental Status: He is awake, alert, and oriented x3. Speech is fluent. Knowledge of medical history is intact. Affect is appropriate. Cranial nerves: II, III, IV, VI:, EOMI, unable to appreciate right upper field cut V:facial sensations intact VII:face is symmetric at rest and with activation. VIII: hearing grossly intact IX, X, XII:Palate elevates symmetrically. Tongue protrusion midline XI: SCM/ Trapezius with normal bulk and tone Motor: Strength is full in all extremities Sensory: Grossly intact to light touch DTRs: 2+ and symmetric in all extremities, Toes are downgoing b/l Coordination: no dysmetria or ataxia Gait: Normal gait, heel/toe/tandem walk intact  Pertinent Results: ___ 07:40PM PLT COUNT-249 ___ 07:40PM WBC-6.5 RBC-4.46* HGB-14.4 HCT-40.7 MCV-91 MCH-32.4* MCHC-35.4* RDW-12.3 ___ 07:40PM VALPROATE-58 ___ 07:40PM ALBUMIN-4.4 CALCIUM-9.3 PHOSPHATE-4.2 MAGNESIUM-2.0 ___ 07:40PM ALT(SGPT)-43* AST(SGOT)-30 ALK PHOS-38* TOT BILI-0.2 ___ 07:40PM estGFR-Using this ___ 07:40PM GLUCOSE-136* UREA N-18 CREAT-1.2 SODIUM-141 POTASSIUM-4.3 CHLORIDE-101 TOTAL CO2-31 ANION GAP-___ yo Male with a distant traumatic brain injury and seizures since ___ of multiple semiologies including generalized tonic clonic movements as well as episodes concerning for complex partial seizures with amnesia, confusion, disorientation. He has been on a few different agents previously including oxcarbazepine, lamictal, valproic acid with some success. Currently, he continues to have events concerning for complex partial seizures and is admitted for further characterization of events. Neuro: Mr. ___ was admitted to Neuromedicine under Dr. ___ ___ monitored on LTM. For the first day his home medications were continued and then his depakote was weaned to off on ___. He was also discontinued from ambien at night. His interictal EEG showed left temporal spikes. On ___, EEG recording showed A single seizure was recorded. This appeared to begin with slowing in the left temporal region, but it cannot be localized more precisely. For the first eight seconds or so of the seizure, there was rhythmic chewing or lipsmacking automatisms, and the muscle artifact from this movement obscured much of the tracing. Within 10 seconds, the seizure spread to much of the left hemisphere and remained this way for the rest of the seizure, lasting just over a minute. This event was not noticed by Mr. ___ and had not been identified prior to his discharge on ___. This event was relayed to his outpatient providers, Dr. ___ ___ Dr. ___. Prior to discharge, Mr. ___ was restarted on medication based on his abnormal background EEG. Due to his desire to not restart Depakote and concern for possible behavioral effects if Keppra was started, we opted for Vimpat and started him initially at 100mg BID which we increased to 150mg BID at the time of discharge. Mr. ___ has an appt scheduled with Dr. ___ on ___ for follow up. Cardio/Pulm: stable on room air during the hospital stay. EKG performed during the admission as a baseline was found to be normal. CXR at admission was also normal FENGI: Electrolytes were normal upon admission. He remained on a regular diet throughout the hospital course ID: no sign of acute infection  Medications on Admission: Depakote 1000mg BID Lamictal 25mg qAM Ambien 10mg qhs Effexor 150mg qAM  Discharge Medications: 1. venlafaxine 75 mg Capsule, Ext Release 24 hr Sig: Two (2) Capsule, Ext Release 24 hr PO DAILY (Daily). 2. lacosamide 50 mg Tablet Sig: Three (3) Tablet PO BID (2 times a day). Disp:*180 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2* 3. zolpidem 5 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO HS (at bedtime) as needed for insomnia.  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Traumatic Brain Injury Seizures  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: Dear Mr. ___, You were admitted to ___ on ___ for long term monitoring of your seizures and medication adjustments. Your EEG showed spikes between seizure activity. We weaned you off of your depakote and lamictal in order to try to elicit seizure activity but unfortunately were unable to observe any seizures. Your EEG does have some abnormal activity even when you are not having seizures and for this reason we will continue on a anti-seizure medications. As the depakote had unwanted side effects, we chose to start you on a new anti-epileptic medication called Vimpat. You will be discharged on this medication at 150mg twice daily. We made the following changes to your medications: Discontinued Depakote Discontinued Lamictal Continue Effexor 150mg every morning Try to limit use of Ambien for sleep If you experience any of the below listed danger signs, please call your doctor or go to the nearest Emergency Department.  It was a pleasure taking care of you during your hospital stay.   Followup Instructions: ___

He has been on a few different agents previously including oxcarbazepine, lamictal, valproic acid with some success. Currently, he continues to have events concerning for complex partial seizures and is admitted for further characterization of events. Neuro: Mr. ___ was admitted to Neuromedicine under Dr. ___ ___ monitored on LTM. For the first day his home medications were continued and then his depakote was weaned to off on ___. He was also discontinued from ambien at night. His interictal EEG showed left temporal spikes. On ___, EEG recording showed A single seizure was recorded. This appeared to begin with slowing in the left temporal region, but it cannot be localized more precisely. For the first eight seconds or so of the seizure, there was rhythmic chewing or lipsmacking automatisms, and the muscle artifact from this movement obscured much of the tracing. Within 10 seconds, the seizure spread to much of the left hemisphere and remained this way for the rest of the seizure, lasting just over a minute. This event was not noticed by Mr. ___ and had not been identified prior to his discharge on ___. This event was relayed to his outpatient providers, Dr. ___ ___ Dr. ___. Prior to discharge, Mr. ___ was restarted on medication based on his abnormal background EEG. Due to his desire to not restart Depakote and concern for possible behavioral effects if Keppra was started, we opted for Vimpat and started him initially at 100mg BID which we increased to 150mg BID at the time of discharge. Mr. ___ has an appt scheduled with Dr. ___ on ___ for follow up. Cardio/Pulm: stable on room air during the hospital stay. EKG performed during the admission as a baseline was found to be normal. CXR at admission was also normal FENGI: Electrolytes were normal upon admission. He remained on a regular diet throughout the hospital course
fields.出院情况: Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.
fields.出院医嘱: Discharge Medications: 1. venlafaxine 75 mg Capsule, Ext Release 24 hr Sig: Two (2) Capsule, Ext Release 24 hr PO DAILY (Daily). 2. lacosamide 50 mg Tablet Sig: Three (3) Tablet PO BID (2 times a day). Disp:*180 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2* 3. zolpidem 5 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO HS (at bedtime) as needed for insomnia.   Discharge Instructions: Dear Mr. ___, You were admitted to ___ on ___ for long term monitoring of your seizures and medication adjustments. Your EEG showed spikes between seizure activity. We weaned you off of your depakote and lamictal in order to try to elicit seizure activity but unfortunately were unable to observe any seizures. Your EEG does have some abnormal activity even when you are not having seizures and for this reason we will continue on a anti-seizure medications. As the depakote had unwanted side effects, we chose to start you on a new anti-epileptic medication called Vimpat. You will be discharged on this medication at 150mg twice daily. We made the following changes to your medications: Discontinued Depakote Discontinued Lamictal Continue Effexor 150mg every morning Try to limit use of Ambien for sleep If you experience any of the below listed danger signs, please call your doctor or go to the nearest Emergency Department.  It was a pleasure taking care of you during your hospital stay.

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: Bacitracin / Quinidine / Allopurinol And Derivatives  Attending: ___  Chief Complaint: cellulitis  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None  History of Present Illness: ___ year old male with history of right leg cellulitis and abscess in ___, A. Fib, HTN, and venous insufficiency who presents with left leg erythema, edema and tenderness for 6 days duration. Patient recently went to an ___ show in ___ and noticed increased erythema of his left leg with tenderness to palpation approximately 5 days prior to admission. Denies trauma. Denies drainage or prulence. He returned from ___ on ___ and saw his PCP 1 day prior to admission for worsening erythema. His PCP did doppler in the office which showed no DVTs and asked the patient to be admitted to ___ due to concern over medication compliance. Patient denies fevers, chills, calf pain, flights or car rides over 10 hours. The erythema and pain has not limited his ability to ambulate, work or do his daily exercises. Patient denies recent hospitalization or recent antibiotics use. Of note patient had left leg cellulitis after trauma to the leg in ___ that was treated on an outpatient basis with 10 days of Keflex. In the ED, initial vitals T 98.4, HR 67, BP 149/72, RR 20, 99% RA. Exam notable for left lower extremity erythema and warmth. Labs showed WBC 10.0 with 72.7% neutrophils, Cr of 1.6 and INR of 1.9. Patient recevied 2 doses of Vancomycin in the ED. Upon arriving on the floor patient was ambulating easily, denies leg pain, fevers, chills. He says he feels well and believes he can take his antibiotics at home. ROS: per HPI, denies fever, chills, night sweats, headache, vision changes, rhinorrhea, congestion, sore throat, cough, shortness of breath, chest pain, abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, BRBPR, melena, hematochezia, dysuria, hematuria  Past Medical History: Right leg MSSA cellulitis and abscess s/p I/D in ___ Recurrent Left leg cellulitis ___ and ___ Atrial fibrillation (CHADS 2) Hypertension Hypercholesterolemia Venous insufficiency GERD Tonsilectomy Hypertension Slight kyphosis  Social History: ___ Family History: NC  Physical Exam: ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM: VS - 97.8, 154/59, 59, 18, 100% GENERAL - well-appearing man in NAD, comfortable, appropriate HEENT - NC/AT, PERRLA, EOMI, sclerae anicteric, MMM, OP clear NECK - no JVD LUNGS - CTA bilat, no r/rh/wh, good air movement, resp unlabored, no accessory muscle use HEART - RRR, nl S1-S2, ___ holosystolic murmur best heard at apex with radiation into the axillae ABDOMEN - NABS, soft/NT/ND, no masses or HSM, no rebound/guarding EXTREMITIES - left lower extremity with erythema from ankles up to knees, warmer compared to right leg, and 1+ edema up to knees, mildly tender to palpation. Erythema not extending over demarcations superiorly or inferiorly. No purulence. Right lower extremity with erythema from ankle ___ way up tibia with scar on the medial side of tibia approximately ___ way up. No calf tenderness bilaterally. No ___ sign. bilateral 2+ pitting edema of both feet with venous stasis changes bilaterally. 2+ DP pulses bilaterally. NEURO - awake, A&Ox3, CNs II-XII grossly intact, muscle strength ___ throughout, sensation grossly intact throughout, DTRs 2+ and symmetric, cerebellar exam intact, steady gait . DISCHARGE PHYSICAL EXAM: VS - 97.8, 154/59, 59, 18, 100% GENERAL - well-appearing man in NAD, comfortable, appropriate HEENT - NC/AT, PERRLA, EOMI, sclerae anicteric, MMM, OP clear NECK - no JVD LUNGS - CTA bilat, no r/rh/wh, good air movement, resp unlabored, no accessory muscle use HEART - RRR, nl S1-S2, ___ holosystolic murmur best heard at apex with radiation into the axillae ABDOMEN - NABS, soft/NT/ND, no masses or HSM, no rebound/guarding EXTREMITIES - left lower extremity with erythema from ankles up to the level of tibial tuberosities, receeding approximately ___ inches compared to yesterday, warmer compared to right leg, and 1+ edema up to knees, mildly tender to palpation. No purulence, or crepitus. Right lower extremity with erythema from ankle ___ way up tibia with scar on the medial side of tibia approximately ___ way up. No calf tenderness bilaterally. No ___ sign. bilateral 2+ pitting edema of both feet with venous stasis changes bilaterally. 2+ DP pulses bilaterally. content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: No Allergies/ADRs on File  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: s/p cardiac arrest  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: chest tube placement right femoral central venous catheter placement left femoral central venous catheter placement (prior to transfer she was intubated)  History of Present Illness: Ms. ___ is a ___ lady who is 12 weeks pregnant and was transferred from an OSH after VFib arrest, prolonged CPR, intubation, and cooling protocol. Per family's report, she had been stressed recently as she is in her last year of law school. She took a 2 hour exam yesterday. Apparently was in a verbal altercation with her significant other (police did question and release him). She was doing fine until this morning when her mother noted her to look somewhat short of breath. Then she collapsed to the ground, was breathing deeply in distress and then she became unconscious. After a few minutes had passed and nobody could wake her up, her fiance began chest compressions and EMS was called. EMS arrived and started CPR; initial rhythm was VF. She was coded for 20 minutes, receiving defibrillation x5, Amiodarone, Lidocaine, and Epinephrine x4. On arrival to ___, she was in VFib VS on arrival were T 96.8, BP 73/51, HR 107, RR 10. She had no spontaneous pulse or respiration, heart sounds were not audible, GCS 3 with pupils fixed and dilated. She was coded for 90 minutes at the OSH, receiving defibrillation x3, Epi x11, Amiodarone 300, Lidocaine 100, bicarb x6. Started Norepinephrine and Phenylephrine. After 90 minutes, she had ROSC. Was cooled to 31 degrees and was transferred to ___. On arrival to ___ ED, initial VS were HR 130, BP 95/50, O2 sat 93% on A/C with RR 20, TV 550, PEEP 8, FiO2 100%. Labs were notable for WBC 24, Hct 43, plt 266. BUN 15/Cr 1.3. Lactate 6.9. CXR showed left-sided pneumothorax and right-sided aspiration or extensive atelectasis. CT head with diffuse cerebral edema, no herniation. CT C-spine and CTA chest showed left apical pneumothorax, incompletely imaged bilateral dependent parenchymal consolidative opacities, and right ___ rib fractures. content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: Penicillins / Tequin / amoxicillin / NSAIDS (Non-Steroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drug) / aspirin  Attending: ___.  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None attach  Pertinent Results: LABS: WBC 11.5, hgb 14.8, Cr 0.9 Imaging: CT neck: Left parotiditis with reactive lymph nodes. No drainable fluid collection visualized. I personally reviewed the CT neck and my interpretation is: Left parotid inflammation with swelling of surrounding soft tissue. prior records reviewed: history of roux en y, gastric ulcer. Discharge Labs: ___ 07:10AM BLOOD WBC-6.0 RBC-4.52 Hgb-13.8 Hct-42.4 MCV-94 MCH-30.5 MCHC-32.5 RDW-12.5 RDWSD-43.3 Plt ___ ___ 07:10AM BLOOD Plt ___ ___ 07:10AM BLOOD Glucose-74 UreaN-7 Creat-0.9 Na-142 K-4.7 Cl-105 HCO3-29 AnGap-8* ___ 07:10AM BLOOD Mg-2.0  Brief Hospital Course: #Parotitis #facial swelling #sepsis- She presented with ___ SIRS criteria (fever, leukocytosis) with source of infection from parotitis on CT scan. She presented with 3 days of L facial swelling, not improving with outpatient PO doxycycline. CT showed parotitis with lymphadenopathy with no drainable fluid collections. Unclear etiology (viral vs. bacteria). Blood cultures ___ have shown NGTD. Had received MMR as a child. Seen by OMFS who advised IV antibiotics with lemon wedeges. She was trialed on unasyn with no difficulties (despite reported PCN allergy). She was discharged on augmentin BID for another 10 days per ___ recs. On discharge her pain and swelling had improved. Her leukocytosis and fever resolved. She was discharged with oxycodone (5mg) #10 tabs to help with pain on discharge. #s/p roux en y bypass- continued vitamins #GERD-continued omeprazole #asthma: continued albuterol #Anxiety: continued home Lexapro #restless leg: continued home trazodone Discharge Physical Exam: Vitals: temp 98.1; BP 120/77; HR 64; RR 18; O2 95% RA GENERAL: Alert and in no apparent distress, facial swelling. EYES: Anicteric, pupils equally round ENT: Left sided facial swelling from mandible extending down to neck. tender. minimal erythema receding from marked boarder. no Oropharynx abnormality. CV: Heart regular, no murmur, no S3, no S4. No JVD. RESP: Lungs clear to auscultation with good air movement bilaterally. Breathing is non-labored GI: Abdomen soft, non-distended, non-tender to palpation. Bowel sounds present. No HSM GU: No suprapubic fullness or tenderness to palpation MSK: Neck supple, moves all extremities, strength grossly full and symmetric bilaterally in all limbs SKIN: No rashes or ulcerations noted NEURO: Alert, oriented, face symmetric, gaze conjugate with EOMI, speech fluent, moves all limbs, sensation to light touch grossly intact throughout PSYCH: pleasant, appropriate affect Transitional Issues: [] f/u with OMFS as an outpatient, they will call to schedule an appointment  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list may be inaccurate and requires further investigation. 1. Omeprazole 20 mg PO BID 2. Albuterol Inhaler 2 PUFF IH Q4H:PRN SOB 3. Cyanocobalamin 500 mcg PO DAILY 4. Vitamin D 1000 UNIT PO DAILY 5. Escitalopram Oxalate Dose is Unknown PO DAILY 6. TraZODone Dose is Unknown PO QHS:PRN insomnia 7. Propranolol Dose is Unknown PO Q3H:PRN anxiety  Discharge Medications: 1. Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO Q6H:PRN Pain - Mild/Fever RX *acetaminophen [Pain Relief Extra Strength] 500 mg 2 tablet(s) by mouth every eight (8) hours Disp #*30 Tablet Refills:*0 2. Amoxicillin-Clavulanic Acid ___ mg PO Q12H RX *amoxicillin-pot clavulanate [Augmentin] 875 mg-125 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth twice a day Disp #*20 Tablet Refills:*0 3. Lactobacillus acidophilus 1 mg oral DAILY RX *Lactobacillus acidophilus [Probiotic] 10 billion cell 1 capsule(s) by mouth once a day Disp #*10 Capsule Refills:*0 4. OxyCODONE (Immediate Release) ___ mg PO Q4H:PRN Pain - Severe RX *oxycodone 5 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth every four (4) hours Disp #*10 Tablet Refills:*0 5. Escitalopram Oxalate 5 mg PO DAILY 6. TraZODone 25 mg PO QHS:PRN insomnia, restless leg 7. Albuterol Inhaler 2 PUFF IH Q4H:PRN SOB 8. Cyanocobalamin 500 mcg PO DAILY 9. Omeprazole 20 mg PO BID 10. Vitamin D 1000 UNIT PO DAILY 11. HELD- Propranolol Dose is Unknown PO Q3H:PRN anxiety This medication was held. Do not restart Propranolol until discharge  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Parotitis  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: You were admitted to the hospital due to an infection of your salivary glands called parotitis. We treated this with an antibiotic and it improved. On discharge from the hospital, you should continue taking augmentin for another 10 days. Please follow up with OMFS who will call you to schedule on ___. Best of luck in your recovery, Your ___ care team  Followup Instructions: ___  Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: PLASTIC  Allergies: Latex / Nickel  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Facial and neck skin laxity  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: rhytidectomy with neck lift  History of Present Illness: ___ year old female with desire to enhance facial and neck tissue appearance with cosmetic surgery.  Past Medical History: osteoarthritis, significant pulmonary disease including emphysema with apical bullae, bronchiectasis, bilateral aspergilloma and intermittentremote hemoptysis, pneumonia, depression, bilateral breast implants, anxiety, asthma, multiple prior cosmetic procedures incl abdominoplasty, augmentation, facelift  Social History: ___ Family History: non-contributory  Physical Exam: Pre-procedure physical exam as documented in Anesthesia Record: General: wd, thin Mental/psych: a/o; anxious Airway: as documented in detail in anesthesia record Dental: good head/neck range of motion: Free range of motion heart: rrr Lungs: clear to auscultation Abdomen: soft nt Other: symmetric facial appearance w/intact sensation  Brief Hospital Course: The patient was admitted to the plastic surgery service on ___ and had a face and neck lift. The patient tolerated the procedure well. . Neuro: Post-operatively, the patient received Morphine IV with good effect and adequate pain control. When tolerating oral intake, the patient was transitioned to oral pain medications. . CV: The patient was stable from a cardiovascular standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. . Pulmonary: The patient was stable from a pulmonary standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. . GI/GU: Post-operatively, the patient was given IV fluids until tolerating oral intake. Her diet was advanced when appropriate, which was tolerated well. She was also started on a bowel regimen to encourage bowel movement. Foley was removed post-operatively and patient was able to void spontaneously, without issue. Intake and output were closely monitored. . ID: Post-operatively, the patient was started on IV cefazolin, then switched to PO keflex for discharge home. The patient's temperature was closely watched for signs of infection. . Prophylaxis: The patient was encouraged to get up and ambulate as early as possible. . At the time of discharge on POD#1, the patient was doing well, afebrile with stable vital signs, tolerating a regular diet, ambulating, voiding without assistance, and pain was well controlled. Patient's face was symmetrical, facial nerve and sensation intact. No obvious deficits or swelling. Head wrap in place.  Medications on Admission: Tylenol prn albuterol prn naprosyn prn MVI qd voriconazole 200mg po bid Vicodin prn xanax 0.25-0.5mg prn activella HRT  Discharge Medications: 1. Keflex ___ mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO every six (6) hours for 5 days. Disp:*20 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0* 2. alprazolam 1 mg Tablet Sig: 0.5 Tablet PO TID (3 times a day) as needed for anxiety. 3. albuterol sulfate 90 mcg/Actuation HFA Aerosol Inhaler Sig: ___ Puffs Inhalation Q4H (every 4 hours) as needed for wheezing.  4. voriconazole 200 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q12H (every 12 hours). 5. hydrocodone-acetaminophen ___ mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q6H (every 6 hours) as needed for pain: Max 8/day. Each tab has 500mg of tylenol. Do not exceed 4gm/4000mgs of tylenol per day. Disp:*40 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Facial and neck skin laxity  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: * Please contact Dr. ___ any serious change in your symptoms, or any new symptoms that concern you. * Please resume all regular home medications and take any new meds as ordered. * Do not drive or operate heavy machinery while taking any narcotic pain medication. You may have constipation when taking narcotic pain medications (oxycodone, percocet, vicodin, hydrocodone, dilaudid, etc.); you should continue drinking fluids, you may take stool softeners, and should eat foods that are high in fiber. . You may take a bath or shower, but do not get your incisions wet until instructed by Dr. ___. * Keep your dressings in place until your follow up appointment with Dr. ___ * No strenuous activity until instructed by Dr. ___. * Finish all of your antibiotics * Please call Dr. ___ if you have any acute swelling in your neck or face * Sleep with head elevated to help with swelling * do not take any medicines such as Motrin, Aspirin, Advil or Ibuprofen etc . Return to the ER if: * If you are vomiting and cannot keep in fluids or your medications. * If you have shaking chills, fever greater than 101.5 (F) degrees or 38 (C) degrees, increased redness, swelling or discharge from incision, chest pain, shortness of breath, or anything else that is troubling you.  Followup Instructions: ___

sub_contents.sub_content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: PLASTIC  Allergies: Latex / Nickel  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Facial and neck skin laxity  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: rhytidectomy with neck lift  History of Present Illness: ___ year old female with desire to enhance facial and neck tissue appearance with cosmetic surgery.  Past Medical History: osteoarthritis, significant pulmonary disease including emphysema with apical bullae, bronchiectasis, bilateral aspergilloma and intermittentremote hemoptysis, pneumonia, depression, bilateral breast implants, anxiety, asthma, multiple prior cosmetic procedures incl abdominoplasty, augmentation, facelift  Social History: ___ Family History: non-contributory  Physical Exam: Pre-procedure physical exam as documented in Anesthesia Record: General: wd, thin Mental/psych: a/o; anxious Airway: as documented in detail in anesthesia record Dental: good head/neck range of motion: Free range of motion heart: rrr Lungs: clear to auscultation Abdomen: soft nt Other: symmetric facial appearance w/intact sensation  Brief Hospital Course: The patient was admitted to the plastic surgery service on ___ and had a face and neck lift. The patient tolerated the procedure well. . Neuro: Post-operatively, the patient received Morphine IV with good effect and adequate pain control. When tolerating oral intake, the patient was transitioned to oral pain medications. . CV: The patient was stable from a cardiovascular standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. . Pulmonary: The patient was stable from a pulmonary standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. . GI/GU: Post-operatively, the patient was given IV fluids until tolerating oral intake. Her diet was advanced when appropriate, which was tolerated well. She was also started on a bowel regimen to encourage bowel movement. Foley was removed post-operatively and patient was able to void spontaneously, without issue. Intake and output were closely monitored. . ID: Post-operatively, the patient was started on IV cefazolin, then switched to PO keflex for discharge home. The patient's temperature was closely watched for signs of infection. . Prophylaxis: The patient was encouraged to get up and ambulate as early as possible. . At the time of discharge on POD#1, the patient was doing well, afebrile with stable vital signs, tolerating a regular diet, ambulating, voiding without assistance, and pain was well controlled. Patient's face was symmetrical, facial nerve and sensation intact. No obvious deficits or swelling. Head wrap in place.  Medications on Admission: Tylenol prn albuterol prn naprosyn prn MVI qd voriconazole 200mg po bid Vicodin prn xanax 0.25-0.5mg prn activella HRT  Discharge Medications: 1. Keflex ___ mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO every six (6) hours for 5 days. Disp:*20 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0* 2. alprazolam 1 mg Tablet Sig: 0.5 Tablet PO TID (3 times a day) as needed for anxiety. 3. albuterol sulfate 90 mcg/Actuation HFA Aerosol Inhaler Sig: ___ Puffs Inhalation Q4H (every 4 hours) as needed for wheezing.  4. voriconazole 200 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q12H (every 12 hours). 5. hydrocodone-acetaminophen ___ mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q6H (every 6 hours) as needed for pain: Max 8/day. Each tab has 500mg of tylenol. Do not exceed 4gm/4000mgs of tylenol per day. Disp:*40 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Facial and neck skin laxity  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: * Please contact Dr. ___ any serious change in your symptoms, or any new symptoms that concern you. * Please resume all regular home medications and take any new meds as ordered. * Do not drive or operate heavy machinery while taking any narcotic pain medication. You may have constipation when taking narcotic pain medications (oxycodone, percocet, vicodin, hydrocodone, dilaudid, etc.); you should continue drinking fluids, you may take stool softeners, and should eat foods that are high in fiber. . You may take a bath or shower, but do not get your incisions wet until instructed by Dr. ___. * Keep your dressings in place until your follow up appointment with Dr. ___ * No strenuous activity until instructed by Dr. ___. * Finish all of your antibiotics * Please call Dr. ___ if you have any acute swelling in your neck or face * Sleep with head elevated to help with swelling * do not take any medicines such as Motrin, Aspirin, Advil or Ibuprofen etc . Return to the ER if: * If you are vomiting and cannot keep in fluids or your medications. * If you have shaking chills, fever greater than 101.5 (F) degrees or 38 (C) degrees, increased redness, swelling or discharge from incision, chest pain, shortness of breath, or anything else that is troubling you.  Followup Instructions: ___

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: UROLOGY  Allergies: Penicillins / pseudoephedrine / lisinopril  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: left renal mass  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: Robotic left partial nephrectomy  History of Present Illness: ___ is now ___ years old, I have followed her for the last ___ years for a left kidney complex cystic mass. It is partially cystic partially solid, exophytic from the left midpole, not invading surrounding structures and totally asymptomatic. It has slowly grown from 1.6 to 2.7 cm over the last ___ years.  Past Medical History: The patient has the following new medical problems: trigger finger aortic regurg taking prolea for osteoporosis in spine  Social History: ___ Family History:   Physical Exam: Gen: Appears well, AAOx3 CV: RRR Resp: Normal effort, no distress Abdomen: Soft, mildly distended, attp, no rebound or guarding Wound: Incisions C/D/I Ext: Warm, well perfused, no  Pertinent Results: ___ 07:43AM BLOOD WBC-9.3 RBC-3.69* Hgb-10.2* Hct-34.3 MCV-93 MCH-27.6 MCHC-29.7* RDW-13.2 RDWSD-44.6 Plt ___ ___ 07:43AM BLOOD Glucose-81 UreaN-15 Creat-0.9 Na-137 K-3.7 Cl-103 HCO3-20* AnGap-14 ___ 07:43AM BLOOD Calcium-8.3* Mg-1.9  Brief Hospital Course: Patient was admitted to Urology after undergoing laparoscopic left partial nephrectomy. No concerning intraoperative events occurred; please see dictated operative note for details. The patient received perioperative antibiotic prophylaxis. The patient was transferred to the floor from the PACU in stable condition. On POD0, pain was well controlled on PCA, hydrated for urine output >30cc/hour, and provided with pneumoboots and incentive spirometry for prophylaxis. On POD1, the patient ambulated, restarted on home medications, basic metabolic panel and complete blood count were checked, pain control was transitioned from PCA to oral analgesics, diet was advanced to a clears/toast and crackers diet. On POD2, JP and urethral Foley catheter were removed without difficulty and diet was advanced as tolerated. The remainder of the hospital course was relatively unremarkable. The patient was discharged in stable condition, eating well, ambulating independently, voiding without difficulty, and with pain control on oral analgesics. On exam, incision was clean, dry, and intact, with no evidence of hematoma collection or infection. The patient was given explicit instructions to follow-up in clinic in approximately four weeks time.  Medications on Admission: 1. amLODIPine 5 mg PO DAILY 2. Atorvastatin 80 mg PO QPM 3. Famotidine 20 mg PO QHS 4. LORazepam 0.5 mg PO DAILY PRN anxiety 5. Ramelteon 8 mg PO QPM:PRN Insomnia Should be given 30 minutes before bedtime 6. Sertraline 100 mg PO DAILY 7. Zolpidem Tartrate 5 mg PO QHS:PRN insomnia  Discharge Medications: 1. Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO Q8H 2. Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID 3. OxyCODONE (Immediate Release) 5 mg PO Q4H:PRN Pain - Moderate Reason for PRN duplicate override: Alternating agents for similar severity 4. amLODIPine 5 mg PO DAILY 5. Atorvastatin 80 mg PO QPM 6. Famotidine 20 mg PO QHS 7. LORazepam 0.5 mg PO DAILY PRN anxiety 8. Ramelteon 8 mg PO QPM:PRN Insomnia Should be given 30 minutes before bedtime 9. Sertraline 100 mg PO DAILY 10. Zolpidem Tartrate 5 mg PO QHS:PRN insomnia  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Left renal mass  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: No lifting >10lbs for 2 weeks. No strenuous exercise for 4 weeks.  Followup Instructions: ___  记录时间：

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Malaise  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None  History of Present Illness: ___ M morbidly obese gentleman with PMH of poorly-controlled IDDM2, CKD, anemia, hyperlipidemia, HTN, s/p bilateral BKA for recurrent MRSA osteomyelitis and pseudomonal ulcers presents with malaise and LUQ pain, now found to have likely LLE cellulitis. For the past 5 days, he has had nausea, chills without fever, and left upper quadrant abdominal pain. He describes the L upper quadrant abdominal pain as a spasms that occurs during movement and resolves with rest that started this morning. has also noted a blistering of his left BKA stump during the same time. No pain or warmth over his stump. The blister popped four days ago and he noticed that his LLE appeared more erythematous. Sugars have been under usual control (170s-210s). No chest pain or shortness of breath. No vomiting, no constipation or diarrhea. Denies URI like symptoms, dysuria, hematuria. No new medications or over the counter medications. On arrival to the ED, initial VS: 98.4 107 140/51 18 100% RA - Exam: cellulitis to LLE BKA stump. - Labs significant for WBC 23.1, K 5.5, BUN 37, creatinine 2.8, bicarb 19, VBG shows pH 7.32 (pCO2 43, pO2 19, HCO3 23). LFTs: ALT 60, AST 54, AP 134 - Got Vancomycin IV 1 g x 1, Clindamycin IV 600mg x 1, Cefepime 2g x1 VS on transfer: 101.7 110 110/64 24 99% RA On arrival to the floor, he denies any complaints. Review of sytems: (+) Per HPI (-) Denies changes in weight, headache, sinus tenderness, rhinorrhea or congestion. Denies cough, shortness of breath. Denies chest pain or tightness, palpitations. Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation or abdominal pain. No recent change in bowel or bladder habits. No dysuria. Denies arthralgias or myalgias. Ten point review of systems is otherwise negative.  Past Medical History: CKI Obesity recurrent MRSA/Psudomonas osetomyelitis & cellulits s/p bilat amput. EKG ___ Sinus, 97bpm, no peaked T waves, normal QRS and PR intervals  Brief Hospital Course: ___ year old male with a past medical history significant for IDDM2, CKD, anemia, HLD, HTN, obesity and is now s/p bilateral BKA for recurrent MRSA osteomyelitis and pseudomonal ulcers who presented with malaise and LLE cellulitis here found to be septic upon admission likely ___ cellulitis.  ACTIVE ISSUES ============== # Sepsis ___ LLE cellulitis Patient states that came to the hospital due to feeling generally unwell; however he denied any localizing symptoms such as fever, cough, chest pain, dysuria. He did note a LLE blister which he states he poppped a few days ago. He met ___ SIRS criteria upon admission with WBC 23, temp 101.7, HR 110s with source likely left leg cellulitis. Previous cellulitis and osteomyelitis cultures have grown MRSA, pan-sensitive pseudomonas, klebsiella, grp B strep. On exam, cellulitis appears to be superficial- no evidence of crepitus or deeper infection. He was initially treated with Vanc/Zosyn overnight on the night of admission and then broadened to Vanc/Cefepime/Flagyl (based on prior culture data). Cultures remained negative, WBC improved and the patient remained afebrile, so he was transitioned to oral antibiotics on ___ (Augmentin/Doxycycline). He will complete 7 more days of PO antibiotics once discharged. # ___ on CKD: Cr. 2.8 on admission but then 3.4 the next day, baseline 2.2-2.5. Likely secondary to sepsis. He received 1L NS upon admission. Cr was trended and on day of discharge creatinine was 2.4. Home dose losartan was initially held but was retsarted on day of discharge.  # Transaminitis: Patient has not had any LFTs checked since ___, so unclear if this is new onset or his baseline. DDx includes NASH (if chronic) vs. ___ muscular breakdown (if newonset)Likely secondary to muscular breakdown ___ cellulitis. Currently, very low concern for intra-abdominal pathology as abdominal exam is benign. Downtrended during hospitalization, so most likely ___ muscular breakdown/cellulitis. Should be followed up outpatient for complete resolution. # LUQ pain: Patient initially complained of LUQ pain x1 day upon admission which was thought to be musculoskeletal in nature as he described it as spasms and worsened with movement. Improved by the day of discharge. # Contact dermatitis: Erythematous rash noted throughout back. Per wife, began using new detergent one week ago and rash coincides with using the new detergent. He was treated with hydrocortisone topical cream 1% BID. # DM2: Previosuly poorly controlled but last A1C 8.3 on ___ which is significantly improved from 14.8. He was continued on his home dose lantus and sliding scale insulin. # HTN: Initially held home dose metoprolol and losartan in the setting ___ and sepsis. Metoprolol was restarted on ___ and well tolerated. Losartan was restarted upon discharge once Cr was back at baseline. - holding home dose metoprolol and losartan in setting ___ and sepsis # Hyperkalemia: Potassium noted to be elevated to 5.5 upon admission and was thought to be ___ his ___ on CKD. EKG without any changes. He received 1 dose of kayexalate and potassium then improved to wnl and remained normal throughout the rest of his hospitalization. CHRONIC ISSUES =============== # Anemia: HCT generally upper ___, currently 32% # HLD: Continued simvastatin # BPH: Continued Tamsulosin TRANSITIONAL ISSUES ==================== - patient was initially treated with IV Vanc/Cefepime/Flagyl for LLE cellulits, blood cultures remained negative and patient was transitioned to oral antibiotics (augmentin/doxycycline) on ___ - patient to complete 7 more days of Augmentin/Doxycycline upon discharge - Cr uptrended initially, thought to be ___ sepsis, downtrended prior to admission and home dose Losartan was restarted upon discharge - please f/u pending blood cultures (no growth at time of discharge) - please f/u for resolution of LLE cellulitis - please f/u for resolution of mild transaminitis (thought to be ___ muscular breakdown ___ cellulitis) and was improving on day of discharge  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Glargine 30 Units Breakfast Glargine 85 Units Bedtime Insulin SC Sliding Scale using HUM InsulinMax Dose Override Reason: home dose 2. Loratadine 10 mg PO DAILY:PRN allergies 3. Simvastatin 40 mg PO DAILY 4. Tamsulosin 0.4 mg PO HS 5. Hydrocortisone Cream 1% 1 Appl TP BID back RX *hydrocortisone [Cortizone-10] 1 % Apply to back twice a day Refills:*0 6. Fexofenadine 60 mg PO DAILY 7. Amoxicillin-Clavulanic Acid ___ mg PO Q12H RX *amoxicillin-pot clavulanate 875 mg-125 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth twice a day Disp #*14 Tablet Refills:*0 8. Doxycycline Hyclate 100 mg PO Q12H RX *doxycycline hyclate 100 mg 1 capsule(s) by mouth twice a day Disp #*14 Capsule Refills:*0 9. Lactic Acid 12% Lotion 1 Appl TP ASDIR Please apply to thickened skin area on lower extremity RX *ammonium lactate [Lac-Hydrin] 12 % Apply to thickened skin daily Refills:*0 10. DuoDERM Hydroactive (hydrocolloid dressing) topical every 3 days RX *hydrocolloid dressing [DuoDERM Hydroactive] Apply to wound every 3 days Refills:*0 11. Metoprolol Succinate XL 50 mg PO DAILY 12. Losartan Potassium 50 mg PO DAILY  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: PRIMARY Sepsis Cellulitis Acute kidney injury SECONDARY Chronic Kidney Disease  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Discharge Instructions: Dear Mr. ___, It was a pleasure taking care of you at ___. You were admitted due to feeling unwell with an infection in your left leg. You were treated with IV antibiotics and then transitioned to oral medications. For wound: - Cleanse wound with wound cleanser then pat dry - apply duoderm wound gel to wound bed - then place mepilex border 6x6 or 4x 4 - change every 3 days For dry thick skin- apply lac hydrin Please be sure to complete the antibiotics and to followup with your doctors. ___, Your ___ Physicians  ___: ___

content: Name: ___. Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: SURGERY  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___  ___ Complaint: unneeded ileostomy  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: Ileostomy takedown  History of Present Illness: Previous total colectomy and end ileostomy or Ulcerative Colitis. Jpouch creation. Presented to ___ for elective ileostomy takedown to complete his surgical treatment plan.  Past Medical History: PMH: h/o drug abuse (narcotics, EtOH, illicits) Ulcerative colitis C. difficile colitis in ___ intraabdominal abscess ___ portal vein thrombosis ___ Depression PSH: total colectomy and end ileostomy, meningioma resection  Social History: ___ Family History: Daughter with type 1 diabetes, no hx of GI disorders  Physical Exam: General: Patient doing well, ambulating independently, pain controlled, tolerating a regular diet, passing bowel movements and flatus. VSS Neuro: A&OX3 cardio/pulm: no shortness of breath, no chest pain abd: ileostomy takedown site without wick, small amount of serous drainage, no errythems ___: no edema, equal strength  Pertinent Results: ___ 07:30AM BLOOD WBC-7.6 RBC-4.04* Hgb-11.4* Hct-35.8* MCV-89 MCH-28.3 MCHC-32.0 RDW-14.3 Plt ___ ___ 07:30AM BLOOD Plt ___ ___ 07:30AM BLOOD Calcium-8.6 Phos-3.2 Mg-1.9  Brief Hospital Course: Mr. ___ was admitted to the inpatient Colorectal Surgery Service after an ileostomy takedown. He was doing well. All post-operative labs were stable. His pain was controlled with intravenous pain medications. He was given intravenous fluids. On the afternoon of post-operative day one, he reported passing flatus and his diet was advanced. He was transitioned to oral pain medications. On the morning of post-operative day two the patient tolerated regular food. Pain was controlled with oral medications. He had 6 bowel movements, and was started on Metamucil wafers. When getting ready for discharge, the patient was noted to have a temp of 100.1 and a heart rate of 110 up from baseline of 60-80. He was kept in house. Overnight, the tachycardia resolved and he did not have an additional fever. He was discharged home in stable condition.  Medications on Admission: gabapentin-300mg PO TID Keppra 1500 mg tablet PO BID tamsulosin ER 0.4 mg capsule,extended release 24 hr  Discharge Medications: 1. Acetaminophen ___ mg PO Q8H:PRN pain do not give more than 3000mg of tylenol in 24 hours or drink alcohol RX *acetaminophen 500 mg ___ tablet(s) by mouth every eight (8) hours Disp #*45 Tablet Refills:*0 2. Gabapentin 300 mg PO Q8H 3. HYDROmorphone (Dilaudid) ___ mg PO Q3H:PRN pain do not drink alcohol or drive a car while taking this medication RX *hydromorphone [Dilaudid] 2 mg ___ tablet(s) by mouth every fthree (3) hours Disp #*45 Tablet Refills:*0 4. LeVETiracetam 1500 mg PO BID 5. Tamsulosin 0.4 mg PO HS 6. Psyllium Wafer 1 WAF PO DAILY please keep track of daily stool output RX *psyllium [Metamucil] 1 wafer by mouth daily Disp #*30 Each Refills:*1  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Ileostomy status post ileoanal pouch procedure.  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: You were admitted to the hospital after an ileostomy takedown. You have recovered from this procedure well and you are now ready to return home. You have tolerated a regular diet, passing gas and your pain is controlled with pain medications by mouth. You may return home to finish your recovery. Please monitor your bowel function closely. You have had a bowel movement prior to your discharge. After anesthesia it is not uncommon for patients to have some decrease in bowel function but your should not have prolonged constipation. Some loose stool and passing of small amounts of dark, old appearing blood are expected however, if you notice that you are passing bright red blood with bowel your please seek medical attention. If you are passing loose stool without improvement please call the office or go to the emergency room if the symptoms are severe. ___ bowel movements after an ileostomy takedown with a JPouch. If you have having more frequent bowel movements than this please take the metamucil wafer and if your output is not slowed please. If you are taking narcotic pain medications there is a risk that you will have some constipation. Please take an over the counter stool softener such as Colace, and if the symptoms does not improve call the office. It is also not uncommon after an ileostomy takedown to have freqent loose stool until you are taking more regular food however this should improve. The muscles of the spincters have not been used in quite some time and you may experience urgency or small amounts of incontinence however this should improve. If you do not show improvment in these symptoms within ___ days please call the office for advice. Occationally, patients will need to take a medication to slow their bowel movmements as their bodies adjust to the new normal without an ileostomy, you should consult with our office for advice. If you have any of the following symptoms please call the office for advice or go to the emergency room if severe: increasing abdominal distension, increasing abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, inability to tolerate food or liquids, prolonged loose stool, or constipation. You have a small wound where the old ileostomy once was. This should be covered with a dry sterile gauze dressing. THe wound no longer requires packing with gauze packing strip. Please monitor the incision for signs and symptoms of infection including: increasing redness at the incision, opening of the incision, increased pain at the incision line, draining of white/green/yellow/foul smelling drainage, or if you develop a fever. Please call the office if you develop these symptoms or go to the emergency room if the symptoms are severe. You may shower, let the warm water run over the wound line and pat the area dry with a towel, do not rub. Please apply a new gauze dressing after showering. You may apply a small amount of barrier cream after bowel movements to prevent skin irritation. Please be sure that your skin is clean and dry prior to applying. No heavy lifting for at least 6 weeks after surgery unless instructed otherwise by Dr. ___ Dr. ___. You may gradually increase your activity as tolerated but clear heavy exercise with Dr. ___ Dr. ___. You will be prescribed a small amount of the pain medication Dilaudid. Please take this medication exactly as prescribed. You may take Tylenol as recommended for pain. Please do not take more than 4000mg of Tylenol daily. Do not drink alcohol while taking narcotic pain medication or Tylenol. Please do not drive a car while taking narcotic pain medication. Thank you for allowing us to participate in your care! Our hope is that you will have a quick return to your life and usual activities. Good luck!  Followup Instructions: ___

She did not require Hydralazine this admission. Amlodipine was discontinued on discharge. #CINV: Intermittent nausea in the evenings. History of nausea with previous cycles. She continued Ativan and zofran as needed with good response. CHRONIC ISSUES: -------------------- ___: Fluctuated during admission from ___. Improved after IVF boluses. Prior admissions with ___ attributed to pre-renal etiology. #NORMOCYTIC ANEMIA: MCV 84, could be due to anemia of chronic disease vs. iron deficiency. Her most recent ferritin is normal but on the lower range; therefore, she was initiated on ferrous sulfate as she may be iron deficient. No evidence of acute blood loss. She continued on ferrous sulfate daily and bowel regimen PRN.  #ALLERGIES: No acute symptoms on admission. Claritin daily PRN. #MEMORY LOSS: Noted overall memory issues on prior admissions with poor historic timeline. Most recent CT head showed atrophy, probable small vessel ischemic changes, and atherosclerotic vascular disease which is likely age-related but no other abnormalities. CTM. #INFECTIOUS PROPHYLAXIS: Continued acyclovir 400mg BID.

Chief Complaint: Confusion and falls  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None  History of Present Illness: ___ with history of paranoid schizophrenia being treated at ___ presents from Bourned with confusion and falls. Patient was admitted to ___ on ___ for increasing paranoia and auditory hallucinations. During her stay there, she has been treated with multiple psychoactive drugs including clozaril, lorazepam, trazodone, mirtazipine, haldol, benztropine, and gabapentin. Per ___, it appears that her clozaril was increased to 175mg daily from 100mg on ___ and had ativan started on ___ . On the morning of ___, she noted to have several witnessed falls (no apparent headstrike or LOC) and confusion and she was brought to the ___ ED. In the ED intial vitals were pain 0, T97.5, HR 95, BP 117/72, RR 20 O2 97%RA. Initial exam was notable for AAO to person and place. Initial labs were notable for WBC of 13.9 with 76%PMN. Otherwise Chem10, CBC, LFTs, and serum tox were wnl. UA negative with Utox pending. Head CT and CXR showed no acute process. She was admitted to medicine for further management.  On the floor, patient can report that she has been treated at ___ and has been falling today. She states on her own that she thinks it was due to increased dose of clozaril. She notes some urinary urgancy, but denies fevers, chills, or headache. No shortness of breath or cough. No chest pain. No nausea vomiting or diarrhea. Review of systems is otherwise unremarkable.  Past Medical History: -Paranoid schizophrenia -DM2 -?Parkinsonism -Glaucoma  Social History: ___ Family History: Unable to obtain from patient.  Physical Exam: ============================= ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM ============================= Vitals- 97.6 134/76 87 18 97%RA General- Alert, oriented to person. Unable to answer year, and not oriented to ___, but does know she has recently been at ___. Somewhat difficult to engage, but is redirectable and cooperative to exam with repeated requests. HEENT- Sclera anicteric, dry MM, oropharynx clear Neck- supple, JVP not elevated, no LAD Lungs- Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, rales, ronchi CV- Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2, no murmurs, rubs, gallops Abdomen- Obese, soft, mildly tender diffusely, non-distended, bowel sounds present, no rebound tenderness or guarding Ext- warm, well perfused, 2+ pulses, no clubbing, cyanosis or edema Neuro- CNs2-12 intact, motor strength equal and symetric in all extremitis. Sluggish 4->3 pupils b/l, Occasional choreiform movements of LUE. . . ============================= DISCHARGE PHYSICAL EXAM ============================= Vitals- 98 130/75 86 18 96/RA General- Alert, oriented to person. Somewhat difficult to engage, but is redirectable and cooperative to exam with repeated requests. HEENT- Sclera anicteric, dry MM, oropharynx clear Neck- Supple, JVP not elevated, no LAD Lungs- Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, rales, ronchi CV- Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2, no murmurs, rubs, gallops Abdomen- Obese, soft, mildly tender diffusely, non-distended, bowel sounds present, no rebound tenderness or guarding Ext- Warm, well perfused, 2+ pulses, no clubbing, cyanosis or edema Neuro- CNs2-12 and motor grossly intact Psych: Blunted affect. Occasionally tangential. The ventricles and sulci are unremarkable. Basilar cisterns are patent. Gray-white matter differentiation is preserved. Scattered opacification seen within the right mastoid air cells. Mild mucosal thickening seen in maxillary sinuses and left frontoethmoidal recess. Other included paranasal sinuses and left mastoids are clear. Skull and extracranial soft tissues are unremarkable. She remained afebrile and hemodynamically stable. She was not orthostatic. Infectious workup (UA and CXR) was not concerning for acute infection. Inpatient Psychiatry evaluated the patient and recommended restarting the clozapine at 100mg daily, decreasing cogentin to 0.5mg BID, and stopping the mirtazapine. If possible, she should have her trazadone and cogentin discontinued in the future. Her medications were adjusted and she passed evaluated by Physical Therapy. She was medically cleared to return to ___. . #Paranoid schizophrenia/Depression: She has a long standing history of paranoid schizophrenia. She mentioned to ED nurse that she is 'always suicidal', although denies SI during admission. Her medications were adjusted as per above by the Inpatient Psychiatry team and she had a 1:1 sitter for safety. ============================= TRANSITIONAL ISSUES ============================= - Patient medically cleared to return to ___. - Inpatient psych adjusted her psychiatric medications. She would benefit from ultimately discontinuing her trazadone and cogentin.  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list may be inaccurate and requires futher investigation. 1. Clozapine 175 mg PO HS 2. Aspirin 81 mg PO DAILY 3. Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID 4. Senna with Docusate Sodium (sennosides-docusate sodium) 8.6-50 mg oral bid 5. Lorazepam 0.5 mg PO Q8H:PRN agitation 6. TraZODone 100 mg PO HS:PRN insomnia 7. Mylanta 30 mL oral q4 hr prn dyspepsia 8. Milk of Magnesia 30 mL PO QHS:PRN constipation 9. Haloperidol 5 mg PO Q6H:PRN agitation 10. Acetaminophen 650 mg PO Q4H:PRN pain 11. Benztropine Mesylate 1 mg PO BID 12. Gabapentin 400 mg PO TID 13. Paroxetine 40 mg PO QHS 14. Atorvastatin 80 mg PO DAILY 15. Omeprazole 20 mg PO BID 16. Mirtazapine 7.5 mg PO HS 17. travoprost 0.004 % ophthalmic qhs  Discharge Medications: 1. Acetaminophen 650 mg PO Q4H:PRN pain 2. Aspirin 81 mg PO DAILY 3. Benztropine Mesylate 0.5 mg PO BID 4. Clozapine 100 mg PO HS 5. Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID 6. Haloperidol 5 mg PO Q6H:PRN agitation 7. Lorazepam 0.5 mg PO Q8H:PRN agitation 8. Omeprazole 20 mg PO BID 9. Paroxetine 40 mg PO QHS 10. Atorvastatin 80 mg PO DAILY 11. Gabapentin 400 mg PO TID 12. Milk of Magnesia 30 mL PO QHS:PRN constipation 13. Mylanta 30 mL oral q4 hr prn dyspepsia 14. Senna with Docusate Sodium (sennosides-docusate sodium) 8.6-50 mg oral bid 15. travoprost 0.004 % ophthalmic qhs 16. TraZODone 100 mg PO HS:PRN insomnia  Discharge Disposition: Extended Care  Facility: ___  Discharge Diagnosis: Primary diagnosis: - Confusion Secondary diagnoses: - Paranoid Schizophrenia  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Confused - sometimes. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: Dear Ms. ___, It was our pleasure participating in your care. You were admitted on ___ after being dizzy and having some falls after your medications were adjusted. You were seen by the Psychiatry who recommended decreasing your medications. You heart rate and blood pressure were all stable. It will be very important that you drink lots of fluid and take your medications as prescribed.  Followup Instructions: ___

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: No Drug Allergy Information on File  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Abdominal Pain  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: EGD  History of Present Illness: ___ y/o hispanic female visiting from ___ who presents with complaints of abdominal pain of of four days duration. Patient lives in the country side of ___, having contact with chickens and cows. She drinks water from a well. She complains of having frequent watery stools over the last year, not noting blood in her stool, but has noted mucous. Her son, who currently lives in ___, has been treated for similar symptoms in the past for amebomas. Patient endorses a 30 pound weight loss of the last year, but says it was intentional to combat her diabetes. She does not describe, however, any restricted diet or exercise to explain the weight. Four days prior to presentation, the patient developed severe ___ epigastric pain. She has noted black stools since that time and has experienced some light headedness. She denies any fever/chills. She has not had any shortness of breath, chest pain, or palpitatons. To treat her abdominal pain she was taking NSAIDS.  Past Medical History: Hysterectomy ___ to fibroids Diabetes  Social History: ___ Family History: No history of GI malignancy or other disorders. Son has had ___.  Physical Exam: Vitals: T: 98.1 BP:120/84 HR:71 RR: 12 O2Sat: 98% on RA GEN: Well-appearing, well-nourished, no acute distress HEENT: EOMI, PERRL, sclera anicteric, no epistaxis or rhinorrhea, MMM, OP Clear NECK: No JVD, carotid pulses brisk, no bruits, no cervical lymphadenopathy, trachea midline COR: RRR, no M/G/R, normal S1 S2 PULM: Lungs CTAB, no W/R/R ABD: Soft, mild tenderness in epigastrum, ND, +BS, no HSM, no masses EXT: No C/C/E, no palpable cords NEURO: alert, oriented to person, place, and time. CN II  XII grossly intact. Moves all 4 extremities. Strength ___ in upper and lower extremities. Patellar DTR +1. Plantar reflex downgoing. No gait disturbance. No cerebellar dysfunction. SKIN: No jaundice, cyanosis, or gross dermatitis. No ecchymoses.
