There has been interval decrease in the amount of pneumocephalus and rightward shift of midline structures, now measuring 4 mm. There is overall less edema and mass effect, as the sulci are better defined on this study. There is no intraparenchymal hemorrhage or right extra- axial collection. Basal cisterns are patent. Burr holes within the left frontal and parietal calvarium are unchanged. IMPRESSION: Left subdural collection, unchanged in size, with slight decrease in rightward shift of midline structures and sulcal effacement. CT thorax ___ BASES: There are small bilateral pleural effusions, with associated atelectasis. Pacer wires are incompletely imaged on this study. Median sternotomy and surgical clips are consistent with history of CABG. There is no pericardial effusion. CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: The liver, gallbladder, spleen, and adrenals are normal. The kidneys are atrophic, with multiple vascular calcifications, unchanged. Pancreas is atrophic. Nasogastric tube terminates just distal to the gastroesophageal junction. There is fluid within the dependent portion of the esophagus (2:4). Small bowel is unremarkable. The colon diffusely is markedly distended, measuring up to 7.7 cm in diameter, and contains a large amount of fecal material, particularly in the rectum. There is no pneumatosis, bowel wall thickening, or abnormal bowel wall enhancement. There is no free fluid or free air within the abdomen. There is no mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The aorta demonstrates marked atherosclerotic calcifications and noncalcified ulcerative plaques. The origins of the celiac axis and superior mesenteric artery are calcified but patent. The inferior mesenteric artery which arises from the aortic aneurysm, does not opacify. Infrarenal aortic aneurysm, measuring up to 4.5 cm in diameter, is stable and demonstrates peripheral thrombus. The iliac arteries are heavily calcified but patent as visualized. CT OF THE PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: There is significant amount of fecal material throughout the colon. The rectum is distended with stool. There is a Foley catheter within the urinary bladder, with air in the non-dependent portion of the urinary bladder. There is no free pelvic fluid, and no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy. BONE WINDOWS: There are multilevel degenerative changes of the lumbar spine and calcified intervertebral discs of the thoracolumbar junction. IMPRESSION: 1. No secondary signs of bowel ischemia. 2. The inferior mesenteric artery does not opacify, and originates from the infrarenal aortic aneurysm. The celiac axis and superior mesenteric artery are heavily calcified but patent. 3. Stable infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm, measuring up to 4.5 cm in diameter, and demonstrating peripheral thrombus. 4. Distended colon measuring up to 7.7 cm, containing abundant amount of fecal material. EKG: afib ~105. IVCD. LAD. no ischemic changes.  Brief Hospital Course: A/P: ___ y/o man with ESRD on HD and SDH s/p recent evacuation per NSG admitted with altered mental status. . # Shock, likely septic in etiology: Potential sources include urine (though only ___ WBCs with culture pending) and intra-abdominal. Should also consider CNS source with recent instrumentation. Of these most concerning would be mesenteric ischemia secondary to atrial fibrillation - aggressive volume repletion to CVP ___ - continue vasopressors as necessary - surgery consult for acute abdomen - broad spectrum antibiotics Surgical consult revealed a massively distended, diffusely tender abdomen with increase tenderness in left lower quadrant with guarding and rebound tenderness. His lactate level was 8 and he was hemodynamically unstable. I discussion was held with his 3 children. The very poor prognosis, even with surgical intervention was conveyed to them, but they wished to proceed with an exploratory laparotomy. The patient was found to have a completely necrotic sigmoid colon from the distal descending colon to the peritoneal reflection despite good vascular pulses in the mesentary. The transverse colon was massively dilated, but viable. There were two patchy areas of complete full thickness ischemia in the distal terminal ileum. Once again there were strong pulses leading to these ischemia areas. A total abdominal colectomy with resection of the ischemic ileum in continuity was performed and an end ileostomy was made. The patient was taken intubated and on pressors to the ICU for recovery. He remained unstable for 2 days, and then became hemodynamically refractile to pressors with increasing acidosis. A family conference was held< and he was made CMO.  . # Altered mental status: likely related to abdominal sepsis in compromised host. no clear change in head imaging per Neurosurg to explain. - Neurosurgery team following . # Respiratory failure: intubated for airway protection during altered mental status. currently oxygenation well on current vent settings. - continue current vent settings with wean FIO2 by sats - VAP prevention bundle - PPI . # ESRD on HD: - Renal team consulted from ED . # Elevated INR: Unclear if drawn from heparinized line or due to primary coagulopathy (i.e., DIC). Platelets normal so DIC less likely. Received vitamin K and FFP in the ED with plan for reversal to < 1.4 per NSG. - repeat coags . # Hepatitis: likely related to shock liver from sepsis # FEN: NPO . # ACCESS: Right femoral line, AV fistula . # PPx: no heparin given, BID PPI, no need for bowel meds currently . # CODE: CMO . # COMMUNICATION: with daughters and son . # DISPO: ICU care On ___ Patient made CMO after discussion with his daughters and son. Pt expired soon after extubation. Family meeting was conducted, all questions/concerns answered.  Medications on Admission: renagel 800 TID with meals levothyroxine 50 mcg daily amlodipine 5 mg daily simvastatin 10 mg daily omeprazole 20 mg daily terazosin 2 mg QHS furosemide 40 mg BID tylenol ___ mg PO q4h prn ri-gel suspension 30 mL q4h prn dyspepsia bisacodyl 10 mg PR prn MVI daily keppra 500 mg BID senna 8.6 mg bid hep 5000 U SC TID colace 100 mg BID oxycodone 5 mg PO prn pain oxycodone 10 mg PO prn severe pain zofran 4 mg PO q8h prn  Discharge Medications: none  Discharge Disposition: Expired  Discharge Diagnosis: End stage renal disease. Left subdural hematome, s/p evacuation on ___ CAD s/p MI and pacemaker placement, CABG ___ years ago Hypothyroidism Hypercholesterolemia s/p left carotid endarterectomy, with right carotid stenosis and AAA. Death secondary to multiorgan failure and respiratory insufficiency.  Discharge Condition: expired  Discharge Instructions: none  Followup Instructions: ___

Date: ___ Time:___ _ ________________________________________________________________ PCP: Name: ___ MD Email: ___ _ ________________________________________________________________ HPI: The patient is a ___ year old female with a long standing history of Crohn's disease on humira who has chonic RLQ pain and then developed LUQ over the course of 3 days. Her pain is worsened by eating and drinking, even drinking water. It is also worsened by moving. She rates the pain as ___. She has not had a bm for 2 weeks. She was taking senna, colace, golytely for two weeks and then she was straining to have a bm today which made the pain worse, then she had sensation that something in the LUQ popped and a severe onset of LUQ associated with dizziness, nausea and blood in toilet. She has been having vaginal and rectal bleeding for the past three weeks but no stool until today which was a small amount. When she came out of the BR her family members asked her to go the ED because she looked pale. She also felt very weak. She has been having intermittent vaginal bleeding and stooling for 3 weeks. +chills and sweats without fevers. She thinks that she has lost weight but she is not sure. She has been having occasional blindness in R eye since ___ which improves with special glasses and when she eats sugar. No CP. She has SOB when she is straining to have a BM. No DOE. + nausea without overt emesis but she does spit up mucus. + gas/bloating and distension. No oral ulcers. Urinary urgency without dysuria. + dysphagia s/p dilatation x 2. b/l lower forearm/elbow tenderness which is new. + easy bruising. . Has rubber band in fistula at anus - ___ time placed Drainage of green from the rubber band about every day. Cervical bleeding mild with clots guiac pos- brown stool Diagnosis: crohn's,fistulas ED Course (labs, imaging, interventions, consults): Disposition/Pending: Admission Vitals: kub nl GI- admit to medicine, if kub is nl, she'll be admitted to medicine she's appearing and stable for the floor discussed her care with GI on call. Pertinent Results: ADMISSION LABS: ___ 11:09PM LACTATE-0.9 ___ 10:50PM GLUCOSE-93 UREA N-9 CREAT-0.7 SODIUM-141 POTASSIUM-3.5 CHLORIDE-107 TOTAL CO2-27 ANION GAP-11 ___ 10:50PM estGFR-Using this ___ 10:50PM ALT(SGPT)-10 AST(SGOT)-13 ALK PHOS-44 TOT BILI-0.2 ___ 10:50PM LIPASE-38 ___ 10:50PM ALBUMIN-3.8 CALCIUM-8.3* PHOSPHATE-3.9 MAGNESIUM-2.0 ___ 10:50PM HCG-<5 ___ 10:50PM WBC-4.9 RBC-3.79* HGB-9.0* HCT-30.5* MCV-81* MCH-23.7* MCHC-29.4* RDW-15.9* ___ 10:50PM NEUTS-54.1 ___ MONOS-7.5 EOS-10.6* BASOS-0.5 ___ 10:50PM PLT COUNT-443* ___ 10:50PM ___ PTT-29.9 ___ ========================== DISCHARGE LABS: ___ 06:20AM BLOOD WBC-3.4* RBC-3.30* Hgb-8.0* Hct-26.2* MCV-80* MCH-24.1* MCHC-30.3* RDW-15.9* Plt ___ ___ 06:20AM BLOOD Plt ___ ___ 10:50PM BLOOD ESR-5 ___ 07:25AM BLOOD Glucose-89 UreaN-5* Creat-0.7 Na-137 K-4.1 Cl-104 HCO3-26 AnGap-11 ___ 07:25AM BLOOD Mg-1.8 ___ 06:20AM BLOOD Calcium-8.3* Phos-4.0 Mg-1.8 Iron-9* ___ 06:20AM BLOOD calTIBC-311 Ferritn-8.8* TRF-239 ___ 10:50PM BLOOD HCG-<5 ___ 10:50PM BLOOD CRP-0.2 . ============================ MICROBIOLOGY: Blood Culture, Routine (Final ___: NO GROWTH. Urine culture: ___ CULTURE (Final ___: MIXED BACTERIAL FLORA ( >= 3 COLONY TYPES), CONSISTENT WITH SKIN AND/OR GENITAL CONTAMINATION. Chlamydia trachomatis, Nucleic Acid Probe, with Amplification (Final ___: Negative for Chlamydia trachomatis by PANTHER System, APTIMA COMBO 2 Assay. NEISSERIA GONORRHOEAE (GC), NUCLEIC ACID PROBE, WITH AMPLIFICATION (Final ___: Negative for Neisseria gonorrhoeae by PANTHER System, APTIMA COMBO 2 Assay. ============================ CXR: IMPRESSION: PA and lateral chest compared to ___:  Lungs are clear. There is no pneumothorax or pleural effusion. Cardiomediastinal and hilar silhouettes and pleural surfaces are normal.  I see no displaced rib fracture, but conventional chest radiographs are not sensitive in finding subtle chest cage trauma. Focal findings should be marked for rib detail views. . . CT ABDOMEN: CT Abdomen: There are small bilateral pleural effusions and dependent atelectasis at both lung bases.  The liver enhances homogeneously. There are no focal concerning liver lesions. There is no intra or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. Gallbladder is normal. The spleen has normal attenuation and enhancement. The pancreas and the spleen enhance homogeneously. There is no evidence of splenic infarct. The kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete contrast promptly. There is no ascites or abdominal adenopathy. The aorta and its major branches are patent. The stomach and proximal loops of bowel have normal caliber and appearance.  CT Pelvis: There is moderate wall thickening of the distal 20-25 cm of terminal ileum (6:20 33). There is mild thin mucosal hyperenhancement and no significant intramural hyperenhancemnt or adjacent mesenteric stranding. No adenopathy. Although bowel distention is limited by poor passage or oral contrast, there is no evidence of stricture or proximal dilation. There is no evidence of interloop fistula. The appendix is normal. There is a small volume of free pelvic fluid. A left tubal ligation clip is in appropriate position. The right tubal ligation clip is positioned abnormally in the midline peritoneum, anterior to the uterus and not in the vicinity proximity of the right fallopian tube (3: 127).  Radiopaque setons are noted within perianal fistulae which were previously assessed with pelvic MRI. The bladder and uterus are unremarkable.  There are no concerning lytic or sclerotic bone lesions.   IMPRESSION: 1. Chronic disease of the distal 20-25 cm of terminal ileum with minimal to mild active disease. No evidence of stricture. No abscess.  2. No splenic infarct, colitis, or other explanation for left upper quadrant pain.  3. Right tubal ligation clip appears dislodged and is anterior to the uterus. A secondary contraception method may be necessary.  4. Known perianal fistulae with setons. . . FINDINGS:  Approximately 8 mm above the anal verge is a thin fistulous tract arising from the lower anal canal at 7 o'clock position (1002:79) coursing inferiorly along the intersphincteric plane, opens directly onto the skin in the right perianal region. This tract predominantly contains granulation tissue, and minimal if any fluid. Approximately a centimeter above the anal verge is a left-sided fistulous tract arising at ___ o'clock position (1002:82), which now has a ___ in place. This fistula continues to have an intersphincteric course opening onto the left gluteal skin. The tract is adequately drained with fluid. A posterior branching tract from the left perianal fistula has less inflammation compared to the prior study and continues to extend to the left gluteal skin. No new fistulas are identified, especially no evidence of a rectovaginal fistula. There is no evidence of a perirectal abscess.  The uterus is anteverted and retroflexed. The endometrium is normal, measuring 6 mm. A 11 mm T2 hypointense enhancing lesion in the uterus is consistent with a fibroid. The ovaries are normal. The imaged portion of the rectum is normal. The urinary bladder is normal. There is a small amount of pelvic free fluid. Few reactive inguinal lymph nodes are seen bilaterally. No marrow signal abnormality is seen. The pelvic vasculature is widely patent.  IMPRESSION: 1. Right- and left-sided small intersphincteric perianal fistulas, demonstrating minimal, if any fluid. The left fistulous tract containing a ___ has decreased inflammation compared to the prior study of ___. 2. No new perianal fistulas, especially no evidence of rectovaginal fistula or abscess. . . KUB: FINDINGS: Supine and upright views of the abdomen. The bowel gas pattern is normal without dilated small or large bowel to suggest obstruction. No air-fluid level or pneumoperitoneum is seen on the upright view. No pneumatosis or portal venous gas. Two metallic densities projecting over the left hemipelvis are consistent with tubal ligation clips. IMPRESSION: Normal bowel gas pattern without evidence of obstruction.  Brief Hospital Course: ASSESSMENT / PLAN: ___ year old female with h/o Crohn's on ___ admitted in ___ admitted with LUQ pain . #ABDOMINAL PAIN: The patient's pain was likely due to constipation versus functional. She had an MRI pelvis that did not demonstrate new fistula formation or abscess. She had a CT abdomen that did not demonstrate significant colitis or acute pathology. CXR did not demonstrate thoracic pathology such as pneumonia or rib fracture. Her inflammatory markers were normal. She was afebrile with negative urine and blood cultures. She reported not having a bowel movement in over ten days. She was given a bowel regimen of senna/colase/miralax. She was also given 1 liter of golytely, which was effective in producing multiple bowel movements resulting in improvement in her symptoms. #FREE TUBAL LIGATION CLIP: CT scan demonstrated that the right tubal ligation clip was likely dislodged. This was discussed with the patient, including the need for alternative contraception. She was also scheduled for follow up with OB-GYN. #CROHN DISEASE: She was continued on humira. #IRON DEFICIENCY ANEMIA: Her hematocrit was stable. She was continued on supplemental iron.  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Humira (adalimumab) 40 mg/0.8 mL subcutaneous Q ___ 2. Cyanocobalamin 1000 mcg IM/SC Q MONTH 3. Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID 4. Senna 8.6 mg PO BID:PRN constipation 5. Polyethylene Glycol 17 g PO DAILY 6. Lidocaine 5% Ointment 1 Appl TP Q2H:PRN rectal pain 7. Ferrous Sulfate 325 mg PO DAILY  Discharge Medications: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID 2. Lidocaine 5% Ointment 1 Appl TP Q2H:PRN rectal pain 3. Polyethylene Glycol 17 g PO BID 4. Senna 8.6 mg PO BID 5. Maalox/Diphenhydramine/Lidocaine ___ mL PO TID:PRN epigastric pain RX *alum-mag hydroxide-simeth [Maalox Advanced] 200 mg-200 mg-20 mg/5 mL ___ teaspoonful by mouth twice daily Disp #*1 Bottle Refills:*0 6. Milk of Magnesia 30 mL PO Q8H:PRN constipation 7. Cyanocobalamin 1000 mcg IM/SC Q MONTH 8. Ferrous Sulfate 325 mg PO DAILY 9. Humira (adalimumab) 40 mg/0.8 mL subcutaneous Q ___ 10. TraMADOL (Ultram) 50 mg PO Q8H:PRN pain RX *tramadol 50 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth Q8hr Disp #*15 Tablet Refills:*0 11. Lorazepam 0.5 mg PO HS:PRN insomnia RX *lorazepam 0.5 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth at bedtime Disp #*10 Tablet Refills:*0  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Crohn's disease Abdominal pain Constipation Dislodged tubal ligation clip  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: It was a pleasure to participate in your care. You were admitted with abdominal pain. You had an MRI that did not show any new fistulas. You had a CT scan that was also negative. You were seen by the GI specialists. Constipation may have been contributing to your symptoms so it is important to take medication to prevent this. If you don't have a bowel movement despite taking these medications please contact your GI doctor ___ Dr. ___ for further instructions. Your symptoms improved and you were discharged home. Importantly, as we discussed, the CT scan showed that one of the tubal ligation clips has dislodged. It may be possible for you to become pregnant at this time so we recommend using an alternative form of contraception. We have arranged for a follow up appointment with your OB-GYN doctor.  Followup Instructions: ___

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: SURGERY  Allergies: Penicillins  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: Vomiting  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: ___: 1. Distal gastrectomy. 2. Resection of peripancreatic lymph node. 3. Pedicle falciform flap for protection of pancreatic node excision site. 4. Placement of gold fiducials for possible CyberKnife. 5. Placement of gastrojejunostomy rube for stomach drainage and tube feeds. . ___: 1. Reopening of recent laparotomy. 2. Resection of afferent limb, including proximal jejunum, and near complete duodenectomy. 3. Roux-en-Y duodenojejunostomy, hand sewn. 4. Cholecystectomy with catheter exploration of cystic duct and common duct with identification of the ampulla of Vater using ___ balloon catheter. . ___: Placement of a percutaneous tracheostomy. . ___: 1. Wound exploration and debridement. 2. Exploration of midline wound. 3. Injection of methylene blue to check for a leak. 4. Placement of 19 ___ drain in proximity to the abdominal wound at the fistula site. 5. Partial complex wound closure. 6. Placement of VAC dressing.  History of Present Illness: ___ with gastric outlet obstruction and pyloric mass that has been seen recently as an outpatient now presents with nausea/vomiting. Patient was seen last week in clinic after a recent admission for the same symptoms. She was discharged tolerating clears and ensure. Biopsies obtained from EUS have shown high grade dysplasia and significant esophagitis. She had been booked for diagnostic laparoscopy and possible distal gastrectomy vs gastrojejunal bypass for ___ but had several bouts of non-bloody non-bilious vomiting yesterday and one episode this morning prompting her visit to ED. Currently, she feels ok and without pain but has nausea and feels as though things are held up in her stomach. Patient has had regular BM as recently as yesterday and is passing flatus still. Denies fevers, chills.  Past Medical History: PMH: hypertension, hyperlipidemia, gastric ulcer (___) PSH: s/p TAH for fibroids, Left Breast biopsy in ___, surgery at age ___ following polio complications  Social History: ___ Family History: hypertension, no family history of solid organ tumors  Physical Exam: Admission Physical Exam Vitals: T 98.8, P 70-80, BP 100-110/50-70, RR ___, SpO2 100% on RA Gen: NAD, AAOx3, spitting saliva into bucket, burped several times Neck: Trachea midline Lungs: CTAB Abd: soft, ND, Gastric mass could not be readily appreciated on palpation, non-tender, no rebound/guarding, well-healed scar from prior hysterectomy, no cervical, inguinal, periumbilical lymphadenopathy Ext: Extr non-edematous, no cyanosis Discharge Physical Exam VS: 97.6, 93/62, 76, 100% FiO2 35%, TV 350, PEEP 5. GEN: NAD CV: RRR PULM: Ventilator support with spontaneous respiration ABD: Midline incision covered with ostomy bag to gravity drainage with biliary-fecal material. Left JP to bulb with biliary- fecal drainage. J-tube to gravity drainage. EXTR: Warm  Pertinent Results: Imaging: CT Abd/Pel ___ 1. Re- demonstration of gastric pyloric soft tissue mass with gastric outlet obstruction. Overall, the degree of gastric dilatation has worsened. 2. New distal esophageal mucosal hyper enhancement and wall thickening, likely due to recent vomiting. 3. 15 x 17 mm right interpolar renal lesion, grossly unchanged, more completely characterized on previous MRI. CT Abd/Pel ___ 1. Findings worrisome for afferent limb closed loop obstruction in a patient who is status post partial gastrectomy with gastrojejunostomy. Transition point at gastrojejunostomy. Small amount of free fluid. Preserved mucosal hyperenhancement. No pneumatosis. 2. Distended stomach and esophagus with oral contrast may reflect gastric outlet obstruction, also at the gastrojejunostomy. 3. Percutaneous gastrojejunostomy tube in appropriate position, extending into the collapsed efferent (enteric) limb. 4. Stable intermediate density 1.5 cm left renal lesion, incompletely characterized. ECHO ___ Suboptimal study. Normal biventricular cavity sizes with normal regional and low normal global left ventricular systolic function. Mild pulmonary artery systolic hypertension. No definite valvular pathology or pathologic flow identified. CT Torso ___ 1. At least 3 distinct sites worrisome for necrotic small bowel with loss of bowel wall enhancement. The involved areas include the new afferent limb (duodenojejunostomy) and small bowel within the lower pelvis bilaterally. 2. New focal hypodensities scattered throughout the liver are worrisome for hepatic abscesses with ill-defined hypodensity within the left hepatic lobe possibly representing postsurgical change. 3. Heterogeneous 2.7 cm collection within gallbladder fossa with drain may represent phlegmon or postsurgical change. No drainable collection. 4. Increased small volume ascites. 5. Diffuse anasarca.  CT Abdomen/Pel ___ 1. Persistent hypoenhancement of multiple loops of small and large bowel, including the proximal afferent limb as well as the distal ascending and proximal transverse colon, as above. Findings concerning for small bowel and colonic ischemia. 2. Uniform enhancement of the peritoneum most apparent in the pelvis is concerning for peritonitis. 3. CT abd/pelvis was obtained on admission (___) which showed worsening gastric outlet obstruction with a greater degree of gastric dilatation compared to prior imaging. NGT was placed for gastric decompression. PICC line was placed for TPN. The decision was subsequntly made with the patient and her family for surgical resection of her pyloric mass. She presented to the pre-op holding area on ___ for a planned distal gastrectomy. Her intraoperative course was unremarkable and she underwent a distal gastrectomy, resection of peripancreatic lymph node, pedicle falciform flap for protection of pancreatic node excision site, placement of gold fiducials for possible CyberKnife, and placement of gastrojejunostomy rube for stomach drainage and tube feeds. Please see the operative report for details regarding the operation. Pathology report showed a 4.5 cm pT4aN1Mx invasive adenocarcinoma. She was transferred to the surgical floor after a brief uneventful PACU stay. On POD5 the patient developed worsening SOB with destauration with O2 sats in the ___ and tachycardia in the setting of intolerance to G-tube clamping with nausea and vomiting. On arrival to the surgical ICU, the patient was found to be in afib with RVR, HR 170s. She was subsequently given 150mg of amiodarone x1 for hemodynamic instability and her rhythm was normalized on a esmolol gtt. Neo was subsequently started for hypotension. On exam she was found to have diffusely tender abdomen with pertioneal signs. AST/ALT were elevated to 1117/___ with Tbili elevation to 3.6. CT abdomen and pelvis was obtained which showed evidence of an afferent limb closed loop obstruction. Patient was taken to the OR for emergent exploratory laparotomy on ___. She ultimately underwent a resection of afferent limb including proximal jejunum and near complete duodenectomy, roux-en-Y duodenojejunostomy (hand sewn), and cholecystectomy. Please see Dr. ___ report for details regarding the operation. She was taken to the SICU post-operatively intubated and on pressors x3 (norepi, neo, and vasopressin). Given her severe lactic acidosis of 9.7, pH of 7.1 with bicarb of 13, and no improvement in pH after receiving several amps of Bicarb, patient was placed on CVVH from ___. CT abdomen/pelvis was repeated on ___ showing 3 distinct areas worrisome for necrotic small bowel. Given downtrending WBC and benign exam, the decision was made for watchful waiting and resusitation. Given bloodly output from JP drain, patient underwent a CTA on ___ concerning for small bowel and colonic ischemia. Patient continued to remain stable hemodynamically. However, becuase she was unable to wean from ventilatory support, she underwent tracheostomy placement on ___. Tube feeds were started on ___, however she became febrile with purulent drainage from her JP drains. Tube feeds were subsequently stopped and she was resumed on TPN. On ___, her midline abdominal incision began to dehisce with purulent stool drainage. a wound VAC with Malecot was therefore fashioned to control her enterocutaneous fistula. It was deemed that Ms. ___ was not surgical candidate and multiple family meetings were held to discuss treatment options. The patient was continue to be managed conservatively. The patient underwent GJ tube study on ___ secondary to high wound VAC output. Study demonstrated multiple cutaneuos fistulas. On ___ patient was taken back in OR, she underwent wound exploration and debridement and wound VAC placement. Post operatively patient continued to be managed by ICU team. She continued to require intermittent pressors support to keep her MAP > 65, daily wound VAC change. Her right JP drain was removed on ___, site was sutured to prevent leak. On ___, patient spiked fever, she was started on Vanc/Cefepime, pan-cultured and ID was called for consult. Patient's PICC was removed and temporary IJ was placed. PICC line cultures were positive for VRE and Vancomycin was changed to Daptomycin. Patient was continued on Ceftriaxone secondary to positive bronchial lavage cultures. Patient received total 14 days of Dapto, her following blood cultures were negative. On ___, patient's wound VAC was replaced to ostomy appliance. At time of discharge patient continued to receive Haldol prn for agitation, Zyprexa for insomnia and Dilaudid IV with Fentanyl patch for pain control. Amiodarone was discontinued secondary to QT prolongations and intermittent bradycardia. Last ECHO demonstrated LVEF > 55%. Pulmonary: Prolonged respiratory failure s/p tracheostomy (___). Multiple SW evaluations for PSM valve failed. Continue to required Vent support. GI/Abdomen: Patient is s/p distal gastrectomy for invasive adenocarcinoma c/b with afferent limb ischemia requiring takeback and resection of afferent limb, roux-en-Y duodenojejunostomy (hand sewn), and cholecystectomy, now further complicated by bowel ischemia and development of enterocutaneous fistula. Patient had a wound appliance placed over her fistula site, malecot catheter was also removed. Her g-tube remained to have bilious output as well. Patient continued on TPN. Nutrition: TPN was continued due to enteric intolerance. Renal: Patient suffered from acute renal failure requiring CVVH from ___ to ___. Her kidney function has now normalized with now normal urinary output. Hematology: The patient receive 5 units of pRBC for her anemia of chronic disease. Hct remained low stable. SENSITIVITIES: MIC expressed in MCG/ML  _________________________________________________________ KLEBSIELLA PNEUMONIAE | AMPICILLIN/SULBACTAM-- 4 S CEFAZOLIN------------- <=4 S CEFEPIME-------------- <=1 S CEFTAZIDIME----------- <=1 S CEFTRIAXONE----------- <=1 S CIPROFLOXACIN---------<=0.25 S GENTAMICIN------------ <=1 S MEROPENEM-------------<=0.25 S PIPERACILLIN/TAZO----- <=4 S TOBRAMYCIN------------ <=1 S TRIMETHOPRIM/SULFA---- =>16 R ___ CXR: IMPRESSION: Comparison to ___. Minimally increasing overall small left pleural effusion as well as right basal parenchymal opacity, likely reflecting atelectasis, given the elevation of the right hemidiaphragm. The tracheostomy tube and a left PICC line are in unchanged correct position. No new parenchymal opacities. No pneumothorax. Normal size of the cardiac silhouette without pulmonary edema. LABS PRIOR TO DISCHRGE: ___ 02:33AM BLOOD WBC-12.6* RBC-2.55* Hgb-7.8* Hct-25.5* MCV-100* MCH-30.6 MCHC-30.6* RDW-17.2* RDWSD-62.8* Plt ___ ___ 02:00AM BLOOD WBC-16.4* RBC-2.75* Hgb-8.4* Hct-27.7* MCV-101* MCH-30.5 MCHC-30.3* RDW-17.3* RDWSD-63.7* Plt ___ ___ 02:00AM BLOOD Glucose-103* UreaN-61* Creat-1.0 Na-142 K-4.3 Cl-114* HCO3-20* AnGap-12 ___ 02:00AM BLOOD ALT-49* AST-58* LD(LDH)-217 AlkPhos-127* TotBili-0.5 ___ 02:00AM BLOOD Calcium-11.2* Phos-4.8* Mg-2.4  Brief Hospital Course: Ms. ___ was admitted to the ___ surgery service after presenting with worsening nausea/vomiting. CT abd/pelvis was obtained on admission (___) which showed worsening gastric outlet obstruction with a greater degree of gastric dilatation compared to prior imaging. NGT was placed for gastric decompression. PICC line was placed for TPN. The decision was subsequntly made with the patient and her family for surgical resection of her pyloric mass. She presented to the pre-op holding area on ___ for a planned distal gastrectomy. Her intraoperative course was unremarkable and she underwent a distal gastrectomy, resection of peripancreatic lymph node, pedicle falciform flap for protection of pancreatic node excision site, placement of gold fiducials for possible CyberKnife, and placement of gastrojejunostomy rube for stomach drainage and tube feeds. Please see the operative report for details regarding the operation. Pathology report showed a 4.5 cm pT4aN1Mx invasive adenocarcinoma. She was transferred to the surgical floor after a brief uneventful PACU stay. On POD5 the patient developed worsening SOB with destauration with O2 sats in the ___ and tachycardia in the setting of intolerance to G-tube clamping with nausea and vomiting. On arrival to the surgical ICU, the patient was found to be in afib with RVR, HR 170s. She was subsequently given 150mg of amiodarone x1 for hemodynamic instability and her rhythm was normalized on a esmolol gtt. Neo was subsequently started for hypotension. On exam she was found to have diffusely tender abdomen with pertioneal signs. AST/ALT were elevated to 1117/___ with Tbili elevation to 3.6. CT abdomen and pelvis was obtained which showed evidence of an afferent limb closed loop obstruction. Patient was taken to the OR for emergent exploratory laparotomy on ___. She ultimately underwent a resection of afferent limb including proximal jejunum and near complete duodenectomy, roux-en-Y duodenojejunostomy (hand sewn), and cholecystectomy. Please see Dr. ___ report for details regarding the operation. She was taken to the SICU post-operatively intubated and on pressors x3 (norepi, neo, and vasopressin). Given her severe lactic acidosis of 9.7, pH of 7.1 with bicarb of 13, and no improvement in pH after receiving several amps of Bicarb, patient was placed on CVVH from ___. CT abdomen/pelvis was repeated on ___ showing 3 distinct areas worrisome for necrotic small bowel. Given downtrending WBC and benign exam, the decision was made for watchful waiting and resusitation. Given bloodly output from JP drain, patient underwent a CTA on ___ concerning for small bowel and colonic ischemia. Patient continued to remain stable hemodynamically. However, becuase she was unable to wean from ventilatory support, she underwent tracheostomy placement on ___. Tube feeds were started on ___, however she became febrile with purulent drainage from her JP drains. Tube feeds were subsequently stopped and she was resumed on TPN. On ___, her midline abdominal incision began to dehisce with purulent stool drainage. a wound VAC with Malecot was therefore fashioned to control her enterocutaneous fistula. It was deemed that Ms. ___ was not surgical candidate and multiple family meetings were held to discuss treatment options. The patient was continue to be managed conservatively. The patient underwent GJ tube study on ___ secondary to high wound VAC output. Study demonstrated multiple cutaneuos fistulas. On ___ patient was taken back in OR, she underwent wound exploration and debridement and wound VAC placement. Post operatively patient continued to be managed by ICU team. She continued to require intermittent pressors support to keep her MAP > 65, daily wound VAC change. Her right JP drain was removed on ___, site was sutured to prevent leak. On ___, patient spiked fever, she was started on Vanc/Cefepime, pan-cultured and ID was called for consult. Patient's PICC was removed and temporary IJ was placed. PICC line cultures were positive for VRE and Vancomycin was changed to Daptomycin. Patient was continued on Ceftriaxone secondary to positive bronchial lavage cultures. Patient received total 14 days of Dapto, her following blood cultures were negative. On ___, patient's wound VAC was replaced to ostomy appliance. Systems-based Review: Neurologic: Patient was given appropriate post-operative pain medication as needed. At time of discharge patient continued to receive Haldol prn for agitation, Zyprexa for insomnia and Dilaudid IV with Fentanyl patch for pain control. Cardiovascular: She developed atrial fibrilation with RVR requiring amiodarione gtt. She was subsequently transitioned to PO amiodarone. Amiodarone was discontinued secondary to QT prolongations and intermittent bradycardia. Last ECHO demonstrated LVEF > 55%. Pulmonary: Prolonged respiratory failure s/p tracheostomy (___). Multiple SW evaluations for PSM valve failed. Continue to required Vent support. GI/Abdomen: Patient is s/p distal gastrectomy for invasive adenocarcinoma c/b with afferent limb ischemia requiring takeback and resection of afferent limb, roux-en-Y duodenojejunostomy (hand sewn), and cholecystectomy, now further complicated by bowel ischemia and development of enterocutaneous fistula. Patient was started on octreotide 100 IV q8h and tube feeds have been held given significant amount of stool drainage from her midline woundvac. Her midline wound dehiscence and fisula drainage has ___ controlled with a vac sponge/malecot suction. Wound care consult was obtained and wound VAC was discontinued. Patient had a wound appliance placed over her fistula site, malecot catheter was also removed. Her g-tube remained to have bilious output as well. Patient continued on TPN. Nutrition: TPN was continued due to enteric intolerance. Renal: Patient suffered from acute renal failure requiring CVVH from ___ to ___. Her kidney function has now normalized with now normal urinary output. Hematology: The patient receive 5 units of pRBC for her anemia of chronic disease. Hct remained low stable. Infectious Disease: Multiple potential sources of infection, including urologic (longstanding foley), pulmonary (trach on vent), and importantly GI (known GI leak with wound vac with discharge). ___ MiniBAL: Klebsiella ___, generally pan sensitive, likely colonization Recent antibiotics: - Cefepime ___ - flagyl ___ - micafungin (___) - DC for partial abx holiday, no sign of fungemia - vanco (___) - meropenem (___) - daptomycin (___) - Ceftriaxone (___) Endo: No issues, continue on sliding scale.  Medications on Admission: amlodipine 5mg, omeprazole 40mg, Carafate 1mg qid, Zofran 4mg q8h prn, clobetasol 0.05% ointment, docusate 100mg bid, senna 8.6'', simethicone 80mg TID prn  Discharge Medications: 1. Chlorhexidine Gluconate 0.12% Oral Rinse 15 mL ORAL BID mouth care 2. Dextrose 50% 12.5 gm IV PRN hypoglycemia protocol 3. Glucagon 1 mg IM Q15MIN:PRN hypoglycemia protocol 4. Glucose Gel 15 g PO PRN hypoglycemia protocol 5. Haloperidol 2.5-5 mg IV Q4H:PRN agitation 6. Heparin 5000 UNIT SC BID 7. HYDROmorphone (Dilaudid) 0.5-1 mg IV Q3H:PRN pain 8. Insulin SC Sliding Scale Fingerstick BID Insulin SC Sliding Scale using REG Insulin 9. Magnesium Sulfate Replacement (Critical Care and Oncology) IV Sliding Scale 10. Norepinephrine 0.03-0.25 mcg/kg/min IV DRIP TITRATE TO SBP>100 11. OLANZapine (Disintegrating Tablet) 5 mg PO BID sleep 12. Ondansetron 4 mg IV Q8H:PRN nausea 13. Pantoprazole 40 mg IV Q12H 14. PHENYLEPHrine 0.5-1.4 mcg/kg/min IV DRIP TITRATE TO MAP>65 15. Potassium Chloride Replacement (Critical Care and Oncology) IV Sliding Scale 16. Sarna Lotion 1 Appl TP TID:PRN pruritis 17. Sodium Chloride 0.9% Flush ___ mL IV DAILY and PRN, line flush 18. Sodium Chloride 0.9% Flush 20 mL IV X1 PRN For PICC insertion 19. Ursodiol 300 mg PO BID 20. Hydrocerin 1 Appl TP QID:PRN skin dryness 21. Sodium Chloride 0.9% Flush ___ mL IV DAILY and PRN, line flush 22. Fentanyl Patch 12 mcg/h TD Q72H  Discharge Disposition: Extended Care  Facility: ___  Discharge Diagnosis: 1. Locally advanced gastric adenocarcinoma G2T4aN1. 2. Afferent limb syndrome. 3. Respiratory failure. 4. Acute kidney injury. 5. Sepsis 6. Anastomotic leak, cutaneous fistula. 7. Severe mulnutrition  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Confused - sometimes. Level of Consciousness: Lethargic but arousable. Activity Status: Bedbound.  Discharge Instructions: You were admitted to the surgery service at ___ for surgical resection of your gastric cancer. You recovery was complicated by afferent limb syndrome, respiratory and renal failure, sepsis and cutaneous fistula. You are now safe to be discharged in acute rehabilitation center to complete your recovery with the following instructions: . Please continue to keep J-tube to gravity drainage. Monitor and measure an output. Keep securely attached to the patient to prevent dislocation. . Wound care: Mini-wound manager, empty Qshift and prn. Change weekly. Use strip paste to adhere the pouch. For skin use No Sting Barrier Wipe to protect from effluent all to dry. Cover Gtube and drain sites with tracheostomy foam dressings then Softsorb change daily and prn to keep skin dry. . Left JP Drain Care: To bulb suction. Cleanse insertion site with mild soap and water or sterile saline, pat dry, and place a tracheostomy dressing. Monitor and record quality and quantity of output. Empty bulb frequently. Ensure that the JP is secured to the patient. Monitor for s/s infection or dislocation. . Portex cuffed trach 8.0mm: Care per protocol. Target volume - 350 PEEP - 5 FiO2 - 35% RR - 15  Followup Instructions: ___

fields.入院情况: History of Present Illness: ___ with gastric outlet obstruction and pyloric mass that has been seen recently as an outpatient now presents with nausea/vomiting. Patient was seen last week in clinic after a recent admission for the same symptoms. She was discharged tolerating clears and ensure. Biopsies obtained from EUS have shown high grade dysplasia and significant esophagitis. She had been booked for diagnostic laparoscopy and possible distal gastrectomy vs gastrojejunal bypass for ___ but had several bouts of non-bloody non-bilious vomiting yesterday and one episode this morning prompting her visit to ED. Currently, she feels ok and without pain but has nausea and feels as though things are held up in her stomach. Patient has had regular BM as recently as yesterday and is passing flatus still. Denies fevers, chills.  Past Medical History: PMH: hypertension, hyperlipidemia, gastric ulcer (___) PSH: s/p TAH for fibroids, Left Breast biopsy in ___, surgery at age ___ following polio complications  Social History: ___ Family History: hypertension, no family history of solid organ tumors  Physical Exam: Admission Physical Exam Vitals: T 98.8, P 70-80, BP 100-110/50-70, RR ___, SpO2 100% on RA Gen: NAD, AAOx3, spitting saliva into bucket, burped several times Neck: Trachea midline Lungs: CTAB Abd: soft, ND, Gastric mass could not be readily appreciated on palpation, non-tender, no rebound/guarding, well-healed scar from prior hysterectomy, no cervical, inguinal, periumbilical lymphadenopathy Ext: Extr non-edematous, no cyanosis

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: PSYCHIATRY  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: I've been dealing with a lot of depression  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: none  History of Present Illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ M with a PMH of type-2 diabetes (on insulin) and a PPHx of ADD with no prior psychiatric hospitalizations or suicide attempts, who was BIBA this am after taking ___ Wellbutrin tablets. The pt states that he took about 12 Wellbutrin pills this morning after a fight with his girlfriend at 6am. Pt says he took the pills because he wanted to, sleep for a long time. He thought the pills might, make him sick, like to the stomach. After taking them he googled the potential effects, got scared about the risk of seizures, and called an ambulance. He says, I don't think I was trying to hurt myself, but I don't know. He describes taking the pills as a spur of the moment decision. He denies planning in advance or writing a note. He describes the fight with his girlfriend this am as, nothing crazy. He says they frequently argue about finances and she was upset because he had a friend over last night. . He was prescribed the Wellbutrin on ___ by a new PCP. He said his girlfriend has encouraged him to see a doctor for his increasing depression, so he made the appointment. He wanted to see a psychiatrist, and his new PCP gave him ___ number to call, which he did, but he has not received a call back yet. . Pt has been feeling increasing depression for months. He says, anything upsets me, and describes feeling more sad and easily angered than usual. He describes feelings of hopelessness, worthlessness, guilt, decreased sleep, fatigue, anhedonia, decreased motivation, decreased concentration and memory changes. He says he has trouble focusing. His girlfriend will ask him to do the laundry and he will start it, but he won't remember to finish it. He also endorses racing thoughts, distractibility, and some impulsivity (I sometimes sell all of my things, then buy them all back, then sell them again). He denies risky behavior or an increase in goal-directed behavior. Pt denies AH/VH/IOR/paranoia. . . . Collateral: Girlfriend, ___ (___) On phone this am states that pt has been very depressed lately. She believes that he has been hesitant to get help because he has a lot of pride in being able to handle things on his own. She has been encouraging him to get help and see a doctor, which he finally did last week. For her the events of the am were, a total shocker. She does not believe that he was trying to kill himself and is still not worried that he would do that. Nothing like this has happened before over the course of their ___ year relationship. She does have concerns about his depression, which she think has been steadily increasing since he left his last job. She says, his ADD effects his life everyday, it keeps him from being an adult. . -OMR note from Dr. ___ ___: Pt presented to PCP with increasing depression. He denied SI/HI at that appointment, but PHQ-9 was 22. Excerpt from note: - ADD -- patient reports being diagnosed and treated for ADD as a child, and is hoping to be tested and potentially treated again. He reports difficulty remember things at work and at home and is peturbed by this. - Depression -- reports that his depression has been bad recently given his struggles with caring for his sick son and being out of work. Denies any history of SI/HI but does say that he snaps at people and always wakes up on the wrong side of the bed. He thinks he has taken Zoloft in the past and is willing to try pharmacotherapy and talk therapy again. Pt was started on Wellbutrin, referred to SW and referred to cognitive ___ clinic for evaluation of ADD. . . . On interview pt confirmed hx documented in the initial psychiatric note. Mr. ___ stated that he has been noticing increase of depression x last year in the context of loosing job, having financial stress, having poor day structure. He reported feeling low mood, feeling sensitive (everything upsets me), feeling helpless/hopeless, worthless, fatigued. He reports problems with sleep (initial insomnia over one hour), decreased motivation to do anything during the day. He also reports increased anxiety, racing thoughts, increased problems with focus. He admits that he had more conflicts with gf. In fact, gf called police on him in the context of yelling match. Now, he has to appear in court. He took pills after an argument with his girlfriend on ___ in an attempt to sleep or possibly make himself sleep. He is equivocal about whether this was an attempt to hurt himself. He denies planning ahead and states that he regretted taking the medication after he read that he could have a seizure. He told his gf and called ___ immediately after learning the possible effects of the medication.  Past Medical History: PAST PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY: Diagnoses: ADD (dx at ___ Hospitalizations: denies Current treaters and treatment: denies; previously saw psychiatrist when he was a ___, but looked him up recently and could not find him again. Saw therapist in ___ ___ years ago for problems with anger. Medication and ECT trials: previously on Concerta, but has not taken for many years Self-injury: denies Harm to others: denies (see FORENSICS below) Access to weapons: denies PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Pt has a history of insulin-dependent Type 2 DM (w/proteinuria) MEDICATIONS, confirmed with : --------------- --------------- --------------- --------------- Active Medication list as of ___: Medications - Prescription BUPROPION HCL - bupropion HCl XL 150 mg 24 hr tablet, extended release. 1 tablet(s) by mouth once a day INSULIN DEGLUDEC [TRESIBA FLEXTOUCH U-100] - Tresiba FlexTouch U-100 insulin 100 unit/mL (3 mL) subcutaneous pen. 44 units SC qPM - (Prescribed by Other Provider) INSULIN LISPRO - insulin lispro (U-100) 100 unit/mL subcutaneous solution. ___ units SC before dinner - (Prescribed by Other Provider) LISINOPRIL - lisinopril 5 mg tablet. 1 tablet(s) by mouth once a day METFORMIN - metformin 500 mg tablet. 3 tablet(s) by mouth once a day - (Prescribed by Other Provider) --------------- --------------- --------------- --------------- ALLERGIES: SUBSTANCE ABUSE HISTORY: -Tobacco: smokes ___ PPD since he was a teenager, trying to cut back -ETOH: rarely drinks, last drink was ___ when he had 4 beers. Only drinks beer, never drinks hard etoh -Illicit drugs: Smokes marijuana ___ last use yesterday FORENSIC HISTORY: Arrests: Pt states that about 1-month ago his girlfriend called police during a domestic dispute. He describes the dispute as a yelling match and denies getting physical. He has a court date in late ___ for charges filed at that time. Convictions and jail terms: denies Current status (pending charges, probation, parole): see above   Social History: ___ Family History: FAMILY PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY: Per patient: Mother has history of possible bipolar disorder Father has a history of cocaine use disorder Denies family history of suicide attempts/SIB.  Physical Exam: Physical Exam Note (___) GENERAL - HEENT: - normocephalic, atraumatic - moist mucous membranes, oropharynx clear, supple neck - no scleral icterus - Cardiovascular: - regular rate and rhythm, S1/S2 heard, no murmurs/rubs/gallops - distal pulses ___ throughout - Pulmonary: - no increased work of breathing - lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally - no wheezes/rhonchi/rales - Abdominal: - non-distended, bowel sounds normoactive - no tenderness to palpation in all quadrants - no guarding, no rebound tenderness - Extremities: - warm and well-perfused - no edema of the limbs - Skin: - no rashes or lesions noted - nicotine patch present on left shoulder - Feet: - no ulcers, lesions, or open wounds appreciated NEUROLOGICAL - Cranial Nerves: - I: olfaction not tested - II: PERRL 3 to 2 mm, both directly and consentually; brisk bilaterally, VFF to confrontation - **III, IV, VI: EOMI without nystagmus however mild delay in tracking of L Eye noted, particularly when finger moved in towards nose and towards patients L side -**Mild Exotropia may be present L worse than R - V: facial sensation intact to light touch in all distributions - VII: no facial droop, facial musculature symmetric and ___ strength in upper and lower distributions, bilaterally - VIII: hearing intact to finger rub bilaterally - IX, X: palate elevates symmetrically - XI: ___ strength in trapezii and SCM bilaterally - XII: tongue protrudes in midline - Motor: - normal bulk and tone bilaterally - no abnormal movements, no tremor - strength ___ throughout - Sensory: - no deficits to fine touch throughout - DTRs: - 2 and symmetrical throughout - Coordination: - normal on finger to nose test, no intention tremor noted - Gait: - good initiation - narrow-based, normal stride and arm swing - able to walk in tandem without difficulty - Romberg absent MSE He is alert, friendly, cooperative. Help seeking. He appears much younger than his biological age. Casually dressed and adequately groomed. Maintained good eye contact. No abnormal/involuntary movements noticed. Speech spontaneous, fluent, in normal rate and prosody. TP linear. Answered questions appropriately. Denied sx's of psychosis. Reported episodes of tinnitus (ringing noise). Denied clear cut AH/VH/IoR. Described mood as depressed. His affect is friendly, somewhat upbeat, reactive. Child like. He denies S/H/I. He admits impulsive OD attempt in the context of conflict with gf. He denied any hx of physical violence. His insight is grossly intact: he recognizes problem with mood, impulsivity; he needs psychiatric assistance. His judgment is negatively affected by his mood, impulsivity. On formal exam: A, Ox ___, ___, been here since yesterday, person, situation. It took him three attempts to register ___, recalled ___ after 5 min delay. Recognized remaining two items from the list. Digit span: 7df w second attempt; 3db. He was unable to answer question if the flag waves towards the Southeast, where is the wind coming from?. He responded:I don't know. He signed three day notice that was expiring on ___. While being cooperative with assessments and treatments, he continued to insist on discharge on ___. PSYCHIATRIC: Mr. ___ was BIBA to the ___ ED (self-dialed) after ingestion of ___ Wellbutrin pills after a fight with his girlfriend. He endorses this was impulsive though acknowledges acute (months) on chronic (years) of insomnia (falling asleep and staying asleep), fatigue, anhedonia, amotivation, difficulty with concentration, self reported memory problems, hopelessness, and feelings of guilt. He denies suicidal ideation, intent or plan on admission to the unit but continues to endorse depressive symptoms. Cognitive and mental status exam was notable for limitations in attention, executive function, and recent memory. He demonstrated psychomotor agitation, poor eye contact, circumstantial thought process, distractibility, repetitiveness, and mild lability of affect. Symptoms were ___ suggestive of major depressive disorder given duration, impairment in functioning, and multiple depressive symptoms (hopelessness, guilt, insomnia, anhedonia, irritable mood) in the setting of an impulsive act concerning for purposeful self-injury/death. Furthermore, deficits on cognitive and mental status exam were concerning for neurodevelopmental disorder (r/o intellectual disability), frontal lobe impairment/dysexecutive syndrome and/or attention deficit disorder. Patient completed up to ___ grade of school, has bouts of anger, and impulsivity, and has had difficulty holding jobs. He reports previous diagnosis of ADD and received treatment with concerta and clonidine in the past. Differential diagnosis includes unspecified anxiety disorder given racing thoughts interfering with sleep and concentration. We restarted Wellbutrin at a decreased dose of 100mg in the AM. To address issues of insomnia, frequent awakening and impulsivity, pt and inpt team, we agreed to initiate treatment with clonidine 0.05 po qHS. Dose was up-titrated to 0.1mg which patient tolerated well without report of lightheadedness. We initiated treatment with sertraline 25mg for anxiety. We provided daily motivational interviewing and psychopharmacological evaluation. We coordinated with outpatient providers to establish care after discharge. We initiated referral to neuropsychological testing at ___ to assist with clarification of diagnosis. We provided daily occupational therapy led groups which patient participated initially. Mr. ___ reported improvement in sleep quality and decrease in irritability with medication adjustments. He endorsed resolution of hopelessness and suicidal ideation prior to discharge. Mental status exam on discharge was notable for an okay mood congruent with a euthymic, full, reactive, appropriate affect. Thought process was circumstantial, repetitive, and distractible at times with fair insight and fair judgment. Future interventions include ___ of neuropsychological testing and medication initiation to assist with deficits in concentration, attention, and impulsivity. Patient will benefit from regular follow up with psychiatric medication prescribers for dose adjustment. SUBSTANCE USE DISORDERS: #Tobacco use disorder. ___ packs a day. The patient occasionally attended these groups that focused on teaching patients various coping skills. Mr. ___ was polite, appropriate, and interactive with peers and staff while on the unit. #COLLATERAL CONTACTS & FAMILY INVOLVEMENT Mr. ___ was not interested in collateral contact with his Grandmother who was a source of support in his life. He states that she recently got of the hospital and he does not want to worry her. #INTERVENTIONS - Medications: Zoloft 25, clonidine 0.1, Wellbutrin 100 - Psychotherapeutic Interventions: Individual, group, and milieu therapy. - Coordination of aftercare: PCP, ___, psychiatry ___. - Behavioral Interventions (e.g. encouraged DBT skills, ect): encouraged utilization of coping skills, cognitive behavioral therapy for anxiety. - Guardianships: none ON ___, Mr. ___ continued to ask to be discharged. He clearly denied S/H/I. He said that he would be able to return to the Emergency room if he feels unsafe. He denied A/V/H. He is in agreement to attend partial hospital program. When educated about elevated blood glucose, he still was asking to be discharged. He said he has been dealing with diabetes for many years. He would like to be followed in the ___. After repeating pt's labs (CMP, CBC, Blood glucose) and after being evaluated by ___ consult, pt was discharged home. Though patient could have benefitted from longer hospital treatment, there were no legal grounds to extend inpatient treatment. After weighing out risks and benefits, we agreed to proceed with discharge on ___. RISK ASSESSMENT & PROGNOSIS On presentation, the patient was evaluated and felt to be at an increased risk of harm to himself based upon acute worsening of depressive symptoms with feelings of hopelessness in setting of psychosocial stressors. His static factors noted at that time include history of abuse, chronic mental illness, chronic medical illness. The modifiable risk factors were also addressed at that time: lack of engagement with outpatient treatment, hopelessness, limited coping skills, no established outpatient treatment, insomnia, lack of purpose. We provided initiation of medications which assisted with insomnia and irritability and coordinated with outpatient providers for ongoing treatment and continued clarification of diagnosis (neuropsych. testing). Finally, the patient is being discharged with many protective risk factors; help-seeking nature, future-oriented viewpoint, sense of responsibility to family, children in the home, life satisfaction, lack of suicidal ideation, no history of substance use disorder, strong social supports. Overall, based on the totality of our assessment at this time, the patient is not at an acutely elevated risk of self-harm nor danger to others. sub_contents.sub_content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: PSYCHIATRY  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: I've been dealing with a lot of depression  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: none  History of Present Illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ M with a PMH of type-2 diabetes (on insulin) and a PPHx of ADD with no prior psychiatric hospitalizations or suicide attempts, who was BIBA this am after taking ___ Wellbutrin tablets. The pt states that he took about 12 Wellbutrin pills this morning after a fight with his girlfriend at 6am. Pt says he took the pills because he wanted to, sleep for a long time. He thought the pills might, make him sick, like to the stomach. After taking them he googled the potential effects, got scared about the risk of seizures, and called an ambulance. He says, I don't think I was trying to hurt myself, but I don't know. He describes taking the pills as a spur of the moment decision. He denies planning in advance or writing a note. He describes the fight with his girlfriend this am as, nothing crazy. He says they frequently argue about finances and she was upset because he had a friend over last night. . He was prescribed the Wellbutrin on ___ by a new PCP. He said his girlfriend has encouraged him to see a doctor for his increasing depression, so he made the appointment. He wanted to see a psychiatrist, and his new PCP gave him ___ number to call, which he did, but he has not received a call back yet. . Pt has been feeling increasing depression for months. He says, anything upsets me, and describes feeling more sad and easily angered than usual. He describes feelings of hopelessness, worthlessness, guilt, decreased sleep, fatigue, anhedonia, decreased motivation, decreased concentration and memory changes. He says he has trouble focusing. His girlfriend will ask him to do the laundry and he will start it, but he won't remember to finish it. He also endorses racing thoughts, distractibility, and some impulsivity (I sometimes sell all of my things, then buy them all back, then sell them again). He denies risky behavior or an increase in goal-directed behavior. Pt denies AH/VH/IOR/paranoia. . . . Collateral: Girlfriend, ___ (___) On phone this am states that pt has been very depressed lately. She believes that he has been hesitant to get help because he has a lot of pride in being able to handle things on his own. She has been encouraging him to get help and see a doctor, which he finally did last week. For her the events of the am were, a total shocker. She does not believe that he was trying to kill himself and is still not worried that he would do that. Nothing like this has happened before over the course of their ___ year relationship. She does have concerns about his depression, which she think has been steadily increasing since he left his last job. She says, his ADD effects his life everyday, it keeps him from being an adult. . -OMR note from Dr. ___ ___: Pt presented to PCP with increasing depression. He denied SI/HI at that appointment, but PHQ-9 was 22. Excerpt from note: - ADD -- patient reports being diagnosed and treated for ADD as a child, and is hoping to be tested and potentially treated again. He reports difficulty remember things at work and at home and is peturbed by this. - Depression -- reports that his depression has been bad recently given his struggles with caring for his sick son and being out of work. Denies any history of SI/HI but does say that he snaps at people and always wakes up on the wrong side of the bed. He thinks he has taken Zoloft in the past and is willing to try pharmacotherapy and talk therapy again. Pt was started on Wellbutrin, referred to SW and referred to cognitive ___ clinic for evaluation of ADD. . . . On interview pt confirmed hx documented in the initial psychiatric note. Mr. ___ stated that he has been noticing increase of depression x last year in the context of loosing job, having financial stress, having poor day structure. He reported feeling low mood, feeling sensitive (everything upsets me), feeling helpless/hopeless, worthless, fatigued. He reports problems with sleep (initial insomnia over one hour), decreased motivation to do anything during the day. He also reports increased anxiety, racing thoughts, increased problems with focus. He admits that he had more conflicts with gf. In fact, gf called police on him in the context of yelling match. Now, he has to appear in court. He took pills after an argument with his girlfriend on ___ in an attempt to sleep or possibly make himself sleep. He is equivocal about whether this was an attempt to hurt himself. He denies planning ahead and states that he regretted taking the medication after he read that he could have a seizure. He told his gf and called ___ immediately after learning the possible effects of the medication.  Past Medical History: PAST PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY: Diagnoses: ADD (dx at ___ Hospitalizations: denies Current treaters and treatment: denies; previously saw psychiatrist when he was a ___, but looked him up recently and could not find him again. Saw therapist in ___ ___ years ago for problems with anger. Medication and ECT trials: previously on Concerta, but has not taken for many years Self-injury: denies Harm to others: denies (see FORENSICS below) Access to weapons: denies PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Pt has a history of insulin-dependent Type 2 DM (w/proteinuria) MEDICATIONS, confirmed with : --------------- --------------- --------------- --------------- Active Medication list as of ___: Medications - Prescription BUPROPION HCL - bupropion HCl XL 150 mg 24 hr tablet, extended release. 1 tablet(s) by mouth once a day INSULIN DEGLUDEC [TRESIBA FLEXTOUCH U-100] - Tresiba FlexTouch U-100 insulin 100 unit/mL (3 mL) subcutaneous pen. 44 units SC qPM - (Prescribed by Other Provider) INSULIN LISPRO - insulin lispro (U-100) 100 unit/mL subcutaneous solution. ___ units SC before dinner - (Prescribed by Other Provider) LISINOPRIL - lisinopril 5 mg tablet. 1 tablet(s) by mouth once a day METFORMIN - metformin 500 mg tablet. 3 tablet(s) by mouth once a day - (Prescribed by Other Provider) --------------- --------------- --------------- --------------- ALLERGIES: SUBSTANCE ABUSE HISTORY: -Tobacco: smokes ___ PPD since he was a teenager, trying to cut back -ETOH: rarely drinks, last drink was ___ when he had 4 beers. Only drinks beer, never drinks hard etoh -Illicit drugs: Smokes marijuana ___ last use yesterday FORENSIC HISTORY: Arrests: Pt states that about 1-month ago his girlfriend called police during a domestic dispute. He describes the dispute as a yelling match and denies getting physical. He has a court date in late ___ for charges filed at that time. Convictions and jail terms: denies Current status (pending charges, probation, parole): see above   Social History: ___ Family History: FAMILY PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY: Per patient: Mother has history of possible bipolar disorder Father has a history of cocaine use disorder Denies family history of suicide attempts/SIB.  Physical Exam: Physical Exam Note (___) GENERAL - HEENT: - normocephalic, atraumatic - moist mucous membranes, oropharynx clear, supple neck - no scleral icterus - Cardiovascular: - regular rate and rhythm, S1/S2 heard, no murmurs/rubs/gallops - distal pulses ___ throughout - Pulmonary: - no increased work of breathing - lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally - no wheezes/rhonchi/rales - Abdominal: - non-distended, bowel sounds normoactive - no tenderness to palpation in all quadrants - no guarding, no rebound tenderness - Extremities: - warm and well-perfused - no edema of the limbs - Skin: - no rashes or lesions noted - nicotine patch present on left shoulder - Feet: - no ulcers, lesions, or open wounds appreciated NEUROLOGICAL - Cranial Nerves: - I: olfaction not tested - II: PERRL 3 to 2 mm, both directly and consentually; brisk bilaterally, VFF to confrontation - **III, IV, VI: EOMI without nystagmus however mild delay in tracking of L Eye noted, particularly when finger moved in towards nose and towards patients L side -**Mild Exotropia may be present L worse than R - V: facial sensation intact to light touch in all distributions - VII: no facial droop, facial musculature symmetric and ___ strength in upper and lower distributions, bilaterally - VIII: hearing intact to finger rub bilaterally - IX, X: palate elevates symmetrically - XI: ___ strength in trapezii and SCM bilaterally - XII: tongue protrudes in midline - Motor: - normal bulk and tone bilaterally - no abnormal movements, no tremor - strength ___ throughout - Sensory: - no deficits to fine touch throughout - DTRs: - 2 and symmetrical throughout - Coordination: - normal on finger to nose test, no intention tremor noted - Gait: - good initiation - narrow-based, normal stride and arm swing - able to walk in tandem without difficulty - Romberg absent MSE He is alert, friendly, cooperative. Help seeking. He appears much younger than his biological age. Casually dressed and adequately groomed. Maintained good eye contact. No abnormal/involuntary movements noticed. Speech spontaneous, fluent, in normal rate and prosody. TP linear. Answered questions appropriately. Denied sx's of psychosis. Reported episodes of tinnitus (ringing noise). Denied clear cut AH/VH/IoR. Described mood as depressed. His affect is friendly, somewhat upbeat, reactive. Child like. He denies S/H/I. He admits impulsive OD attempt in the context of conflict with gf. He denied any hx of physical violence. His insight is grossly intact: he recognizes problem with mood, impulsivity; he needs psychiatric assistance. His judgment is negatively affected by his mood, impulsivity. fields.入院情况: History of Present Illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ M with a PMH of type-2 diabetes (on insulin) and a PPHx of ADD with no prior psychiatric hospitalizations or suicide attempts, who was BIBA this am after taking ___ Wellbutrin tablets. The pt states that he took about 12 Wellbutrin pills this morning after a fight with his girlfriend at 6am. Pt says he took the pills because he wanted to, sleep for a long time. He thought the pills might, make him sick, like to the stomach. After taking them he googled the potential effects, got scared about the risk of seizures, and called an ambulance. He says, I don't think I was trying to hurt myself, but I don't know. He describes taking the pills as a spur of the moment decision. He denies planning in advance or writing a note. He describes the fight with his girlfriend this am as, nothing crazy. He says they frequently argue about finances and she was upset because he had a friend over last night. . He was prescribed the Wellbutrin on ___ by a new PCP. He said his girlfriend has encouraged him to see a doctor for his increasing depression, so he made the appointment. He wanted to see a psychiatrist, and his new PCP gave him ___ number to call, which he did, but he has not received a call back yet. . Pt has been feeling increasing depression for months. He says, anything upsets me, and describes feeling more sad and easily angered than usual. He describes feelings of hopelessness, worthlessness, guilt, decreased sleep, fatigue, anhedonia, decreased motivation, decreased concentration and memory changes. He says he has trouble focusing. His girlfriend will ask him to do the laundry and he will start it, but he won't remember to finish it. He also endorses racing thoughts, distractibility, and some impulsivity (I sometimes sell all of my things, then buy them all back, then sell them again). He denies risky behavior or an increase in goal-directed behavior. Pt denies AH/VH/IOR/paranoia. . . . Collateral: Girlfriend, ___ (___) On phone this am states that pt has been very depressed lately. She believes that he has been hesitant to get help because he has a lot of pride in being able to handle things on his own. She has been encouraging him to get help and see a doctor, which he finally did last week. For her the events of the am were, a total shocker. She does not believe that he was trying to kill himself and is still not worried that he would do that. Nothing like this has happened before over the course of their ___ year relationship. She does have concerns about his depression, which she think has been steadily increasing since he left his last job. She says, his ADD effects his life everyday, it keeps him from being an adult. . -OMR note from Dr. ___ ___: Pt presented to PCP with increasing depression. He denied SI/HI at that appointment, but PHQ-9 was 22. Excerpt from note: - ADD -- patient reports being diagnosed and treated for ADD as a child, and is hoping to be tested and potentially treated again. He reports difficulty remember things at work and at home and is peturbed by this. - Depression -- reports that his depression has been bad recently given his struggles with caring for his sick son and being out of work. Denies any history of SI/HI but does say that he snaps at people and always wakes up on the wrong side of the bed. He thinks he has taken Zoloft in the past and is willing to try pharmacotherapy and talk therapy again. Pt was started on Wellbutrin, referred to SW and referred to cognitive ___ clinic for evaluation of ADD. . . . On interview pt confirmed hx documented in the initial psychiatric note. Mr. ___ stated that he has been noticing increase of depression x last year in the context of loosing job, having financial stress, having poor day structure. He reported feeling low mood, feeling sensitive (everything upsets me), feeling helpless/hopeless, worthless, fatigued. He reports problems with sleep (initial insomnia over one hour), decreased motivation to do anything during the day. He also reports increased anxiety, racing thoughts, increased problems with focus. He admits that he had more conflicts with gf. In fact, gf called police on him in the context of yelling match. Now, he has to appear in court. He took pills after an argument with his girlfriend on ___ in an attempt to sleep or possibly make himself sleep. He is equivocal about whether this was an attempt to hurt himself. He denies planning ahead and states that he regretted taking the medication after he read that he could have a seizure. He told his gf and called ___ immediately after learning the possible effects of the medication.  Past Medical History: PAST PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY: Diagnoses: ADD (dx at ___ Hospitalizations: denies Current treaters and treatment: denies; previously saw psychiatrist when he was a ___, but looked him up recently and could not find him again. Saw therapist in ___ ___ years ago for problems with anger. Medication and ECT trials: previously on Concerta, but has not taken for many years Self-injury: denies Harm to others: denies (see FORENSICS below) Access to weapons: denies PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Pt has a history of insulin-dependent Type 2 DM (w/proteinuria) MEDICATIONS, confirmed with : --------------- --------------- --------------- --------------- Active Medication list as of ___: Medications - Prescription BUPROPION HCL - bupropion HCl XL 150 mg 24 hr tablet, extended release. 1 tablet(s) by mouth once a day INSULIN DEGLUDEC [TRESIBA FLEXTOUCH U-100] - Tresiba FlexTouch U-100 insulin 100 unit/mL (3 mL) subcutaneous pen. 44 units SC qPM - (Prescribed by Other Provider) INSULIN LISPRO - insulin lispro (U-100) 100 unit/mL subcutaneous solution. ___ units SC before dinner - (Prescribed by Other Provider) LISINOPRIL - lisinopril 5 mg tablet. 1 tablet(s) by mouth once a day METFORMIN - metformin 500 mg tablet. 3 tablet(s) by mouth once a day - (Prescribed by Other Provider) --------------- --------------- --------------- --------------- ALLERGIES: SUBSTANCE ABUSE HISTORY: -Tobacco: smokes ___ PPD since he was a teenager, trying to cut back -ETOH: rarely drinks, last drink was ___ when he had 4 beers. Only drinks beer, never drinks hard etoh -Illicit drugs: Smokes marijuana ___ last use yesterday FORENSIC HISTORY: Arrests: Pt states that about 1-month ago his girlfriend called police during a domestic dispute. He describes the dispute as a yelling match and denies getting physical. He has a court date in late ___ for charges filed at that time. Convictions and jail terms: denies Current status (pending charges, probation, parole): see above   Social History: ___ Family History: FAMILY PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY: Per patient: Mother has history of possible bipolar disorder Father has a history of cocaine use disorder Denies family history of suicide attempts/SIB.  Physical Exam: Physical Exam Note (___) GENERAL - HEENT: - normocephalic, atraumatic - moist mucous membranes, oropharynx clear, supple neck - no scleral icterus - Cardiovascular: - regular rate and rhythm, S1/S2 heard, no murmurs/rubs/gallops - distal pulses ___ throughout - Pulmonary: - no increased work of breathing - lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally - no wheezes/rhonchi/rales - Abdominal: - non-distended, bowel sounds normoactive - no tenderness to palpation in all quadrants - no guarding, no rebound tenderness - Extremities: - warm and well-perfused - no edema of the limbs - Skin: - no rashes or lesions noted - nicotine patch present on left shoulder - Feet: - no ulcers, lesions, or open wounds appreciated NEUROLOGICAL - Cranial Nerves: - I: olfaction not tested - II: PERRL 3 to 2 mm, both directly and consentually; brisk bilaterally, VFF to confrontation - **III, IV, VI: EOMI without nystagmus however mild delay in tracking of L Eye noted, particularly when finger moved in towards nose and towards patients L side -**Mild Exotropia may be present L worse than R - V: facial sensation intact to light touch in all distributions - VII: no facial droop, facial musculature symmetric and ___ strength in upper and lower distributions, bilaterally - VIII: hearing intact to finger rub bilaterally - IX, X: palate elevates symmetrically - XI: ___ strength in trapezii and SCM bilaterally - XII: tongue protrudes in midline - Motor: - normal bulk and tone bilaterally - no abnormal movements, no tremor - strength ___ throughout - Sensory: - no deficits to fine touch throughout - DTRs: - 2 and symmetrical throughout - Coordination: - normal on finger to nose test, no intention tremor noted - Gait: - good initiation - narrow-based, normal stride and arm swing - able to walk in tandem without difficulty - Romberg absent MSE He is alert, friendly, cooperative. Help seeking. He appears much younger than his biological age. Casually dressed and adequately groomed. Maintained good eye contact. No abnormal/involuntary movements noticed. Speech spontaneous, fluent, in normal rate and prosody. TP linear. Answered questions appropriately. Denied sx's of psychosis. Reported episodes of tinnitus (ringing noise). Denied clear cut AH/VH/IoR. Described mood as depressed. His affect is friendly, somewhat upbeat, reactive. Child like. He denies S/H/I. He admits impulsive OD attempt in the context of conflict with gf. He denied any hx of physical violence. His insight is grossly intact: he recognizes problem with mood, impulsivity; he needs psychiatric assistance. His judgment is negatively affected by his mood, impulsivity. On formal exam: A, Ox ___, ___, been here since yesterday, person, situation. It took him three attempts to register ___, recalled ___ after 5 min delay. Recognized remaining two items from the list. Digit span: 7df w second attempt; 3db. He was unable to answer question if the flag waves towards the Southeast, where is the wind coming from?. He responded:I don't know.
