content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: s/p fall  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: N/A  History of Present Illness: ___ yo man with hx of severe dementia (non-verbal, receives help at home with home health aid), multiple recent falls at home, BPH, and HTN who presents s/p fall. Per wife, the home health aid was attempting to dress the patient today, and the patient became agitated and fell. She says he fell against the bath tub with reported headstrike but no LOC. He has notable bruising and swelling over the back of his head, left shoulder, and left forearm. The wife did not witness the fall, but is recounting history from home health aide. The aide also stated that he was not complaining of chest pain, palpitations, or SOB prior to the fall. CT head in the ED was negative for intra-cranial abnormality, but notable for large subgleal hematoma. CT c-spine also negative. EKG unchanged from prior, troponin <0.01. CXR and UA unremarkable, but urine and blood cultures are pending. Per report, the patient walks well without any assistance (no weakness/focal deficits) but has home health aid to help with ADLs at home. Given recent falls, the family is concerned that home nursing staff is not watching the patient. They believe nursing staff are letting him go to the bathroom/walk without assistance. Case management was consulted in the ED. Given concern for safety at home, plan to admit to medicine for case management eval in AM and ___ set up. In the ED, initial VS were: T: 96.6, HR: 76, BP: 197/108, RR: 18, 97% RA Exam notable for: Patient does not respond to questions, intermittently tries to get up from the bed. Heart- regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs Lungs - clear to auscultation Abdomen - nontender, patient does not react to palpation Neuro - moving all extremities spontaneously, pupils of normal size but difficult to examine; no apparent focal weakness. Per family the patient is at his baseline. Skin - large swelling on the upper/posterior head with small skin abrasions; left arm abrasion; left scapular abrasions Extremities- no cyanosis or edema ECG: Sinus rhythm, HR 91bpm, 1st degree AV delay, no ST changes  Labs showed: - Troponin <0.01 - WBC 16.3, HgB 15.7, Hct 46.4, Plt 228 - Chemistry panel, LFTs within normal limits Imaging showed: - CT c-spine: No acute fracture or traumatic subluxation. - CT head: 1. No acute intracranial abnormality or fracture. 2. Bilateral 4 mm low-density subdural fluid collections, potentially chronic subdural hematomas or hygromas. 3. Large subgaleal hematoma near the skull vertex. - CXR: No acute cardiopulmonary process.  Consults: Neurosurgery: Collections are chronic hygromas. No need for repeat imaging. No need for neurosurgical follow up. Case management: Unsafe d/c plan, no homecare assistance available. Plan for patient to remain in hospital to allow for set up of ___ ___ to provide respite for spouse and care for the patient. Voicemail left for ___, Case Manager from ___ (coordinates pt's ___ ___. ___ CM will follow up. Physical therapy: Patient at baseline. Consult d/ced. Patient received: ___ 00:56 PO OLANZapine (Disintegrating Tablet) 5 mg ___ 01:00 PO Tamsulosin ___ 02:55 IM Haloperidol 5 mg ___ 21:03 PO Tamsulosin .4 mg ___ 21:03 PO OLANZapine (Disintegrating Tablet) 5 mg ___ 21:03 PO Acetaminophen 650 mg Transfer VS were: T: 98.0, BP: 160/76, BP: 102, RR: 18, 95% RA  On arrival to the floor, the patient is not able to communicate symptoms. He says alright intermittently, but does not answer questions or follow commands. His wife is at bedside, and believes he is having head pain/headache. He is also restless and attempting to get out of bed. Otherwise, no complaints per wife.  Past Medical History: - Tarsal toe injury - HTN - Gout - Vitamin D deficiency - Constipation - Allergic rhinitis/conjunctivitis that result in Mr. ___ rubbing his eyes rigorously - BPH that has been well controlled with tamsulosin 0.5 mg once per day -Fungal toenail infection - Frontotemporal dementia significant behavioral issues - Palliative care consulted  Social History: ___ Family History: Non-contributory as it relates to presentation  Physical Exam: ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM: ======================== VS: ___ 2300 Temp: 98.0 Axillary BP: 160/76 HR: 102 RR: 18 O2 sat: 95% O2 delivery: RA GENERAL: NAD, does not follow commands or answer questions at baseline HEENT: AT/NC, anicteric sclera, MMM NECK: supple, no LAD CV: RRR, S1/S2, no murmurs, gallops, or rubs PULM: CTAB, no wheezes, rales, rhonchi, breathing comfortably without use of accessory muscles GI: abdomen soft, nondistended, nontender in all quadrants, no rebound/guarding, no hepatosplenomegaly EXTREMITIES: no cyanosis, clubbing, or edema PULSES: 2+ radial pulses bilaterally NEURO: Moving all 4 extremities with purpose, face symmetric DERM: warm and well perfused, no excoriations or lesions, no rashes DISCHARGE PHYSICAL EXAM: ======================== GENERAL: Alert, in NAD, does not follow commands or answer questions (at baseline) HEENT: (+) swelling of the L upper head/occiput with skin abrasions, AT/NC, anicteric sclera, MMM NECK: Supple, no LAD CV: RRR, S1/S2, no murmurs, gallops, or rubs PULM: CTAB at the anterior lung fields (could not assess back ___ cooperation), no wheezes, rales, rhonchi, breathing comfortably without use of accessory muscles GI: Abdomen soft, nondistended, nontender in all quadrants, no hepatosplenomegaly EXTREMITIES: No cyanosis, clubbing, or edema DERM: Warm, no rashes, (+) abrasion on L scapula with scabbing  Pertinent Results: ADMISSION LABS: =============== ___ 05:25PM BLOOD WBC-12.4* RBC-5.28 Hgb-15.2 Hct-45.7 MCV-87 MCH-28.8 MCHC-33.3 RDW-12.2 RDWSD-38.7 Plt ___ ___ 04:36PM BLOOD ___ PTT-33.2 ___ ___ 05:25PM BLOOD Glucose-137* UreaN-10 Creat-1.0 Na-140 K-4.3 Cl-101 HCO3-24 AnGap-15 ___ 05:25PM BLOOD ALT-16 AST-36 AlkPhos-86 TotBili-0.3 ___ 08:37PM BLOOD cTropnT-<0.01 ___ 05:25PM BLOOD Albumin-3.8 Calcium-9.2 Phos-3.1 Mg-1.7 DISCHARGE LABS: =============== ___ 08:22AM BLOOD WBC-9.3 RBC-5.07 Hgb-15.1 Hct-45.4 MCV-90 MCH-29.8 MCHC-33.3 RDW-12.9 RDWSD-41.7 Plt ___ ___ 08:22AM BLOOD Plt ___ ___ 08:22AM BLOOD Glucose-129* UreaN-14 Creat-1.3* Na-145 K-4.5 Cl-105 HCO3-28 AnGap-12 ___ 08:22AM BLOOD Calcium-9.5 Phos-3.8 Mg-2.0 STUDIES: ======== ___ HCT IMPRESSION: 1. No acute intracranial abnormality or fracture. Global volume loss, particularly in the temporal lobes. 2. Bilateral 4 mm low-density subdural fluid collections, potentially chronic subdural hematomas or hygromas. 3. Large subgaleal hematoma near the skull vertex. ___ CT C-spine IMPRESSION: No acute fracture or traumatic subluxation.  Brief Hospital Course: ___ with history of severe frontotemporal dementia (essentially non-verbal), multiple recent falls at home, BPH, and HTN who presents s/p mechanical fall, with concerns for safety at home. ACUTE ISSUES: ============= #Falls, home safety Patient presented to the ED after a fall, likely mechanical in nature. Per the patient's wife, their home health aid was attempting to dress the patient, and he fell against the bath tub. He hit his head but did not lose consciousness. The aide reported the patient did not have chest pain or SOB prior to the fall, although the patient appears essentially non-verbal at baseline. Reassuringly, in the ED, his EKG did not show ischemic changes, and cardiac biomarkers were negative. HCT showed bilateral chronic subdural hematomas or hygromas and a large subgaleal hematoma without acute findings; Neurosurgery was consulted who felt these findings were most c/w hygromas and recommended no acute intervention and no further NSGY needed. CT C-spine showed no acute fracture. The patient reportedly walks well without assistance but needs help with ADL's at home. However, in light of recurrent falls over the past year, evaluation for additional home services was warranted. Patient was admitted and seen by case management, and ultimately discharged home with resumption of home care. He was seen in the ED by physical therapy, who noted that he was at baseline with respect to his gait and activity. CM did start the discussion surrounding placement in a memory care setting. Wife was receptive to the discussion and stated she knows Mr. ___ will need to eventually transition to this or a nursing home but she was not ready. She was placed in contact with ___ Senior long term CM. #Leukocytosis Patient presented with a WBC 12.4, which normalized by the next morning. His leukocytosis was thought to be reactive in the setting of this recent fall. He otherwise had no fevers or localizing symptoms, and CXR and UA were unremarkable on admission. Blood and urine cultures were still pending at discharge but no growth to date. #HTN, uncontrolled Patient's blood pressures were noted to be in the 150-160's systolic. He is not on any home anti-hypertensives. In the setting of recent falls, intervention was deferred. #Acute kidney injury Cr rose on the morning of hospital day 1 to 1.3 from 1.0 on presentation to the ED. Unclear baseline. Suspect pre-renal ___ ___ poor PO intake while in the ED. Improved with IVFs. CHRONIC ISSUES: =============== #Frontotemporal dementia Patient is essentially non-verbal and does not follow commands at baseline. He was continued on home quietiapine 6.25mg each night as needed for agitation. #Osteoarthritis Continued Tylenol PRN #Constipation Continued home docusate #Gout Continued home colchicine PRN #Blepharitis/allergic conjunctivitis Continued home ketotifen fumarate TRANSITIONAL ISSUES: ==================== - Please ensure patient has sufficient home health care to account for his ADL needs and to help prevent falls - ___ conversation with ___ Senior Long Term CM re: eventual placement in a more monitored setting (___ ___ vs ___, etc) - Please consider addressing code status with the patient and his wife depending on what the patient had expressed prior to progression of his dementia; he is currently full code as per discussion with pts wife MEDICATION CHANGES: =================== None Contact: ___ (wife), ___ (cell) Full code  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list may be inaccurate and requires futher investigation. 1. Acetaminophen 325 mg PO Q6H:PRN Pain - Mild 2. ketotifen fumarate 0.025 % (0.035 %) ophthalmic (eye) BID:PRN 3. Colchicine 0.6 mg PO DAILY:PRN gout flare 4. QUEtiapine Fumarate 6.25 mg PO QHS:PRN agitation 5. Docusate Sodium 200 mg PO DAILY 6. Multivitamins W/minerals 1 TAB PO DAILY 7. Ibuprofen Suspension 200-400 mg PO Q8H:PRN Pain - Mild 8. Tamsulosin 0.4 mg PO DAILY 9. Vitamin D ___ UNIT PO QMONTHLY 10. Ascorbic Acid ___ mg PO BID  Discharge Medications: 1. Acetaminophen 325 mg PO Q6H:PRN Pain - Mild 2. Ascorbic Acid ___ mg PO BID 3. Colchicine 0.6 mg PO DAILY:PRN gout flare 4. Docusate Sodium 200 mg PO DAILY 5. Ibuprofen Suspension 200-400 mg PO Q8H:PRN Pain - Mild 6. ketotifen fumarate 0.025 % (0.035 %) ophthalmic (eye) BID:PRN 7. Multivitamins W/minerals 1 TAB PO DAILY 8. QUEtiapine Fumarate 6.25 mg PO QHS:PRN agitation 9. Tamsulosin 0.4 mg PO DAILY 10. Vitamin D ___ UNIT PO QMONTHLY  Discharge Disposition: Home With Service  Facility: ___  Discharge Diagnosis: PRIMARY DIAGNOSIS ================= Mechanical fall SECONDARY DIAGNOSES =================== Frontotemporal dementia Hygroma Acute kidney injury Uncontrolled HTH Leukocytosis  Discharge Condition: Unable to assess mental status Level of Consciousness: Alert but not responsive to questions Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: Dear Mr. ___, It was a pleasure to care for you at the ___ ___. Why did you come to the hospital? - You had a fall at home and hit your head and shoulder What did you receive in the hospital? - We performed CT scans of your head and neck, which did not show any bleeding, broken bones, or other trauma. - We will coordinate with your case manager to arrange for an aide at home. What should you do once you leave the hospital? - Please continue to go to all of your outpatient appointments. - Please continue to take your medications as prescribed. We wish you the best! Your ___ Care Team  Followup Instructions: ___

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: PSYCHIATRY  Allergies: Haldol / Zyprexa  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: I am having PTSD  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None  History of Present Illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ year old male with a history of bipolar disorder, self-reported history of PTSD, multiple psychiatric hospitalizations who brought himself into the ___ ED for symptoms of PTSD and inability to care for himself after being thrown out of the ___ for loitering.  Pt reports that he had worked as an ___ on ___ at ___ and was triggered by the negative emotions expressed by his co-workers in the aftermath of the election. Additionally, he says that he has been experiencing several 'coincidences' recently including meeting a small child who was selling paintings in ___ and who he thought was abandoned. This sparked memories of his own childhood issues with feeling abandoned and neglected by his mother and the bad things his stepfather did. He had initially presented to the ___ ED, had done intake, and decided to go to the hospital chapel. At that point he was accosted by 10 hospital security guards who twisted his arm, took photos of his hospital ID and face, and threw him out on to the street after yelling go back to your country. He ran over to the ___ ED and feels less depressed here since everyone has been nice. He feels connected to the BI since he knows the daughter of a surgeon here and his cousin was born here.  In early ___, he has feeling quite depressed for which his psychiatrist Dr. ___ (___) put him on a trial of a stimulant. This medication helped with the depression but put him into a manic phase for 4 weeks during which he felt high. He was treated at ___ for PTSD and mania and placed him on Lithium which he says did not help much. He was there for 4 days after which he was supposed to go to a partial hospital program but did not end up going. He says that he has been inconsistent with taking his Lithium (per his memory, he was on 1000-1200 mg of Lithium and his last ___ level was 0.8). He says that he does not need any sleep or anxiety medications since he has been sleeping well and feels rested upon waking up. He does note decreased interest in activities recently.  Per patient, denies SI, HI, AVH, nightmares, paranoia.  Past Medical History: ___  Social History: -Born in ___ and raised in ___ (___ in ___. Moved to ___ with his family in ___. Reports being ___ decent, although previous notes report being of ___ background. -Reports history of trauma by step-father, who passes away several months ago in ___ -Grew up with mom, stepfather, and sister, ___. -Completed HS and some college, reports being kick out of ___ after his first psychotic break. He did take classes at ___ in the past. Worked in the past at ___ for ___, has not worked in years. Receives SSI and SSDI, currently lives in an apt in ___ alone. Past psychiatric history: - Diagnoses: Bipolar I and reported PTSD - Prior Hospitalizations: 21 total. First at age ___ when he was diagnosed w/ bipolar. Most recently at ___ ___. Has been at ___, ___, ___, ___, CCS ___ at ___, ___, ___. - History of assaultive behaviors: Denies - History of suicide attempts or self-injurious behavior: Denies - Prior med trials: Klonopin, lamictal, sertraline, trazodone, Wellbutrin (caused mania), Haldol (caused akasthisia). Has been considered for ECT in the past but refused. - Therapist: ___ - Psychiatrist: ___  Family History: -Sister: post-partum depression, has previously tried anti-depressants -Mom: psychotic, recently treated for shingles at ___, says that his mother tends to spread rumors about his being psychotic since she wants to get him admitted under a ___. He says this is tied to his stepfather's inheritance as his mom and sister do not want him to receive anything.  Physical Exam: PHYSICAL EXAMINATION: VS:T: 98.0 BP: 121/76 HR: 75 RR: 16 Sat 98% * General: NAD. Well-nourished, well-developed. Appears stated age. * HEENT: Normocephalic. PERRL, EOMI. * Neck: Supple, trachea midline. * Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles or wheezes. * CV: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs/rubs/gallops. * Abdomen: Soft, nontender, nondistended * Extremities: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. * Skin: Warm and dry. No readily apparent rashes, scars, or lesions. Neurological: *Cranial Nerves: I: Not tested. II: Pupils equally round and reactive to light bilaterally. Visual fields are full to confrontation. III, IV, VI: Extraocular movements intact bilaterally without nystagmus. V, VII: Facial strength and sensation intact and symmetric. VIII: Hearing intact to voice. IX, X: Palatal elevation symmetrical. XI: Sternocleidomastoid and trapezius normal bilaterally. XII: Tongue midline without fasciculations. *Motor- Normal bulk and tone bilaterally. No abnormal movements, no tremor. Strength full power ___ throughout. No gross focal motor or sensory deficits, normal gait. *Coordination: No ataxia, gross abnormalities. *Sensation: Intact to light touch *Gait: Steady. No truncal ataxia. Normal stance and posture. NEUROPSYCHIATRIC EXAMINATION: * Appearance: Mildly disheveled, appears older than stated age, ___ was open during the interview with no apparent regard, thick glasses * Behavior: Appropriate with good eye contact, engaged, cooperative * Orientation: Oriented to self, location, and date. * Mood: more depressed than manic * Affect: euthymic, full range, congruent to stated mood. * Thought Process: Logical and goal directed. No loosening of associations. No apparent disorganization. * Thought Content: +Paranoia, questionably psychotic. Denies current suicidal thoughts, plans, and actions. Denies HI. * Judgment: Poor. * Insight: Poor. * Speech: Spontaneous. Normal conversational volume, tone, rate, and prosody. COGNITION: Wakefulness/alertness: alert, awake *Attention (digit span, MOYB): maintains appropriate attention throughout exam *Orientation: person, place, date, situation *Memory: recalls aspects of past few days / past treatments *Fund of knowledge: appropriate for age and education level *Speech: normal rate, rhythm, prosody *Language: fluent ___, no paraphasic errors Discharge Examination: VS: T 98.7, BP 133/78, HR 80, RR 16, SaO2 98% on Room Air Mental Status Exam: Appearance: Hispanic male, well groomed, dressed in casual clothing Behavior: calm, cooperative, pleasant; no psychomotor agitation Mood: good Affect: euthymic, full range, congruent, appropriate Thought Process: linear, logical, goal oriented Perceptions: no evidence of Auditory/Visual hallucinations Suicidality/Homicidality: no evidence of SI/HI Insight/Judgment: improved  Pertinent Results: ___ 04:10AM URINE bnzodzpn-NEG barbitrt-NEG opiates-NEG cocaine-NEG amphetmn-NEG oxycodn-NEG mthdone-NEG ___ 04:10AM URINE COLOR-Straw APPEAR-Clear SP ___ ___ 04:10AM URINE BLOOD-NEG NITRITE-NEG PROTEIN-NEG GLUCOSE-NEG KETONE-NEG BILIRUBIN-NEG UROBILNGN-NEG PH-8.0 LEUK-NEG ___ 02:40AM GLUCOSE-108* UREA N-6 CREAT-0.6 SODIUM-139 POTASSIUM-3.6 CHLORIDE-101 TOTAL CO2-27 ANION GAP-15 ___ 02:40AM TSH-0.90 ___ 02:40AM FREE T4-1.1 ___ 02:40AM LITHIUM-1.2 ___ 02:40AM ASA-NEG ETHANOL-NEG ACETMNPHN-NEG bnzodzpn-NEG barbitrt-NEG tricyclic-NEG ___ 02:40AM WBC-8.4 RBC-4.45* HGB-12.4* HCT-39.6* MCV-89 MCH-27.9 MCHC-31.3* RDW-13.9 RDWSD-45.1 ___ 02:40AM NEUTS-56.7 ___ MONOS-6.8 EOS-4.6 BASOS-0.2 IM ___ AbsNeut-4.79 AbsLymp-2.64 AbsMono-0.57 AbsEos-0.39 AbsBaso-0.02 ___ 02:40AM PLT COUNT-317 ___: ___ 0.6  Brief Hospital Course: This is a ___ year old single, domiciled, unemployed man, previously diagnosed with Type I bipolar disorder with psychotic features, no prior suicide attempts, s/p numerous psychiatric admissions, recently discharged from ___, self reported history of PTSD, who self presented to ___ ER for symptoms of PTSD, concern for ability to care for himself after being thrown out of the ___ for loitering. History and interview concerning for decompensated psychosis with erratic, paranoid behavior in the setting of medication non compliance and poor compliance with follow up outpatient team. Upon admission, patient reported he is here for depression and PTSD with relatively euthymic affect, paranoid thought content regarding legal authorities. Admitted to Deaconess 4 due to concerns for worsening psychosis, inability to care for self or protect self in the community with recent indecent exposure at the ___ per OMR. #. Legal/Safety: Patient was admitted on a ___, upon admission, signed a CV, which was accepted. Patient reported he did not want to be on the unit any longer and so signed a 3 day notice dated ___. Given his good behavioral control, appropriate ADL's and interactions, medication compliance, and consistent denial of SI/HI, AVH, I did not believe he met criteria to file a 7&8b. Of note, he maintained good behavioral control throughout his admission on 15 minute checks and did not require physical or chemical restraints. #. Bipolar disorder: Type I, with psychotic features - Patient was compliant in attending groups, where he participated appropriately. - With the patient's permission, we spoke with his outpatient psychiatrist, Dr. ___ agreed with inpatient level of care and initiation of an antipsychotic medication at this time. - After discussion of the risks and benefits, we started Latuda 20 mg po qd, which was titrated to 40 mg po qd. Patient tolerated this well with no complaints of side effects and with improvement in psychotic symptoms with lessening of his persecutory delusions, more organized thought process and with improved insight and judgment. Recommendation is to continue titration of Latuda as clinically indicated and as tolerated. - Given good response the past, we continued his lithium at 900 mg po qhs and 600 mg po qam with no complaints of side effects. Lithium level on ___ was slightly elevated on ___ 1.2 but recheck of Lithium level was 0.6 on ___. - Patient was compliant with medications and participatory in his care, consistently denying suicidal ideation or thoughts of self harm during his admission. By time of discharge, he stated he would like to attend a partial program, which was reasonable, and a referral was made (see discharge worksheet). He refused to allow us to obtain collateral from his family members.  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Lithium Carbonate 600 mg PO DAILY 2. Lithium Carbonate 900 mg PO QHS  Discharge Medications: 1. lurasidone 40 mg oral QHS 2. Lithium Carbonate 600 mg PO DAILY 3. Chief Complaint: I am having PTSD  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: None  History of Present Illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ year old male with a history of bipolar disorder, self-reported history of PTSD, multiple psychiatric hospitalizations who brought himself into the ___ ED for symptoms of PTSD and inability to care for himself after being thrown out of the ___ for loitering.  Pt reports that he had worked as an ___ on ___ at ___ and was triggered by the negative emotions expressed by his co-workers in the aftermath of the election. Additionally, he says that he has been experiencing several 'coincidences' recently including meeting a small child who was selling paintings in ___ and who he thought was abandoned. This sparked memories of his own childhood issues with feeling abandoned and neglected by his mother and the bad things his stepfather did. He had initially presented to the ___ ED, had done intake, and decided to go to the hospital chapel. At that point he was accosted by 10 hospital security guards who twisted his arm, took photos of his hospital ID and face, and threw him out on to the street after yelling go back to your country. He ran over to the ___ ED and feels less depressed here since everyone has been nice. He feels connected to the BI since he knows the daughter of a surgeon here and his cousin was born here.  In early ___, he has feeling quite depressed for which his psychiatrist Dr. ___ (___) put him on a trial of a stimulant. This medication helped with the depression but put him into a manic phase for 4 weeks during which he felt high. He was treated at ___ for PTSD and mania and placed him on Lithium which he says did not help much. He was there for 4 days after which he was supposed to go to a partial hospital program but did not end up going. He says that he has been inconsistent with taking his Lithium (per his memory, he was on 1000-1200 mg of Lithium and his last ___ level was 0.8). He says that he does not need any sleep or anxiety medications since he has been sleeping well and feels rested upon waking up. He does note decreased interest in activities recently. He did take classes at ___ in the past. Worked in the past at ___ for ___, has not worked in years. Receives SSI and SSDI, currently lives in an apt in ___ alone. Has been considered for ECT in the past but refused. - Therapist: ___ - Psychiatrist: ___  Family History: -Sister: post-partum depression, has previously tried anti-depressants -Mom: psychotic, recently treated for shingles at ___, says that his mother tends to spread rumors about his being psychotic since she wants to get him admitted under a ___. History and interview concerning for decompensated psychosis with erratic, paranoid behavior in the setting of medication non compliance and poor compliance with follow up outpatient team. Upon admission, patient reported he is here for depression and PTSD with relatively euthymic affect, paranoid thought content regarding legal authorities. Admitted to Deaconess 4 due to concerns for worsening psychosis, inability to care for self or protect self in the community with recent indecent exposure at the ___ per OMR. #. Legal/Safety: Patient was admitted on a ___, upon admission, signed a CV, which was accepted. Patient reported he did not want to be on the unit any longer and so signed a 3 day notice dated ___. Given his good behavioral control, appropriate ADL's and interactions, medication compliance, and consistent denial of SI/HI, AVH, I did not believe he met criteria to file a 7&8b. Of note, he maintained good behavioral control throughout his admission on 15 minute checks and did not require physical or chemical restraints. #. Bipolar disorder: Type I, with psychotic features - Patient was compliant in attending groups, where he participated appropriately. - With the patient's permission, we spoke with his outpatient psychiatrist, Dr. ___ agreed with inpatient level of care and initiation of an antipsychotic medication at this time. - After discussion of the risks and benefits, we started Latuda 20 mg po qd, which was titrated to 40 mg po qd. Patient tolerated this well with no complaints of side effects and with improvement in psychotic symptoms with lessening of his persecutory delusions, more organized thought process and with improved insight and judgment. Recommendation is to continue titration of Latuda as clinically indicated and as tolerated. - Given good response the past, we continued his lithium at 900 mg po qhs and 600 mg po qam with no complaints of side effects. Lithium level on ___ was slightly elevated on ___ 1.2 but recheck of Lithium level was 0.6 on ___. - Patient was compliant with medications and participatory in his care, consistently denying suicidal ideation or thoughts of self harm during his admission. By time of discharge, he stated he would like to attend a partial program, which was reasonable, and a referral was made (see discharge worksheet). He refused to allow us to obtain collateral from his family members.  Medications on Admission: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Lithium Carbonate 600 mg PO DAILY 2. content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics) / NSAIDS / strawberries  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: post-ERCP; leukocytosis  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: ___ ERCP with sphincterotomy, biliary stent removal and sphincteroplasty  History of Present Illness: ___ yo M, hx of rectal CA s/p colectomy with ostomy, severe anxiety, s/p recent open CCY for choledocholithiasis, c/b wound infection, UTI and lower extremity edema, who presents for elective ERCP for stent pull. Underwent ERCP with successful sphincterotomy, stent pull and sphincteroplasty. . History provided by patient, as well as his family members, wife and daughter (HCP) at the bedside in the post-procedure area in the Advanced Endoscopy unit. . Pt reports stopping Coumadin 5 days prior to procedure. He reports that he has never previously been on Lovenox or heparin bridge for procedures. He currently complains of some mild post-procedure nausea, which has responded to IV Zofran, but denies any fevers, chills, abd pain, chest pain or SOB. . He reports that since his discharge on ___, his ___ edema has improved significantly, although 1 dose of Lasix was held on ___ due to weight loss of > 3 lbs (down to 179lbs on ___. He reports that his wound vac has not been bothering him. Of note, routine AM labs today note WBC# of 20K, and he does note some chills at home and his wife reports more loose BM's in his ostomy bag. He also reports some occ vague abdominal pain / pressure around his ostomy site, although he currently has none. He denies N/V, fever, melena / hematochezia in the ostomy. He denies dysuria, cough, SOB. He does note urinary frequency and nocturia but this has been a chronic problem and he was just seen in Urology Clinc and his urinary symptoms were felt to be most likely residual symptoms from recent UTI. Otherwise he denies any other symptoms except his baseline anxiety. He wishes to be discharged to home as soon as safely possible. . Recent hospital admissions / evaluations: 1. Admitted ___ - ___ for SOB, presumptive dx of ILI vs atypical PNA, discharged to home on course of PO antibiotics. . 2. Admitted ___ for abd pain, F/C, found to have MDR-E. coli sepsis and bacteremia from cholangitis, underwent ERCP with biliary stent and DC'ed to home on 2 week course of carbapenem IV via PICC. . 3. Seen and discharged from ED on ___ for melanotic stools. Had stable vital signs and Hct, felt to be some mild post-ERCP bleeding. . 4. Admitted ___ admitted to Hepatobiliary Surgery for elective open CCY. He received a 5-day course of ciprofloxacin for empiric therapy for UTI, but his urine culture ultimately returned No Growth. . 5. Seen and discharged from ED on ___ for wound infection and possible UTI. Was sent home on course of Keflex for wound infection and nitrofurantoin for UTI. Ultimately both wound and urine cx grew similar strains of E. coli. . 6. Admitted ___ - ___ to Surgery for worsening ___ edema and wound evaluation. His Keflex was stopped and he had a wound vac placed. For his ___ edema, ultrasound was negative for DVT. TTE was concerning for diastolic HF, so he was started on diuresis with good effect. He was ultimately discharged on standing PO Lasix 20mg daily. Repeat urine culture from ___ was negative for infection, but he was continued on his previously determined course of nitrofurantoin. . . Review of Systems: (+) see above HPI (-) fever, night sweats, headache, vision changes, rhinorrhea, congestion, sore throat, cough, shortness of breath, chest pain, vomiting, constipation, BRBPR, melena, hematochezia, dysuria, hematuria.  Past Medical History: -s/p open CCY ___ -Rectal cancer s/p resection & end colostomy, chemotherapy ___ -Asbestosis -Atrial fibrillation -Hyperlipidemia -TIA -Macular degeneration, legally blind -remote duodenal ulcer, evaluated at ___, resolved without tx -Osteoarthritis -Anxiety -___ esophagus (___) -Meniere's disease -Pseudogout -Bilateral inguinal hernia  Social History: ___ Family History: Denies any FH of liver, pancreas or GB disease. FH notable for 1 son with hx of PCKD, s/p kidney transplant.  Physical Exam: Admission Physical Exam: Vitals: T 98, BP 129/52, HR 62, RR 27, O2Sat 94% on 2L General: NAD, lying in stretcher HEENT: anicteric, dry MM CV: irreg irreg, no appreciable murmur Lungs: CTAB/L Abdomen: soft, NT, ND, +BS, ostomy with semi-formed stool, and incision with wound vac in place, some mild erythema around incision, but no frank drainage, no induration/fluctuance. Ext: trace b/l ___ edema Neuro: AAOx3, fluent speech, no facial droop Skin: dry, warm . Discharge Day Exam: VS: AVSS, notably afebrile, adequate O2 sat on RA Gen: sitting in chair eating lunch, looks well, NAD HEENT: anicteric, MMM CV: irreg, irreg, no murmur Lungs: CTAB/L Abd: soft, NT, ND, + ostomy with semi-formed stool. Wound vac and steri-strips in place Ext: trace B/L ___. Right knee without effusion., +FROM, no warmth, no erythema Neuro: AAOx3, fluent speech .  Pertinent Results: . Imaging: ======= ___ ERCP Impression: The previously placed plastic biliary stent was found in good position at the major papilla. A sphincterotomy was performed in the 12 o'clock position over the previously placed biliary stent using a needle-knife. The previously placed plastic biliary was then removed using a snare. Successful biliary duct cannulation using a sphincterotome. A single 12 mm oval stone that was causing partial obstruction was seen at the lower third of the common bile duct. There was mild diffuse post-obstructive dilation of the common bile duct, common hepatic duct, and right and left main hepatic ducts with the CBD measuring 12 mm. Balloon sphincteroplasty was performed using a 12mm wire-guided CRE balloon that was introduced for dilation successfully at the biliary sphincter. Multiple stones, debris, and sludge were extracted successfully using multiple sweeps of the extraction balloon. Final occlusion cholangiogram showed no remaining filling defects. There was excellent flow of bile and contrast at the end of the procedure. Otherwise normal ERCP to third part of the duodenum. . ___ PA / LAT CXR FINDINGS: Frontal and lateral radiographs of the chest show stable air space consolidation the right mid lung field, consistent with pneumonia. The cardiac silhouette appears slightly smaller on this exam, likely secondary to technique. Small right sided pleural effusions is superimposed on known pleural thickening, and has increased slightly over the interval. There is blunting of the left costophrenic angle. No pneumothorax. IMPRESSION: Right lower lobe pneumonia. . ADMISSION LABS: ============== ___ 09:15AM BLOOD WBC-20.9*# RBC-3.81* Hgb-10.2* Hct-33.1* MCV-87 MCH-26.8* MCHC-30.9* RDW-16.0* Plt ___ ___ 09:15AM BLOOD Neuts-88.2* Lymphs-5.1* Monos-6.1 Eos-0.5 Baso-0.1 ___ 09:15AM BLOOD UreaN-9 Creat-0.6 Na-138 K-4.0 Cl-101 HCO3-30 AnGap-11 ___ 09:15AM BLOOD ALT-14 AST-16 AlkPhos-102 Amylase-29 TotBili-1.0 DirBili-0.4* IndBili-0.6 ___ 07:30AM BLOOD Calcium-8.8 Phos-3.5 Mg-1.7 ___ 07:00PM URINE Color-Yellow Appear-Clear Sp ___ ___ 07:00PM URINE Blood-NEG Nitrite-NEG Protein-30 Glucose-NEG Ketone-NEG Bilirub-NEG Urobiln-2* pH-5.5 Leuks-NEG ___ 07:00PM URINE RBC-1 WBC-1 Bacteri-FEW Yeast-NONE Epi-<1 . DISCHARGE LABS: ============== ___ 07:05AM BLOOD WBC-14.5* RBC-3.22* Hgb-8.6* Hct-27.9* MCV-87 MCH-26.6* MCHC-30.7* RDW-15.9* Plt ___ . MICROBIOLOGY: ============= ___ Urine Culture: <10K CFU organisms ___ Blood Cultures x 2 sets: NGTD, final PENDING ___ C. diff toxin: POSITIVE  Brief Hospital Course: ___ yo M with hx of rectal CA, s/p hemicolecomty and ostomy, severe anxiety, A-fib on Coumadin, recent admission for open CCY, c/b wound infection, UTI, volume overload, now presents for elective ERCP for stent removal, s/p successful ERCP, noted to have incidental leukocytosis. . # Choledocholithiasis - previously complicated by cholangitis, s/p ERCP with stent placement, open CCY, now s/p elective ERCP with successful stent pull, sphincterotomy and sphincteroplasty. He tolerated the procedure well and his diet was advanced quickly, which he tolerated. His Coumadin and aspirin were held, but were resumed on day of discharge (POD #3), per d/w ERCP team. The ERCP team recommended ___ antibiotics x 5 days for prophylaxis. He received fluoroquinolone therapy, initially with ciprofloxacin, which was then transitioned to levofloxacin to cover his PNA as well. . # Leukocytosis / #PNA / # C. diff - Incidental elevated WBC on admission labs. Given prior hx of C. diff, reports of loose BM and chills at home, frequent recent abx and ongoing need for abx, he was started on empiric Rx for recurrent C. diff with PO Vancomyinc. His C. diff toxin did return POSITIVE. His UA and Urine Cx were NOT c/w infection. Blood cultures were drawn and show NGTD. His open CCY wound was felt to be healing well and NOT actively infected. Although he did not complain of SOB, he did have a mildly productive cough and CXR confirmed a RML pneumonia. He was switched from periprocedural coverage with ciprofloxacin to levofloxacin to cover for pneumonia as well as post-ERCP prophylaxis. He will receive a 7 day course of antibiotics for PNA and will receive a 21 day course of PO Vancomycin for C. diff (to extend for 2 weeks after completion of fluorquinolone). He should have repeat CXR in 4 weeks to assess for resolution of infiltrate. . # Wound infection - pt had recently undergone open CCY with wound complicated by wound infection with wound vac in place. On admission, he was evaluated by the Hepatobiliary Surgery team, and his wound was felt to be healing well. Wound vac was changed twice during the hospitalization, including on day of discharge. He was also followed by the ___ RN consult service. The plan is to continue with wound vac on discharge for a few more weeks and he will be followed in the Hepatobiliary Surgery Clinic. . # Atrial fibrillation - CHADS2 score is 2 vs 4, as his TIA hx is questionable. Per d/w his PCP, low suspicion for true CVA/TIA in the past, so he has not been bridged in the past. Per d/w ERCP, ok to resume systemic AC on POD#3. His HR was well-controlled on his home CCB regimen. No bridging therapy for anticoagulation was given during hospitalization or on discharge. He is resuming his previous home Coumadin regimen of Coumadin 3mg 2x/week ___ and ___ and Coumadin 2mg 5x/week. ___ will draw his next INR on ___. ___ clinic will continue to follow and the clinic was notified of discharge anticoagulation plan via email. . # Volume overload - he is only mildly volume overload on exam with trace peripheral edema. Lasix was held during hospitalization due to NPO status and ongoing infection. He was tolerating a PO diet well on day of discharge, so he should resume Lasix on discharge. . # Anxiety - pt with severe anxiety. On SSRI and 2 BZD's at baseline. Per d/w pt's family and his outpatient providers, there have been numerous adjustments to his medication regimen. Based on pt and family preferences, will plan for the following regimen: - sertraline 75mg po QPM - alprazolam 0.25mg po BID - clonazepam 0.5mg po QHS PRN sleep . TRANSITIONAL ISSUES: 1. complete course of PO Levaquin (last day = ___ to cover his PNA as well as ___ prophylaxis 2. complete course of PO vancomycin for C. diff, course to extend to 2 weeks after completion of Levaquin. Last day = ___. 3. Resume Coumadin at home regimen, next INR draw on ___ by ___. ___ to follow. 4. Repeat CXR in 4 weeks to assess for resolution of his PNA / infiltrate. 5. Continue wound vac and f/u with Hepatobiliary Surgery Clinic 6. sub_contents.sub_content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: M  Service: MEDICINE  Allergies: Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics) / NSAIDS / strawberries  Attending: ___.  Chief Complaint: post-ERCP; leukocytosis  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: ___ ERCP with sphincterotomy, biliary stent removal and sphincteroplasty  History of Present Illness: ___ yo M, hx of rectal CA s/p colectomy with ostomy, severe anxiety, s/p recent open CCY for choledocholithiasis, c/b wound infection, UTI and lower extremity edema, who presents for elective ERCP for stent pull. Underwent ERCP with successful sphincterotomy, stent pull and sphincteroplasty. . History provided by patient, as well as his family members, wife and daughter (HCP) at the bedside in the post-procedure area in the Advanced Endoscopy unit. . Pt reports stopping Coumadin 5 days prior to procedure. He reports that he has never previously been on Lovenox or heparin bridge for procedures. He currently complains of some mild post-procedure nausea, which has responded to IV Zofran, but denies any fevers, chills, abd pain, chest pain or SOB. . He reports that since his discharge on ___, his ___ edema has improved significantly, although 1 dose of Lasix was held on ___ due to weight loss of > 3 lbs (down to 179lbs on ___. He reports that his wound vac has not been bothering him. Of note, routine AM labs today note WBC# of 20K, and he does note some chills at home and his wife reports more loose BM's in his ostomy bag. He also reports some occ vague abdominal pain / pressure around his ostomy site, although he currently has none. He denies N/V, fever, melena / hematochezia in the ostomy. He denies dysuria, cough, SOB. He does note urinary frequency and nocturia but this has been a chronic problem and he was just seen in Urology Clinc and his urinary symptoms were felt to be most likely residual symptoms from recent UTI. Otherwise he denies any other symptoms except his baseline anxiety. He wishes to be discharged to home as soon as safely possible. . Recent hospital admissions / evaluations: 1. Admitted ___ - ___ for SOB, presumptive dx of ILI vs atypical PNA, discharged to home on course of PO antibiotics. . 2. Admitted ___ for abd pain, F/C, found to have MDR-E. coli sepsis and bacteremia from cholangitis, underwent ERCP with biliary stent and DC'ed to home on 2 week course of carbapenem IV via PICC. . 3. Seen and discharged from ED on ___ for melanotic stools. Had stable vital signs and Hct, felt to be some mild post-ERCP bleeding. . 4. Admitted ___ admitted to Hepatobiliary Surgery for elective open CCY. He received a 5-day course of ciprofloxacin for empiric therapy for UTI, but his urine culture ultimately returned No Growth. . 5. Seen and discharged from ED on ___ for wound infection and possible UTI. Was sent home on course of Keflex for wound infection and nitrofurantoin for UTI. Ultimately both wound and urine cx grew similar strains of E. coli. . 6. Admitted ___ - ___ to Surgery for worsening ___ edema and wound evaluation. His Keflex was stopped and he had a wound vac placed. For his ___ edema, ultrasound was negative for DVT. TTE was concerning for diastolic HF, so he was started on diuresis with good effect. He was ultimately discharged on standing PO Lasix 20mg daily. Repeat urine culture from ___ was negative for infection, but he was continued on his previously determined course of nitrofurantoin. . . Review of Systems: (+) see above HPI (-) fever, night sweats, headache, vision changes, rhinorrhea, congestion, sore throat, cough, shortness of breath, chest pain, vomiting, constipation, BRBPR, melena, hematochezia, dysuria, hematuria.  Past Medical History: -s/p open CCY ___ -Rectal cancer s/p resection & end colostomy, chemotherapy ___ -Asbestosis -Atrial fibrillation -Hyperlipidemia -TIA -Macular degeneration, legally blind -remote duodenal ulcer, evaluated at ___, resolved without tx -Osteoarthritis -Anxiety -___ esophagus (___) -Meniere's disease -Pseudogout -Bilateral inguinal hernia  Social History: ___ Family History: Denies any FH of liver, pancreas or GB disease. FH notable for 1 son with hx of PCKD, s/p kidney transplant.  Physical Exam: Admission Physical Exam: Vitals: T 98, BP 129/52, HR 62, RR 27, O2Sat 94% on 2L General: NAD, lying in stretcher HEENT: anicteric, dry MM CV: irreg irreg, no appreciable murmur Lungs: CTAB/L Abdomen: soft, NT, ND, +BS, ostomy with semi-formed stool, and incision with wound vac in place, some mild erythema around incision, but no frank drainage, no induration/fluctuance. Ext: trace b/l ___ edema Neuro: AAOx3, fluent speech, no facial droop Skin: dry, warm . Discharge Day Exam: VS: AVSS, notably afebrile, adequate O2 sat on RA Gen: sitting in chair eating lunch, looks well, NAD HEENT: anicteric, MMM CV: irreg, irreg, no murmur Lungs: CTAB/L Abd: soft, NT, ND, + ostomy with semi-formed stool. Wound vac and steri-strips in place Ext: trace B/L ___. Right knee without effusion., +FROM, no warmth, no erythema Neuro: AAOx3, fluent speech .  Pertinent Results: . Imaging: ======= ___ ERCP Impression: The previously placed plastic biliary stent was found in good position at the major papilla. A sphincterotomy was performed in the 12 o'clock position over the previously placed biliary stent using a needle-knife. The previously placed plastic biliary was then removed using a snare. Successful biliary duct cannulation using a sphincterotome. A single 12 mm oval stone that was causing partial obstruction was seen at the lower third of the common bile duct. There was mild diffuse post-obstructive dilation of the common bile duct, common hepatic duct, and right and left main hepatic ducts with the CBD measuring 12 mm. Balloon sphincteroplasty was performed using a 12mm wire-guided CRE balloon that was introduced for dilation successfully at the biliary sphincter. Multiple stones, debris, and sludge were extracted successfully using multiple sweeps of the extraction balloon. Final occlusion cholangiogram showed no remaining filling defects. There was excellent flow of bile and contrast at the end of the procedure. Otherwise normal ERCP to third part of the duodenum. . ___ PA / LAT CXR FINDINGS: Frontal and lateral radiographs of the chest show stable air space consolidation the right mid lung field, consistent with pneumonia. The cardiac silhouette appears slightly smaller on this exam, likely secondary to technique. Small right sided pleural effusions is superimposed on known pleural thickening, and has increased slightly over the interval. There is blunting of the left costophrenic angle. No pneumothorax. IMPRESSION: Right lower lobe pneumonia. . ADMISSION LABS: ============== ___ 09:15AM BLOOD WBC-20.9*# RBC-3.81* Hgb-10.2* Hct-33.1* MCV-87 MCH-26.8* MCHC-30.9* RDW-16.0* Plt ___ ___ 09:15AM BLOOD Neuts-88.2* Lymphs-5.1* Monos-6.1 Eos-0.5 Baso-0.1 ___ 09:15AM BLOOD UreaN-9 Creat-0.6 Na-138 K-4.0 Cl-101 HCO3-30 AnGap-11 ___ 09:15AM BLOOD ALT-14 AST-16 AlkPhos-102 Amylase-29 TotBili-1.0 DirBili-0.4* IndBili-0.6 ___ 07:30AM BLOOD Calcium-8.8 Phos-3.5 Mg-1.7 ___ 07:00PM URINE Color-Yellow Appear-Clear Sp ___ ___ 07:00PM URINE Blood-NEG Nitrite-NEG Protein-30 Glucose-NEG Ketone-NEG Bilirub-NEG Urobiln-2* pH-5.5 Leuks-NEG ___ 07:00PM URINE RBC-1 WBC-1 Bacteri-FEW Yeast-NONE Epi-<1 .
fields.诊疗经过: Brief Hospital Course: ___ yo M with hx of rectal CA, s/p hemicolecomty and ostomy, severe anxiety, A-fib on Coumadin, recent admission for open CCY, c/b wound infection, UTI, volume overload, now presents for elective ERCP for stent removal, s/p successful ERCP, noted to have incidental leukocytosis. . # Choledocholithiasis - previously complicated by cholangitis, s/p ERCP with stent placement, open CCY, now s/p elective ERCP with successful stent pull, sphincterotomy and sphincteroplasty. He tolerated the procedure well and his diet was advanced quickly, which he tolerated. His Coumadin and aspirin were held, but were resumed on day of discharge (POD #3), per d/w ERCP team. The ERCP team recommended ___ antibiotics x 5 days for prophylaxis. He received fluoroquinolone therapy, initially with ciprofloxacin, which was then transitioned to levofloxacin to cover his PNA as well. . # Leukocytosis / #PNA / # C. diff - Incidental elevated WBC on admission labs. Given prior hx of C. diff, reports of loose BM and chills at home, frequent recent abx and ongoing need for abx, he was started on empiric Rx for recurrent C. diff with PO Vancomyinc. His C. diff toxin did return POSITIVE. His UA and Urine Cx were NOT c/w infection. Blood cultures were drawn and show NGTD. His open CCY wound was felt to be healing well and NOT actively infected. Although he did not complain of SOB, he did have a mildly productive cough and CXR confirmed a RML pneumonia. He was switched from periprocedural coverage with ciprofloxacin to levofloxacin to cover for pneumonia as well as post-ERCP prophylaxis. He will receive a 7 day course of antibiotics for PNA and will receive a 21 day course of PO Vancomycin for C. diff (to extend for 2 weeks after completion of fluorquinolone). He should have repeat CXR in 4 weeks to assess for resolution of infiltrate. . # Wound infection - pt had recently undergone open CCY with wound complicated by wound infection with wound vac in place. On admission, he was evaluated by the Hepatobiliary Surgery team, and his wound was felt to be healing well. Wound vac was changed twice during the hospitalization, including on day of discharge. He was also followed by the ___ RN consult service. The plan is to continue with wound vac on discharge for a few more weeks and he will be followed in the Hepatobiliary Surgery Clinic. . # Atrial fibrillation - CHADS2 score is 2 vs 4, as his TIA hx is questionable. Per d/w his PCP, low suspicion for true CVA/TIA in the past, so he has not been bridged in the past. Per d/w ERCP, ok to resume systemic AC on POD#3. His HR was well-controlled on his home CCB regimen. No bridging therapy for anticoagulation was given during hospitalization or on discharge. He is resuming his previous home Coumadin regimen of Coumadin 3mg 2x/week ___ and ___ and Coumadin 2mg 5x/week. ___ will draw his next INR on ___. ___ clinic will continue to follow and the clinic was notified of discharge anticoagulation plan via email. . # Volume overload - he is only mildly volume overload on exam with trace peripheral edema. Lasix was held during hospitalization due to NPO status and ongoing infection. He was tolerating a PO diet well on day of discharge, so he should resume Lasix on discharge. . # Anxiety - pt with severe anxiety. On SSRI and 2 BZD's at baseline. Per d/w pt's family and his outpatient providers, there have been numerous adjustments to his medication regimen.

Multivitamins W/minerals 1 TAB PO DAILY 7. Furosemide 10 mg PO DAILY 8. TraMADOL (Ultram) 50 mg PO Q6H:PRN pain 9. ClonazePAM 0.5 mg PO QHS:PRN sleep 10. ALPRAZolam 0.25 mg PO BID anxiety 11. Brimonidine Tartrate 0.15% Ophth. 1 DROP BOTH EYES BID 12. Acetaminophen 500 mg PO Q8H:PRN pain 13. Colchicine 0.6 mg PO DAILY pseudogout pain 14. Levofloxacin 750 mg PO DAILY Duration: 5 Days last day = ___ RX *levofloxacin 750 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth daily Disp #*5 Tablet Refills:*0 15. Vancomycin Oral Liquid ___ mg PO Q6H Duration: 19 Days last day = ___ RX *vancomycin 125 mg 1 capsule(s) by mouth every 6 hours Disp #*76 Capsule Refills:*0 16. Polyethylene Glycol 17 g PO DAILY:PRN constipation 17. Ocuvite (vit C-vit E-lutein-min-om-3) 150-30-5-150 mg-unit-mg-mg oral daily 18. Warfarin 3 mg PO 2X/WEEK (MO,FR) 19. Warfarin 2 mg PO 5X/WEEK (___)  Discharge Instructions: You were admitted to the hospital after your endoscopic procedure for monitoring. You tolerated the procedure well. However, you were found to have evidence of a pneumonia as well as a GI diarrheal infection called C. difficile. You will need to complete a course of antibiotics for both infections. You will need repeat chest x-ray in a few weeks for follow-up. You will follow-up with the surgeons for your wound infection. You will resume Coumadin today at your previous home regimen. You will be followed by ___ services at home. Your wound vac was changed during this hospitalization. . Please take your medications as listed. . Please see your physicians as listed. . Next INR should be drawn on ___.

content: Name: ___ Unit No: ___  Admission Date: ___ Discharge Date: ___  Date of Birth: ___ Sex: F  Service: SURGERY  Allergies: No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions  Attending: ___  Chief Complaint: Abdominal Pain  Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure: ___ Laparoscopic appendectomy  History of Present Illness: ___ female on fertility treatments (currently Clomid, day #5) presents to ED from OSH with c/o abdominal pain, nausea and vomiting that started 6 pm yesterday. The pain is described as sharp, constant in the lower abdomen mainly in the RLQ. She was seen at OSH, with lab work notable for WBC 17.7. She had a CT abdomen/pelvis that identified a thickened appendix with ___ fat stranding as well as a 6.2 x 3.7-cm cyst in the right adnexa with a small amount of pelvic free fluid. She was given Cefoxitin at 03:00am and transferred to ___. She last had something to eat around 18:00. ACS was consulted for further evaluation and treatment. she denies any change in appetite, N&V, diarrhea, dysuria, vaginal bleeding or discharge, fevres, chils, chest pain, SOB.  Past Medical History: PMH: Papilledema PSH: DNC ___  Social History: ___ Family History: Non-contributory  Physical Exam: Physical exam VS: t:98.7 HR: 64 BP: 112/68 RR: 18 O(2)Sat: 99 RA General: No acute distress HEENT: PERRL Heart: RRR, no m/r/g Chest: CTA bilaterally Abdomen: Obese, abdomen soft, focally tender in RLQ, no rebound or guarding EXT: WWP Discharge Physical Exam: 98.4 PO 113 / 74 89 98  Pertinent Results: Imaging: CT st OSH: showed: thickened appendix with ___ fat stranding as well as a 6.2 x 3.7-cm cyst in the right adnexa with a small amount of pelvic free fluid. Ultrasound: Normal appearing uterus. Normal vascular flow in both ovaries. The right ovary is enlarged and demonstrates multiple thick-walled anechoic cysts, measuring up to 6.3 cm. While active torsion was not demonstrated on this exam, sporadic torsion cannot be completely excluded. Trace pelvic fluid, which is in within normal limits. ___ 05:40AM BLOOD WBC-14.0* RBC-3.74* Hgb-11.2 Hct-33.6* MCV-90 MCH-29.9 MCHC-33.3 RDW-13.1 RDWSD-42.7 Plt ___ ___ 05:00AM BLOOD WBC-19.3* RBC-4.05 Hgb-11.9 Hct-36.4 MCV-90 MCH-29.4 MCHC-32.7 RDW-12.8 RDWSD-42.2 Plt ___ ___ 05:40AM BLOOD Glucose-96 UreaN-4* Creat-0.7 Na-141 K-3.9 Cl-105 HCO3-23 AnGap-13 ___ 05:00AM BLOOD Glucose-128* UreaN-9 Creat-0.8 Na-141 K-4.5 Cl-106 HCO3-17* AnGap-18 ___ 06:10AM BLOOD ___ PTT-27.8 ___ ___ 05:09AM BLOOD Lactate-2.6*  Brief Hospital Course: Ms. ___ is a ___ yo F in good health on fertility treatment (Clomid, day #5) who presented to outside hospital with abdominal pain and CT scan concerning for acute appendicitis as well as a 6.2 x 3.7-cm cyst in the right adnexa with a small amount of pelvic free fluid. She underwent transvaginal ultra sound that showed multiple cysts in the right ovary with appearances consistent with multiple follicles. Informed consent was obtained and she was taken to the operating room and underwent laparoscopic appendectomy. Please see operative report for details. After a brief, uneventful stay in the PACU, the patient arrived on the floor tolerating clears and on IV fluids. The patient was hemodynamically stable. When tolerating a diet, the patient was converted to oral pain medication with continued good effect. Diet was progressively advanced as tolerated to a regular diet with good tolerability. The patient voided without problem. During this hospitalization, the patient ambulated early and frequently, was adherent with respiratory toilet and incentive spirometry, and actively participated in the plan of care. The patient received subcutaneous heparin and venodyne boots were used during this stay. At the time of discharge, the patient was doing well, afebrile with stable vital signs. The patient was tolerating a regular diet, ambulating, voiding without assistance, and pain was well controlled. The patient was discharged home without services. The patient received discharge teaching and follow-up instructions with understanding verbalized and agreement with the discharge plan.  Medications on Admission: Clomiphene 100mg daily  Discharge Medications: 1. Acetaminophen 650 mg PO Q6H:PRN Pain - Mild/Fever 2. Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID Hold for loose stool. 3. OxyCODONE (Immediate Release) 5 mg PO Q6H:PRN Pain - Moderate RX *oxycodone 5 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth every six (6) hours Disp #*5 Tablet Refills:*0 4. Senna 8.6 mg PO BID:PRN Constipation - First Line  Discharge Disposition: Home  Discharge Diagnosis: Acute nonperforated appendicitis.  Discharge Condition: Mental Status: Clear and coherent. Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive. Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.  Discharge Instructions: Dear Ms. ___, You were admitted to the Acute Care Surgery Service on ___ with abdominal pain and found to have an infection in your appendix. You were taken to the operating room and had it removed laparoscopically. You tolerated the procedure well and are now ready to be discharged to home with the following discharge instructions: ACTIVITY:  o Do not drive until you have stopped taking pain medicine and feel you could respond in an emergency. o You may climb stairs. o You may go outside, but avoid traveling long distances until you see your surgeon at your next visit. o Don't lift more than ___ lbs for 4 weeks. (This is about the weight of a briefcase or a bag of groceries.) This applies to lifting children, but they may sit on your lap. o You may start some light exercise when you feel comfortable. o You will need to stay out of bathtubs or swimming pools for a time while your incision is healing. Ask your doctor when you can resume tub baths or swimming.  HOW YOU MAY FEEL: o You may feel weak or washed out for a couple of weeks. You might want to nap often. Simple tasks may exhaust you. o You may have a sore throat because of a tube that was in your throat during surgery. o You might have trouble concentrating or difficulty sleeping. You might feel somewhat depressed. o You could have a poor appetite for a while. Food may seem unappealing. o All of these feelings and reactions are normal and should go away in a short time. If they do not, tell your surgeon.  YOUR INCISION: o Tomorrow you may shower and remove the gauzes over your incisions. Under these dressing you have small plastic bandages called steri-strips. Do not remove steri-strips for 2 weeks. (These are the thin paper strips that might be on your incision.) But if they fall off before that that's okay). o Your incisions may be slightly red around the stitches. This is normal. o You may gently wash away dried material around your incision. o Avoid direct sun exposure to the incision area. o Do not use any ointments on the incision unless you were told otherwise. o You may see a small amount of clear or light red fluid staining your dressing or clothes. If the staining is severe, please call your surgeon. o You may shower. As noted above, ask your doctor when you may resume tub baths or swimming.  YOUR BOWELS: o Constipation is a common side effect of narcotic pain medications. If needed, you may take a stool softener (such as Colace, one capsule) or gentle laxative (such as milk of magnesia, 1 tbs) twice a day. You can get both of these medicines without a prescription. o If you go 48 hours without a bowel movement, or have pain moving the bowels, call your surgeon.  PAIN MANAGEMENT: o It is normal to feel some discomfort/pain following abdominal surgery. This pain is often described as soreness. o Your pain should get better day by day. If you find the pain is getting worse instead of better, please contact your surgeon. o You will receive a prescription for pain medicine to take by mouth. It is important to take this medicine as directed. o Do not take it more frequently than prescribed. Do not take more medicine at one time than prescribed. o Your pain medicine will work better if you take it before your pain gets too severe. o Talk with your surgeon about how long you will need to take prescription pain medicine. Please don't take any other pain medicine, including non-prescription pain medicine, unless your surgeon has said its okay. o If you are experiencing no pain, it is okay to skip a dose of pain medicine. o Remember to use your cough pillow for splinting when you cough or when you are doing your deep breathing exercises. If you experience any of the following, please contact your surgeon: - sharp pain or any severe pain that lasts several hours - pain that is getting worse over time - pain accompanied by fever of more than 101 - a drastic change in nature or quality of your pain  MEDICATIONS: Take all the medicines you were on before the operation just as you did before, unless you have been told differently. If you have any questions about what medicine to take or not to take, please call your surgeon.  Followup Instructions: ___

If you find the pain is getting worse instead of better, please contact your surgeon. o You will receive a prescription for pain medicine to take by mouth. It is important to take this medicine as directed. o Do not take it more frequently than prescribed. Do not take more medicine at one time than prescribed. o Your pain medicine will work better if you take it before your pain gets too severe. o Talk with your surgeon about how long you will need to take prescription pain medicine. Please don't take any other pain medicine, including non-prescription pain medicine, unless your surgeon has said its okay. o If you are experiencing no pain, it is okay to skip a dose of pain medicine. o Remember to use your cough pillow for splinting when you cough or when you are doing your deep breathing exercises. If you experience any of the following, please contact your surgeon: - sharp pain or any severe pain that lasts several hours - pain that is getting worse over time - pain accompanied by fever of more than 101 - a drastic change in nature or quality of your pain  MEDICATIONS: Take all the medicines you were on before the operation just as you did before, unless you have been told differently. If you have any questions about what medicine to take or not to take, please call your surgeon.
